sessions.prompt_raw:
## Task ID
BP-012

## Context Loaded
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_bim.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_table.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_cntrl.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv
@planning/interfaces/tage_interfaces.md
@planning/interfaces/tage_table_interfaces.md

## Hypothesis
Two targeted RTL fixes resolve both defects found in
BP-011 without changing any functional logic or test
stimulus. All 27 existing tests pass after both fixes.

## Background

Read all context files completely before making any
changes. Do not skip this step.

Two defects were identified during BP-011 round-trip
testing. Both are in tage_cntrl.sv. Fix both in this
session.

### Defect 1: t_idx_r1 and t_tag_r1 always zero

tage_cntrl.sv declares t_idx_r1 and t_tag_r1 as 2D
arrays and reads them in the prediction logic to
populate prm_idx_p1, alt_idx_p1, alc_idx_p1, and
alc_tag_p1. These arrays are never assigned. They are
always zero.

Root cause: tage_table.sv computes idx_hash and
tag_hash internally but does not expose them as
outputs. tage_bim.sv computes its index internally
and does not expose it as an output.

Fix:

Step 1 -- tage_table.sv:
Add two output ports:
  output logic [THIS_INDEX_BITS-1:0]
    idx_hash_p0[0:NUM_PRED_SLOTS-1]
  output logic [MAX_TAG_WIDTH-1:0]
    tag_hash_p0[0:NUM_PRED_SLOTS-1]

Drive them from the existing combinational idx_hash
and tag_hash signals already computed in the module:
  assign idx_hash_p0[0] = idx_hash[0];
  assign idx_hash_p0[1] = idx_hash[1];
  assign tag_hash_p0[0] = tag_hash[0];
  assign tag_hash_p0[1] = tag_hash[1];

Do not change idx_hash or tag_hash computation.
Do not change any other logic.

Step 2 -- tage_bim.sv:
Add one output port:
  output logic [THIS_INDEX_BITS-1:0]
    idx_hash_p0[0:NUM_PRED_SLOTS-1]

Drive it from the existing index computation. The T0
index formula is:
  pc[INST_OFFSET +: THIS_INDEX_BITS]
for each slot. This is already computed inside
tage_bim.sv. Expose it as a combinational output.
Do not add a tag output -- T0 has no tag.
Do not change any other logic.

Step 3 -- tage_cntrl.sv:
Add input ports to receive the new outputs from
each table instance. The port arrays must match
the existing t_idx_r1 and t_tag_r1 dimensions:
  input logic [MAX_IDX_WIDTH-1:0]
    t_idx_p0[0:TAGE_NUM_TABLES-1][0:NUM_PRED_SLOTS-1]
  input logic [MAX_TAG_WIDTH-1:0]
    t_tag_p0[0:TAGE_NUM_TABLES-1][0:NUM_PRED_SLOTS-1]

Replace the existing undriven t_idx_r1/t_tag_r1
register stage with a single always_ff that registers
t_idx_p0 and t_tag_p0 into t_idx_r1 and t_tag_r1
on posedge clk. Reset to zero. Place this always_ff
in the valid_pipe_ff region or immediately after it.
No other changes to prediction logic -- all reads of
t_idx_r1 and t_tag_r1 remain unchanged.

T0 has no tag output. t_tag_p0[0][s] must be driven
to zero in tage.sv (see Step 4). t_tag_r1[0][s] will
therefore remain zero, which is correct -- T0 has no
tag and tage_cntrl never uses t_tag_r1[0].

Step 4 -- tage.sv:
Add interconnect wires:
  logic [MAX_IDX_WIDTH-1:0]
    w_idx_p0[0:TAGE_NUM_TABLES-1][0:NUM_PRED_SLOTS-1];
  logic [MAX_TAG_WIDTH-1:0]
    w_tag_p0[0:TAGE_NUM_TABLES-1][0:NUM_PRED_SLOTS-1];

Wire T0 index from tage_bim idx_hash_p0 outputs,
zero-extended to MAX_IDX_WIDTH:
  assign w_idx_p0[0][0] =
    MAX_IDX_WIDTH'(u_tage_bim.idx_hash_p0[0]);  -- NO
Instead add idx_hash_p0 to the tage_bim port list
and wire it to w_idx_p0[0]. Drive w_tag_p0[0][s]
to zero for both slots (T0 has no tag).

Wire T1-T4 index and tag from tage_table idx_hash_p0
and tag_hash_p0 outputs inside the generate loop.
Zero-extend to MAX_IDX_WIDTH and MAX_TAG_WIDTH
respectively.

Add t_idx_p0 and t_tag_p0 to the tage_cntrl
instantiation port map.

### Defect 2: T0 prm_ctr mis-extracted

In tage_cntrl.sv the T0 CTR is extracted at two
sites in the pred_logic always_comb block.

The cntrl_bits_p1 layout for T0 as assembled in
tage.sv is:
  {5'b0, bim_cntrl_p1[s], 1'b1}
  bit[0]   = VAL = 1
  bit[2:1] = CTR[1:0]
  bit[7:3] = 0

The current extraction:
  t_cntrl_bits_p1[0][s][TAGE_T0_CTR_BITS-1:0]
evaluates to [1:0] = {CTR[0], VAL}. This is wrong.

The correct extraction is [TAGE_T0_CTR_BITS:1]
which evaluates to [2:1] = CTR[1:0].

Fix both sites in the pred_logic always_comb block:

Site 1 -- primary T0 default:
  Change:
    prm_ctr_p1[s] = MAX_CTR_WIDTH'(
      t_cntrl_bits_p1[0][s][TAGE_T0_CTR_BITS-1:0]);
  To:
    prm_ctr_p1[s] = MAX_CTR_WIDTH'(
      t_cntrl_bits_p1[0][s][TAGE_T0_CTR_BITS:1]);

Site 2 -- alternate T0 default:
  Change:
    alt_ctr_p1[s] = MAX_CTR_WIDTH'(
      t_cntrl_bits_p1[0][s][TAGE_T0_CTR_BITS-1:0]);
  To:
    alt_ctr_p1[s] = MAX_CTR_WIDTH'(
      t_cntrl_bits_p1[0][s][TAGE_T0_CTR_BITS:1]);

No other changes to CTR logic.

## Binding Previous Decisions

- Do not modify tb_tage.sv. All 27 tests must pass
  unchanged.
- Do not modify bp_defines_pkg.sv or bp_structs_pkg.sv.
- Do not change any prediction or update logic beyond
  the two specific defect fixes described above.
- Do not change tage_bim.sv index computation, only
  expose it as an output.
- Do not change tage_table.sv hash computation, only
  expose it as outputs.
- tage.sv contains no logic. New wires and port
  connections only.
- Planning documents are authoritative. If any fix
  produces behavior that conflicts with planning
  documents, report in Results Capture. Do not
  modify planning documents in this session.

## Specific Requirements

1. Read all context files in full before making any
   changes. Do not skip this step.

2. Apply fixes in order: tage_table.sv, tage_bim.sv,
   tage_cntrl.sv, tage.sv. Read each file completely
   before editing it.

3. After all edits run:
     make lint_tage
     make sim_tage_fast
   Fix all errors and warnings before moving to
   Results Capture. All 27 tests must pass.

4. Verify the testbench overrides for tage_prm_idx,
   tage_alt_idx, tage_alc_idx, and tage_alc_tag in
   tb_tage.sv are still present but now redundant.
   Do not remove them -- that is cleanup work outside
   this session scope. Note in Results Capture that
   they are now redundant.

5. Verify the testbench override for tage_prm_ctr in
   rt_correct_t0_tst is still present but now
   redundant. Do not remove it. Note in Results
   Capture that it is now redundant.

6. Write Results Capture to ./prompts/BP-012.md.

## Constraints

- Lint clean with all existing suppression flags.
- 80 column line width. 2 space indent. No tabs.
- -Wno-VARHIDDEN required on tage_cntrl target
  (already in Makefile, do not remove).
- Do not modify tb_tage.sv, bp_defines_pkg.sv, or
  bp_structs_pkg.sv.
- Do not modify the Makefile unless a new port
  requires a new suppression flag. If so, document
  in Results Capture.

## Deliverables

1. frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_table.sv --
   idx_hash_p0 and tag_hash_p0 output ports added.
2. frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_bim.sv --
   idx_hash_p0 output port added.
3. frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_cntrl.sv --
   t_idx_p0 and t_tag_p0 input ports added,
   register stage added, T0 CTR extraction fixed
   at two sites.
4. frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage.sv --
   new interconnect wires and port connections added.
5. frontend/branch_predictor/Makefile -- updated only
   if required.
6. Results Capture filled in below.
sessions.results_raw:
## Summary

Hypothesis confirmed. Both defects fixed with targeted RTL
changes only. No test stimulus modified. All 27 tests pass.
Lint clean. No new Makefile suppression flags required.

## What was delivered

1. tage_table.sv
   - Added idx_hash_p0 and tag_hash_p0 output ports after
     cntrl_bits_p1 in the port list.
   - Added four combinational assigns exposing existing
     idx_hash[0:1] and tag_hash[0:1] internal signals.
   - No computation changed.

2. tage_bim.sv
   - Added idx_hash_p0 output port after cntrl_bits_p1.
   - Added two combinational assigns:
     pc[INST_OFFSET +: THIS_INDEX_BITS] per slot.
   - No computation changed.

3. tage_cntrl.sv
   - Added t_idx_p0 and t_tag_p0 input ports at end of
     port list (t_alc_index_u0 now comma-terminated).
   - Added always_ff block idx_tag_pipe_ff immediately after
     valid_pipe_ff. Registers t_idx_p0 and t_tag_p0 into
     existing t_idx_r1 and t_tag_r1 arrays. Resets to zero.
   - Fixed Defect 2 at two sites in pred_logic always_comb:
     changed [TAGE_T0_CTR_BITS-1:0] to [TAGE_T0_CTR_BITS:1]
     for prm_ctr_p1 and alt_ctr_p1 T0 defaults.

4. tage.sv
   - Added w_idx_p0 and w_tag_p0 interconnect wire arrays
     after the existing update/alloc address bus declarations.
   - Added T0 tag zero drives:
     assign w_tag_p0[0][0] = '0; assign w_tag_p0[0][1] = '0;
   - Added idx_hash_p0 connection to tage_bim port map,
     wired to w_idx_p0[0].
   - Added idx_hash_p0 and tag_hash_p0 connections to
     tage_table port map inside gen_tage_tbl generate loop,
     wired to w_idx_p0[t] and w_tag_p0[t].
   - Added t_idx_p0 and t_tag_p0 connections to tage_cntrl
     port map, wired to w_idx_p0 and w_tag_p0.

5. Makefile -- not modified. No new suppression flags needed.

## Test Case Results

All 27 tests pass with +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.

Lint target (make lint_tage): PASS -- zero warnings.
Sim target (make sim_tage_fast): PASS -- all 27 tests pass.

Individual test results:
  tage_rdy_tst             PASS
  upd_t0_inc_tst           PASS
  upd_t0_dec_tst           PASS
  upd_prm_inc_tst          PASS
  upd_prm_dec_alt_inc_tst  PASS
  upd_alt_inc_tst          PASS
  upd_use_inc_tst          PASS
  upd_alloc_tst            PASS
  upd_use_alt_inc_tst      PASS
  upd_use_alt_dec_tst      PASS
  upd_uaon_inc_tst         PASS
  upd_uaon_dec_tst         PASS
  upd_ctr_max_sat_tst      PASS
  upd_ctr_min_sat_tst      PASS
  upd_alloc_no_cand_tst    PASS
  pred_t0_only_tst         PASS
  pred_t1_single_hit_tst   PASS
  pred_t1t2_dual_hit_tst   PASS
  pred_uaon_override_tst   PASS
  pred_uaon_suppressed_tst PASS
  pred_rdy_timing_tst      PASS
  slot1_pred_tst           PASS
  slot1_upd_tst            PASS
  rt_correct_t0_tst        PASS
  rt_correct_tagged_tst    PASS
  rt_mispredict_alloc_tst  PASS
  rt_no_alloc_last_tbl_tst PASS

Redundant override status (requirement 4):
- tage_prm_idx overrides in rt_correct_t0_tst (line 2607),
  rt_correct_tagged_tst (line 2748), rt_mispredict_alloc_tst
  (line 2908), rt_no_alloc_last_tbl_tst (line 3053):
  PRESENT and now REDUNDANT. DUT produces correct values.
  Not removed per session scope.
- tage_alt_idx overrides in same four tests:
  PRESENT and now REDUNDANT.
- tage_alc_idx overrides in rt_correct_tagged_tst (2750)
  and rt_mispredict_alloc_tst (2910):
  PRESENT and now REDUNDANT.
- tage_alc_tag overrides in rt_correct_tagged_tst (2751)
  and rt_mispredict_alloc_tst (2911):
  PRESENT and now REDUNDANT.

Redundant override status (requirement 5):
- tage_prm_ctr override in rt_correct_t0_tst (line 2612):
  PRESENT and now REDUNDANT. DUT now correctly extracts
  CTR at [TAGE_T0_CTR_BITS:1] = [2:1]. Not removed.

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt

None. All steps were fully specified.

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt

- w_tag_p0 declared as [MAX_TAG_WIDTH-1:0] matching the
  tage_cntrl t_tag_p0 input port width. tage_table outputs
  tag_hash_p0 at MAX_TAG_WIDTH already, so no extension
  needed in the generate loop wiring.
- w_idx_p0 declared as [MAX_IDX_WIDTH-1:0] = 11b.
  tage_bim and tage_table index outputs are also 11b
  (THIS_INDEX_BITS = TAGE_TBL_IDX[t] = 11 for all tables).
  No zero extension required.
- idx_tag_pipe_ff placed immediately after valid_pipe_ff
  per prompt instruction ("or immediately after it").

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed

None. These are structural fixes with no functional impact
on the prediction algorithm.

## Deferred Work

- Cleanup: remove redundant tb_tage.sv overrides for
  tage_prm_idx, tage_alt_idx, tage_alc_idx, tage_alc_tag,
  and tage_prm_ctr in the four round-trip tests. Deferred
  per session scope constraints.
- tage_table_interfaces.md: add idx_hash_p0 and tag_hash_p0
  to T1-TN port list. Deferred per binding decision (no
  planning doc edits this session).
- tage_interfaces.md: no changes required.

## Other Notes

- Defect 1 root cause: hash signals were computed locally
  in each table instance but never surfaced as ports.
  tage_cntrl declared the r1 arrays expecting to register
  values that were never driven. Fix is minimal: expose
  existing signals, register them one stage.
- Defect 2 root cause: T0 cntrl_bits layout is
  {5'b0, CTR[1:0], VAL=1}. Bit[0]=VAL, bits[2:1]=CTR.
  The extraction [TAGE_T0_CTR_BITS-1:0] = [1:0] captured
  {CTR[0], VAL} instead of CTR. Correct extraction is
  [TAGE_T0_CTR_BITS:1] = [2:1] = CTR[1:0].
- No Makefile changes required. -Wno-VARHIDDEN retained
  on tage_cntrl target as required.
- Header Status updated: complete.

## Files Modified
Not captured

       tag_hash_p0[0:NUM_PRED_SLOTS-1]

   Add these after the existing cntrl_bits_p1 output
   port entry in the T1-TN prediction ports section.
   Add a brief semantics entry describing each port:
     idx_hash_p0: combinational index hash computed
       from pc and folded_hist at p0. One value per
       prediction slot. Used by tage_cntrl to populate
       tage_prm_idx, tage_alt_idx, and tage_alc_idx
       in prediction meta.
     tag_hash_p0: combinational tag hash computed
       from pc and folded_hist at p0. One value per
       prediction slot. Used by tage_cntrl to populate
       tage_alc_tag in prediction meta.

   Do not change any other section of
   tage_table_interfaces.md.

rows 1/2, 3/4, 5/6, and 7/8 of tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md.
All four tests use T1 as primary provider and T2 as alternate

sessions.prompt_raw:
## Task ID
BP-014a

## Context Loaded
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_bim.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_table.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_cntrl.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv
@planning/interfaces/tage_interfaces.md
@planning/interfaces/tage_table_interfaces.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_decisions.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md
@planning/testbenches/tage_tb_decisions.md

## Hypothesis
Four round-trip tests covering CTR update rule rows 1/2,
3/4, 5/6, and 7/8 can be added to tb_tage.sv without
modifying any RTL. All 31 tests pass after addition.

## Background

Read all context documents completely before writing any
test code. Do not skip this step.

All four tests use slot 0, T1 as primary provider, T2
as alternate provider. Both T1 and T2 must hit on the
prediction read. No T3 or T4 hits. T0 is present but
its direction is irrelevant for these rows (both
providers are tagged, prm_comp>0 and alt_comp>0).

### Round-trip test pattern

Same as BP-011. Summarized for reference:
  1. Pre-load RAM entries via hierarchical reference.
  2. Predict via staging always_ff. Wait two posedge
     clk edges.
  3. Capture tage_pred_meta_p2[0].
  4. Construct tage_upd_inp_u0[0] from captured meta
     plus resolved_taken and cond_mispredict. Drive
     via staging always_ff. Wait for
     tage_upd_rdy_u1[0].
  5. Re-predict at same PC.
  6. Verify RAM state and re-prediction output.

Staging always_ff is mandatory. Extend existing blocks.
Do not create new always_ff blocks.
All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.

### Dual tagged provider setup

For all four tests T1 and T2 must both hit. This
requires:
  - T1 entry at idx_hash(PC): valid=1, tag matching PC
  - T2 entry at idx_hash(PC): valid=1, tag matching PC

Index hash formula (from tage_table_interfaces.md):
  idx_hash = (pc >> INST_OFFSET) ^ fh_idx
  truncated to THIS_INDEX_BITS (11b).
  With folded_hist=0: idx_hash = (pc >> 2)[10:0]

Tag hash formula:
  tag_hash = (pc >> THIS_INDEX_BITS) ^ fh1 ^ (fh2<<1)
  truncated to THIS_TAG_BITS (8b).
  With folded_hist=0: tag_hash = (pc >> 11)[7:0]

T1 and T2 use the same index and tag hash formulas
with folded_hist=0, so for a given PC both tables
index and tag the same way. Choose a PC such that:
  - idx_hash maps to a convenient bank/row address
  - tag_hash is nonzero (avoid ambiguity with zero-init)
  - Address does not overlap any prior test address

Bank address assignment (from tage_table_interfaces.md):
  bank_addr = index[THIS_INDEX_BITS-1]  (bit 10)
  row_addr  = index[THIS_INDEX_BITS-2:0] (bits 9:0)

### CTR encoding (from tage_cntrl_decisions.md)

  000 sn  strongly not-taken
  001 wn2 weakly not-taken
  010 wn1 weakly not-taken
  011 wn0 weakest not-taken  <- boundary, UAON trigger
  100 wt0 weakest taken      <- boundary, UAON trigger
  101 wt1 weakly taken
  110 wt2 weakly taken
  111 wt3 strongly taken

Direction = CTR[2]. Weak = {011, 100}. Strong = all others.

### UAON hierarchical write

For TC-31 (rows 7/8), using_prm=0 requires:
  - T1 (prm) CTR at boundary: 3'b100 (weakest taken)
  - UAON counter at or above threshold (>= 8)
  - Write tb.u_dut.u_tage_cntrl.uaon[0] = 4'h8
    via hierarchical reference before predicting.
  - This forces the UAON mux to select alt (T2).
  - T2 (alt) becomes the provider. using_prm=0.

After the test completes, restore uaon[0] to 4'h0
so subsequent tests are not affected.

### CTR update rules reference

Rows 1/2 (TC-28):
  using_prm=1, prm correct, both tagged.
  prm_comp>0, alt_comp>0.
  Action: prm INC. alt no change.
  Rows 1 and 2 differ only in direction (taken/not-taken).
  Test one direction per test task.

Rows 3/4 (TC-29):
  using_prm=1, prm wrong, pred_diff=1, both tagged.
  prm_comp>0, alt_comp>0.
  Action: prm DEC, alt INC.
  pred_diff=1 requires T1 and T2 CTR MSBs to differ.

Rows 5/6 (TC-30):
  using_prm=1, prm wrong, pred_diff=0, both tagged.
  prm_comp>0, alt_comp>0.
  Action: prm DEC only. alt no change.
  pred_diff=0 requires T1 and T2 CTR MSBs to be equal.

Rows 7/8 (TC-31):
  using_prm=0 (UAON forces alt as provider), both tagged.
  alt correct.
  Action: alt INC. prm no change.
  Requires uaon[0] >= 8 and prm CTR at boundary.

## Binding Previous Decisions

- All 27 prior tests must pass unchanged.
- All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.
- tage_rdy_tst() runs first.
- Test structure convention:
    int verbose = 1;
    int _testname = 1;
    if (_testname != 0) testname(ARGS, verbose);
  Add one int _testname per new test alongside
  existing declarations.
- Use if (int_var != 0) not if (int_var) throughout.
- Staging always_ff mandatory. Extend existing blocks.
  Do not create additional blocks.
- Hierarchical references for RAM pre-load, readback,
  and uaon[0] write accepted per tage_tb_decisions.md.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Planning documents are authoritative. If RTL
  behavior differs from planning documents, report
  in Results Capture under RVA23 compliance risks.
  Do not modify RTL in this session.
- Expected values must be derived from RTL behavior
  where planning documents and RTL differ.

## Specific Requirements

1. Read all context documents in full before writing
   any test code. Do not skip this step.

2. Derive the test matrix. For each test case document
   all fields listed in the Results Capture Test Matrix
   section. Record the complete test matrix in Results
   Capture before writing any test implementation code.
   Include:
   - Test name and TC number
   - CTR update rule rows exercised (cite document)
   - PC chosen and resulting idx_hash, tag_hash,
     bank, row for T1 and T2
   - T1 and T2 RAM pre-load values (full entry word)
   - uaon[0] pre-load value (if applicable)
   - Expected meta fields after predict
   - resolved_taken and cond_mispredict values
   - Expected T1 RAM after update (CTR field)
   - Expected T2 RAM after update (CTR field)
   - Re-predict expected prm_comp and prm_ctr
   - Pass/fail criterion

3. Implement exactly four round-trip tasks, all slot 0:

   a. rt_ctr_rows1_2_tst (TC-28)
      T1 prm, T2 alt, using_prm=1, prm correct.
      Choose CTR values so prm_pred_tkn=1 (taken)
      and resolved_taken=1. cond_mispredict=0.
      Choose T1 and T2 CTR MSBs to differ so
      pred_diff=1 (exercises Table 7 row 3 as well).
      Verify: T1 CTR incremented. T2 CTR unchanged.
      Verify Table 7 row 3 useful update fires on T1.
      Cite CTR rule rows 1 or 2 and Table 7 row 3.

   b. rt_ctr_rows3_4_tst (TC-29)
      T1 prm, T2 alt, using_prm=1, prm wrong,
      pred_diff=1.
      T1 CTR MSB=1 (taken). T2 CTR MSB=0 (not-taken).
      resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.
      pred_diff=1 (prm_tkn=1, alt_tkn=0).
      Verify: T1 CTR decremented. T2 CTR incremented.
      Cite CTR rule rows 3 or 4.

   c. rt_ctr_rows5_6_tst (TC-30)
      T1 prm, T2 alt, using_prm=1, prm wrong,
      pred_diff=0.
      T1 CTR=3'b100 (weakest taken, MSB=1).
      T2 CTR=3'b101 (weakly taken, MSB=1).
      resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.
      pred_diff=0 (both MSB=1, both predict taken).
      Verify: T1 CTR decremented (3'b100->3'b011).
      Verify: T2 CTR unchanged.
      Cite CTR rule rows 5 or 6.

   d. rt_ctr_rows7_8_tst (TC-31)
      T1 prm, T2 alt, using_prm=0 (UAON forces alt).
      Write uaon[0]=4'h8 via hierarchical reference
      before predicting.
      T1 CTR=3'b100 (boundary, triggers UAON).
      T2 CTR=3'b101 (weakly taken, MSB=1).
      resolved_taken=1. cond_mispredict=0.
      Alt (T2) is provider. Alt correct.
      Verify: T2 CTR incremented (3'b101->3'b110).
      Verify: T1 CTR unchanged.
      Verify: tage_using_primary=0 in captured meta.
      Restore uaon[0]=4'h0 after test completes.
      Cite CTR rule rows 7 or 8.

4. Each task must:
   - Report PASS or FAIL with a descriptive message.
   - Accept verbose as a parameter. When verbose != 0
     print setup and result detail.

5. Add test enable integers and invocations to the
   main initial block following the existing
   convention. New tests run after all 27 existing
   tests.

6. sim_tage_fast must pass all 31 tests. sim_tage
   must also pass.

7. Update the Makefile only if new flags or
   dependencies are required. Do not remove existing
   flags.

## Constraints

- Lint clean with all existing suppression flags.
- 80 column line width. 2 space indent. No tabs.
- Only modify tb_tage.sv and the Makefile if needed.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Write Results Capture to ./prompts/BP-014a.md.
- Complete the Test Matrix section of Results Capture
  before implementing any test code.

## Deliverables

1. frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv --
   four CTR round-trip tests added, all 31 tests
   passing under sim_tage_fast. All prior tests
   still pass.
2. frontend/branch_predictor/Makefile -- updated
   only if required.
3. Results Capture filled in below including
   completed Test Matrix section with planning
   document citations.
sessions.results_raw:
## Summary
Four CTR round-trip tests (TC-28 through TC-31) added to
tb_tage.sv. Tests exercise rows 1/2, 3/4, 5/6, and 7/8 of
tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md using a dual-tagged-provider
scenario where both T1 and T2 hit on prediction and T3/T4
do not. All tests use slot 0 and FAST_INIT=1.

Per RTL ascending-scan logic (last hit wins), T2 is primary
(comp=2, longer history) and T1 is alternate (comp=1). The
prompt labels T1 primary and T2 alternate; the
implementation follows RTL per the "expected values from
RTL" constraint. See RVA23 section.

## Test Matrix

NOTE: "RTL T1" = gen_tage_tbl[1], alt, comp=1.
      "RTL T2" = gen_tage_tbl[2], prm, comp=2.
      Prompt labels are swapped relative to RTL.

---
### TC-28: rt_ctr_rows1_2_tst

CTR rule: tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md rows 1/2
  (using_prm=1, prm correct, both tagged: prm INC)
USE rule: Table 7 row 3
  (pred_diff=1, using_prm=1, mispredict=0: INC prm USE)

PC = 40'h09100
idx_hash = (0x09100 >> 2)[10:0] = 0x440 = 1088
bank = idx_hash[10] = 1
row  = idx_hash[9:0] = 0x040 = 64
tag_hash = (0x09100 >> 11)[7:0] = 0x12

Pre-load RTL T2 (prm, comp=2) gen_tage_tbl[2] mem[1][64]:
  16'h120B = TAG=0x12 EPC=00 USE=00 CTR=101(wt1) VALID=1
  CTR[2]=1 (prm predicts taken)

Pre-load RTL T1 (alt, comp=1) gen_tage_tbl[1] mem[1][64]:
  16'h1207 = TAG=0x12 EPC=00 USE=00 CTR=011(wn0) VALID=1
  CTR[2]=0 (alt predicts not-taken)

T3/T4: FAST_INIT zero. No alloc (mispredict=0).
uaon[0]: not written (stays 0).

Expected meta1:
  tage_prm_comp=2, tage_alt_comp=1
  tage_using_primary=1, tage_pred_tkn=1
  tage_prm_tkn=1, tage_alt_tkn=0, pred_diff=1
  tage_prm_ctr=3'b101, tage_alt_ctr=3'b011

resolved_taken=1, cond_mispredict=0

Expected RTL T2 (prm) mem[1][64] after update:
  CTR INC: 101->110
  USE INC: 00->01 (Table 7 row 3)
  16'h121D = TAG=0x12 EPC=00 USE=01 CTR=110 VALID=1

Expected RTL T1 (alt) mem[1][64] after update:
  Unchanged: 16'h1207

Re-predict: tage_prm_comp=2, tage_prm_ctr=3'b110
Pass criterion: local_fails == 0

---
### TC-29: rt_ctr_rows3_4_tst

CTR rule: tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md rows 3/4
  (using_prm=1, prm wrong, pred_diff=1: prm DEC, alt INC)
USE rule: Table 7 row 4
  (pred_diff=1, using_prm=1, mispredict=1: DEC prm USE)

PC = 40'h0B800
idx_hash = (0x0B800 >> 2)[10:0] = 0x600 = 1536
bank = 1, row = 0x200 = 512
tag_hash = (0x0B800 >> 11)[7:0] = 0x17

Pre-load RTL T2 (prm, comp=2) gen_tage_tbl[2] mem[1][512]:
  16'h171B = TAG=0x17 EPC=00 USE=01 CTR=101(wt1) VALID=1
  CTR[2]=1 (prm predicts taken)
  USE=01 chosen so DEC to 00 is observable.

Pre-load RTL T1 (alt, comp=1) gen_tage_tbl[1] mem[1][512]:
  16'h1707 = TAG=0x17 EPC=00 USE=00 CTR=011(wn0) VALID=1
  CTR[2]=0 (alt predicts not-taken, alt correct when
  resolved_taken=0)

Pre-load T3 gen_tage_tbl[3] mem[1][512]: 16'h0010
  (VALID=0 CTR=0 USE=01 EPC=0 TAG=0) -> ueff=01 != 00
  -> not alloc candidate -> prevents allocation.
Pre-load T4 gen_tage_tbl[4] mem[1][512]: 16'h0010
  Same rationale.

uaon[0]: not written.

Expected meta1:
  tage_prm_comp=2, tage_alt_comp=1
  tage_using_primary=1, tage_pred_tkn=1
  tage_prm_tkn=1, tage_alt_tkn=0, pred_diff=1
  tage_alc_comp=0 (both T3/T4 USE=01, no candidate)

resolved_taken=0, cond_mispredict=1

Expected RTL T2 (prm) mem[1][512] after update:
  CTR DEC: 101->100
  USE DEC: 01->00 (Table 7 row 4)
  16'h1709 = TAG=0x17 EPC=00 USE=00 CTR=100 VALID=1

Expected RTL T1 (alt) mem[1][512] after update:
  CTR INC: 011->100 (pred_diff=1 and alt correct)
  USE: 00, unchanged (USE targets prm T2 only)
  16'h1709 = TAG=0x17 EPC=00 USE=00 CTR=100 VALID=1

Re-predict: tage_prm_comp=2, tage_prm_ctr=3'b100
Pass criterion: local_fails == 0

---
### TC-30: rt_ctr_rows5_6_tst

CTR rule: tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md rows 5/6
  (using_prm=1, prm wrong, pred_diff=0: prm DEC only)
USE rule: Table 7 row 1
  (pred_diff=0: no USE update)

PC = 40'h0C000
idx_hash = (0x0C000 >> 2)[10:0] = 0x000 = 0
bank = 0, row = 0
tag_hash = (0x0C000 >> 11)[7:0] = 0x18

Pre-load RTL T2 (prm, comp=2) gen_tage_tbl[2] mem[0][0]:
  16'h1809 = TAG=0x18 EPC=00 USE=00 CTR=100(wt0) VALID=1
  CTR[2]=1 (prm predicts taken)
  CTR=100 is boundary; uaon[0]=0 so UAON does not fire.

Pre-load RTL T1 (alt, comp=1) gen_tage_tbl[1] mem[0][0]:
  16'h180B = TAG=0x18 EPC=00 USE=00 CTR=101(wt1) VALID=1
  CTR[2]=1 (alt predicts taken, pred_diff=0)

Pre-load T3 gen_tage_tbl[3] mem[0][0]: 16'h0010
Pre-load T4 gen_tage_tbl[4] mem[0][0]: 16'h0010
  Same alloc-prevention rationale as TC-29.

uaon[0]: not written.

Expected meta1:
  tage_prm_comp=2, tage_alt_comp=1
  tage_using_primary=1, tage_pred_tkn=1
  tage_prm_tkn=1, tage_alt_tkn=1, pred_diff=0
  tage_alc_comp=0

resolved_taken=0, cond_mispredict=1

Expected RTL T2 (prm) mem[0][0] after update:
  CTR DEC: 100->011 (rows 5/6 prm DEC, crosses boundary)
  USE: 00, unchanged (pred_diff=0, no USE update)
  16'h1807 = TAG=0x18 EPC=00 USE=00 CTR=011 VALID=1

Expected RTL T1 (alt) mem[0][0] after update:
  Unchanged: 16'h180B
  (pred_diff=0: alt CTR write condition fails)

Re-predict: tage_prm_comp=2, tage_prm_ctr=3'b011
Pass criterion: local_fails == 0

---
### TC-31: rt_ctr_rows7_8_tst

CTR rule: tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md rows 7/8
  (using_prm=0, UAON fires, both tagged, alt correct:
   alt INC, prm no change)
USE rule: Table 7 row 1 (pred_diff=0: no USE update)
  pred_diff=0: T2(prm) CTR[2]=1, T1(alt) CTR[2]=1,
  post-mux CTR is T1 CTR=101 (CTR[2]=1). Both taken.

PC = 40'h12400
idx_hash = (0x12400 >> 2)[10:0] = 0x100 = 256
bank = 0, row = 256
tag_hash = (0x12400 >> 11)[7:0] = 0x24

Pre-load RTL T2 (prm, comp=2) gen_tage_tbl[2] mem[0][256]:
  16'h2409 = TAG=0x24 EPC=00 USE=00 CTR=100(wt0) VALID=1
  CTR=100 triggers UAON condition.

Pre-load RTL T1 (alt, comp=1) gen_tage_tbl[1] mem[0][256]:
  16'h240B = TAG=0x24 EPC=00 USE=00 CTR=101(wt1) VALID=1

T3/T4: FAST_INIT zero. No alloc (mispredict=0).
uaon[0] pre-load: 4'h8 (written before predict via
  u_dut.u_tage_cntrl.uaon[0] = 4'h8)
  uaon[0]=8 >= TAGE_UAON_THRES=8, so UAON fires.

Expected meta1:
  tage_prm_comp=2, tage_alt_comp=1
  tage_using_primary=0 (UAON fires)
  tage_pred_tkn=1 (T1 alt CTR[2]=1)
  tage_prm_tkn=1, tage_alt_tkn=1, pred_diff=0
  tage_prm_ctr=3'b100, tage_alt_ctr=3'b101

resolved_taken=1, cond_mispredict=0

Expected RTL T1 (alt, provider) mem[0][256] after update:
  CTR INC: 101->110 (rows 7/8 alt INC, alt correct)
  USE: 00, unchanged (pred_diff=0, no USE update)
  16'h240D = TAG=0x24 EPC=00 USE=00 CTR=110 VALID=1

Expected RTL T2 (prm) mem[0][256] after update:
  Unchanged: 16'h2409
  (pred_diff=0: prm CTR write via alt path skipped)

UAON analysis: post-mux CTR = T1 CTR=101 -> pred_strong=1
-> uaon_upd_ff condition !pred_strong=0 -> UAON update
does not fire -> uaon[0] remains 4'h8 after update.
Restore u_dut.u_tage_cntrl.uaon[0]=4'h0 before re-predict
to prevent spurious UAON from re-firing on T2 CTR=100.

Re-predict (after restore uaon[0]=0):
  tage_prm_comp=2
  tage_prm_ctr=3'b100 (T2 unchanged)
Pass criterion: local_fails == 0

## What was delivered
1. frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv: four CTR
   round-trip tasks added (TC-28 to TC-31). Four test
   enable integers added. Four invocations added to main
   initial block. Verdict message updated to BP-014a.
2. frontend/branch_predictor/Makefile: no changes needed.

## Test Case Results
sim_tage_fast and sim_tage both exit 0 with all 31 tests passing.

TC-28 rt_ctr_rows1_2_tst: PASS 0 failures
  T2[1][64]=0x121d (CTR 101->110, USE 00->01). T1[1][64]=0x1207.
  Re-predict: prm=2 ctr=110.

TC-29 rt_ctr_rows3_4_tst: PASS 0 failures
  T2[1][512]=0x1709 (CTR 101->100, USE 01->00). T1[1][512]=0x1709.
  Re-predict: prm=2 ctr=100.

TC-30 rt_ctr_rows5_6_tst: PASS 0 failures
  T2[0][0]=0x1807 (CTR 100->011). T1[0][0]=0x180b (unchanged).
  Re-predict: prm=2 ctr=011.

TC-31 rt_ctr_rows7_8_tst: PASS 0 failures
  T1[0][256]=0x240d (CTR 101->110). T2[0][256]=0x2409 (unchanged).
  using_prm=0 confirmed in meta1. Re-predict: prm=2 ctr=100.

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt

A1. The prompt states "T1 as primary provider, T2 as
    alternate provider." RTL ascending scan (last hit wins)
    makes T2 primary (comp=2) and T1 alternate (comp=1)
    when both T1 and T2 hit. Implementation follows RTL.
    All verify statements use RTL T2 as prm, RTL T1 as
    alt. Prompt verify statements are inverted relative
    to RTL for TC-28, TC-29, TC-30. TC-31 expectations
    happen to match RTL because CTR values are assigned
    such that role-swapping preserves the expected change.

A2. uaon[0] UAON update for TC-31: post-mux CTR is the
    actual prediction CTR after UAON mux. With using_prm=0
    and T1 CTR=101, post-mux CTR=101 (strong). uaon_upd_ff
    gates on !pred_strong; since pred_strong=1, UAON does
    not update. uaon[0] stays at 4'h8 after the update
    step. Must restore to 4'h0 before re-predict.

A3. For TC-29 and TC-30 (cond_mispredict=1), T3 and T4
    at the test address are pre-loaded with 16'h0010
    (USE=01, ueff=01 != 00) to block allocation. Without
    this, alc_comp=3 (T3 from FAST_INIT USE=00) would be
    a valid candidate and alloc would write an unintended
    entry into T3, potentially corrupting later tests and
    making CTR verify of T2 ambiguous.

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt

D1. TC-29 RTL T2 (prm) initial USE=01 (16'h171B, not
    16'h170B). USE=01 chosen so Table 7 row 4 DEC is
    visible (01->00). USE=00 would saturate at 00 with
    no observable effect.

D2. TC-31 uaon[0] restore placed before step 6 re-predict,
    not after PASS/FAIL display. Makes re-predict
    deterministic: uaon=0 prevents UAON from re-firing on
    T2 CTR=100 boundary, giving using_primary=1 in meta2.

D3. Allocation-prevention entries 16'h0010 use VALID=0
    and TAG=0. Both conditions ensure no tag hit during
    prediction for any of the four test PCs (all have
    nonzero tag_hash).

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed

R1. Provider labeling inconsistency in planning documents:
    The prompt and some planning documents refer to T1 as
    primary and T2 as alternate in the four-test scenario.
    RTL ascending scan makes T2 primary when both T1 and
    T2 hit. This is not an RTL defect (behavior matches
    TAGE algorithm), but the documentation is misleading.
    No RVA23 violation; TAGE is a microarchitectural
    optimization not subject to RVA23 specification.

R2. UAON counter frozen by pred_strong gate: when the
    post-mux prediction is strong (CTR not in {011,100}),
    uaon_upd_ff does not update. In TC-31, T1 CTR=101
    produces pred_strong=1, freezing uaon at 4'h8. The
    UAON counter would only decrease if the prm/alt agree
    with prediction direction when prm is correct. This
    edge case may cause the UAON counter to drift high
    and remain there, permanently using the alternate
    provider even if the primary improves. Worth reviewing
    the threshold and update policy in future sessions.

## Deferred Work
None identified for this session.

## Other Notes
None.

## Files Modified
Not captured

sessions.overview:
Add two round-trip CTR update tests to tb_tage.sv covering
rows 9/10 and 11/12 of tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md.
Both tests use using_prm=0 (UAON forces alt as provider)
with both T1 and T2 tagged and hitting. All 31 prior tests
must pass unchanged.

Provider labeling note: T2 is primary (comp=2, 13b history) and T1 is alternate (comp=1, 8b history) when both hit. The primary is the longest-history tagged table that hits; the alternate is the next longest below it. UAON forces using_prm=0 so T1 (alt, comp=1) supplies the prediction and is the active provider for these tests.

All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.

sessions.prompt_raw:
## Task ID
BP-014b

## Context Loaded
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_bim.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_table.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_cntrl.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv
@planning/interfaces/tage_interfaces.md
@planning/interfaces/tage_table_interfaces.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_decisions.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md
@planning/testbenches/tage_tb_decisions.md

## Hypothesis
Two round-trip tests covering CTR update rule rows 9/10
and 11/12 can be added to tb_tage.sv without modifying
any RTL. All 33 tests pass after addition.

## Background

Read all context documents completely before writing any
test code. Do not skip this step.

### Provider role assignment

The primary provider is the longest-history tagged table
that hits. The alternate provider is the next longest-history
tagged table that hits below the primary. When both T1 and
T2 hit and T3/T4 do not, T2 is primary (comp=2, 13b
history) and T1 is alternate (comp=1, 8b history). This
assignment was established and verified in BP-014a.

Both tests force using_prm=0 via UAON, so T1 (alt,
comp=1) is the active prediction source. tage_pred_tkn
comes from T1. tage_using_primary=0 in captured meta.

### Round-trip test pattern

Same as BP-011 and BP-014a. Summarized for reference:
  1. Pre-load RAM entries via hierarchical reference.
  2. Predict via staging always_ff. Wait two posedge
     clk edges.
  3. Capture tage_pred_meta_p2[0].
  4. Construct tage_upd_inp_u0[0] from captured meta
     plus resolved_taken and cond_mispredict. Drive
     via staging always_ff. Wait for
     tage_upd_rdy_u1[0].
  5. Re-predict at same PC.
  6. Verify RAM state and re-prediction output.

Staging always_ff is mandatory. Extend existing blocks.
Do not create new always_ff blocks.
All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.

### Dual tagged provider setup

For all tests T1 and T2 must both hit. Setup is
identical to BP-014a. Index and tag hash formulas
with folded_hist=0:
  idx_hash = (pc >> INST_OFFSET)[10:0]
           = (pc >> 2)[10:0]
  tag_hash = (pc >> THIS_INDEX_BITS)[7:0]
           = (pc >> 11)[7:0]

Bank address:
  bank_addr = index[10]     (bit 10)
  row_addr  = index[9:0]    (bits 9:0)

Choose PCs that do not overlap any prior test address.
TC-28 through TC-31 from BP-014a used:
  PC=40'h09100, 40'h0B800, 40'h0C000, 40'h12400.
Do not reuse these PCs.

### UAON hierarchical write

Both TC-32 and TC-33 require using_prm=0. Use the same
mechanism as TC-31 in BP-014a:
  Write tb.u_dut.u_tage_cntrl.uaon[0] = 4'h8 before
  predicting. uaon[0]=8 >= TAGE_UAON_THRES=8 fires UAON.
  T2 primary CTR must be at boundary (3'b100 or 3'b011)
  to trigger the UAON condition.
  Restore uaon[0]=4'h0 after each test completes.

### CTR encoding (from tage_cntrl_decisions.md)

  000 sn  strongly not-taken
  001 wn2 weakly not-taken
  010 wn1 weakly not-taken
  011 wn0 weakest not-taken  <- boundary, UAON trigger
  100 wt0 weakest taken      <- boundary, UAON trigger
  101 wt1 weakly taken
  110 wt2 weakly taken
  111 wt3 strongly taken

Direction = CTR[2]. Boundary = {011, 100}.

### CTR update rules reference

Rows 9/10 (TC-32):
  using_prm=0, pred_diff=1, alt wrong, both tagged.
  prm_comp>0, alt_comp>0.
  Action: prm INC, alt DEC.
  pred_diff=1 requires T2 (prm) and T1 (alt) CTR
  MSBs to differ.
  T1 (alt, provider) predicts taken (CTR[2]=1).
  resolved_taken=0, cond_mispredict=1.
  T2 (prm) CTR[2]=0 (not-taken), so pred_diff=1.
  Action: T2 (prm) CTR INC. T1 (alt) CTR DEC.

Rows 11/12 (TC-33):
  using_prm=0, pred_diff=0, alt wrong, both tagged.
  prm_comp>0, alt_comp>0.
  Action: alt DEC only. prm no change.
  pred_diff=0 requires T2 (prm) and T1 (alt) CTR
  MSBs to be equal.
  T1 (alt, provider) predicts taken (CTR[2]=1).
  resolved_taken=0, cond_mispredict=1.
  T2 (prm) CTR[2]=1 (taken), so pred_diff=0.
  Action: T1 (alt) CTR DEC. T2 (prm) CTR unchanged.

### UAON update analysis for TC-32 and TC-33

At update time: using_prm=0, cond_mispredict=1.
  prm_correct = (resolved_taken == tage_prm_tkn)
  alt_correct = (resolved_taken == tage_alt_tkn)
  For both TC-32 and TC-33: alt is wrong (alt wrong,
  cond_mispredict=1).
  For TC-32: prm (T2) predicts not-taken, resolved=0,
    so prm_correct=1. alt_wrong=1, prm_correct=1 ->
    UAON DEC.
  For TC-33: prm (T2) predicts taken, resolved=0,
    so prm_correct=0. Both wrong -> UAON no change.
  tage_pred_strong: post-mux CTR is T1 CTR, which is
    a boundary value (3'b100) in both cases.
    CTR=100 is NOT weak? No -- 100 is a boundary/weak
    state. NOT WEAK means CTR != 011 and CTR != 100.
    CTR=100 IS weak, so tage_pred_strong=0.
    UAON update fires for both tests.
  TC-32: UAON DEC fires. uaon[0] decrements from 8
    to 7. Restore to 4'h0 before re-predict is still
    required to avoid spurious UAON on re-predict.
  TC-33: UAON no change. uaon[0] stays at 8.
    Restore to 4'h0 before re-predict is required.

### Allocation analysis for TC-32 and TC-33

cond_mispredict=1 for both tests. Allocation may
fire if tage_alloc_comp != 0. To prevent allocation
from corrupting test state or later tests, pre-load
T3 and T4 at the test PC with USE=01 (ueff=01 != 00)
as done in BP-014a TC-29 and TC-30.
Pre-load value: 16'h0010 (VALID=0, USE=01, TAG=0).
This ensures no allocation write occurs for these
test addresses.

## Binding Previous Decisions

- All 31 prior tests must pass unchanged.
- All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.
- tage_rdy_tst() runs first.
- Test structure convention:
    int verbose = 1;
    int _testname = 1;
    if (_testname != 0) testname(ARGS, verbose);
  Add one int _testname per new test alongside
  existing declarations.
- Use if (int_var != 0) not if (int_var) throughout.
- Staging always_ff mandatory. Extend existing blocks.
  Do not create additional blocks.
- Hierarchical references for RAM pre-load, readback,
  and uaon[0] write accepted per tage_tb_decisions.md.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Planning documents are authoritative. If RTL
  behavior differs from planning documents, report
  in Results Capture under RVA23 compliance risks.
  Do not modify RTL in this session.
- Expected values must be derived from RTL behavior
  where planning documents and RTL differ.

## Specific Requirements

1. Read all context documents in full before writing
   any test code. Do not skip this step.

2. Derive the test matrix. For each test case document
   all fields listed in the Results Capture Test Matrix
   section. Record the complete test matrix in Results
   Capture before writing any test implementation code.
   Include:
   - Test name and TC number
   - CTR update rule rows exercised (cite document)
   - PC chosen and resulting idx_hash, tag_hash,
     bank, row for T1 and T2
   - T1 and T2 RAM pre-load values (full entry word)
   - T3 and T4 RAM pre-load values (if applicable)
   - uaon[0] pre-load value
   - Expected meta fields after predict
   - resolved_taken and cond_mispredict values
   - Expected T1 RAM after update (CTR field)
   - Expected T2 RAM after update (CTR field)
   - UAON state after update
   - Re-predict expected prm_comp and prm_ctr
   - Pass/fail criterion

3. Implement exactly two round-trip tasks, both slot 0:

   a. rt_ctr_rows9_10_tst (TC-32)
      T2 prm (comp=2), T1 alt (comp=1).
      using_prm=0 (UAON forces T1 as provider).
      T1 (alt, provider) wrong. pred_diff=1.
      T1 CTR=3'b100 (boundary, triggers UAON).
        CTR[2]=1, predicts taken.
      T2 CTR=3'b011 (boundary, triggers UAON on prm).
        CTR[2]=0, predicts not-taken.
      pred_diff=1 (T1 CTR[2]=1 != T2 CTR[2]=0).
      uaon[0]=4'h8 written before predict.
      resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.
      Action per rows 9/10: prm (T2) INC, alt (T1) DEC.
      Verify: T1 CTR decremented (100->011).
      Verify: T2 CTR incremented (011->100).
      Verify: tage_using_primary=0 in captured meta.
      Restore uaon[0]=4'h0 before re-predict.
      Cite CTR rule rows 9 or 10.

   b. rt_ctr_rows11_12_tst (TC-33)
      T2 prm (comp=2), T1 alt (comp=1).
      using_prm=0 (UAON forces T1 as provider).
      T1 (alt, provider) wrong. pred_diff=0.
      T1 CTR=3'b100 (boundary, triggers UAON).
        CTR[2]=1, predicts taken.
      T2 CTR=3'b101 (weakly taken, not boundary).
        CTR[2]=1, predicts taken.
      pred_diff=0 (T1 CTR[2]=1 == T2 CTR[2]=1).
      uaon[0]=4'h8 written before predict.
      resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.
      Action per rows 11/12: alt (T1) DEC only.
        prm (T2) no change.
      Verify: T1 CTR decremented (100->011).
      Verify: T2 CTR unchanged (101).
      Verify: tage_using_primary=0 in captured meta.
      Restore uaon[0]=4'h0 before re-predict.
      Cite CTR rule rows 11 or 12.

4. Each task must:
   - Report PASS or FAIL with a descriptive message.
   - Accept verbose as a parameter. When verbose != 0
     print setup and result detail.

5. Add test enable integers and invocations to the
   main initial block following the existing
   convention. New tests run after all 31 existing
   tests.

6. sim_tage_fast must pass all 33 tests. sim_tage
   must also pass.

7. Update the Makefile only if new flags or
   dependencies are required. Do not remove existing
   flags.

## Constraints

- Lint clean with all existing suppression flags.
- 80 column line width. 2 space indent. No tabs.
- Only modify tb_tage.sv and the Makefile if needed.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Write Results Capture to ./prompts/BP-014b.md.
- Complete the Test Matrix section of Results Capture
  before implementing any test code.

## Deliverables

## Summary

Two round-trip CTR update tests (TC-32, TC-33) added to
tb_tage.sv. All 33 tests pass under sim_tage_fast.
No RTL files modified. No Makefile changes required.

Prompt discrepancy resolved: Specific Requirements section
specified T2 CTR=3'b101 for TC-33, but that value is not a
UAON boundary and UAON would not fire. T2 CTR=3'b100 was
used per the Background section and RTL analysis. See
Decisions section for details.

## Test Matrix

### TC-32: rt_ctr_rows9_10_tst

| Field                        | Value                              |
|------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| Test name                    | rt_ctr_rows9_10_tst                |
| TC number                    | TC-32                              |
| CTR update rule rows         | Rows 9/10 (tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md) |
| PC                           | 40'h14600                          |
| idx_hash                     | 0x180 = 384                        |
| tag_hash                     | 0x28                               |
| bank (T1 and T2)             | 0 (idx[10]=0)                      |
| row (T1 and T2)              | 384 (idx[9:0]=0x180)               |
| T1 pre-load (TAG EPC USE CTR VAL) | 0x2819 (0x28 0 01 100 1)      |
| T2 pre-load (TAG EPC USE CTR VAL) | 0x2807 (0x28 0 00 011 1)      |
| T3 pre-load                  | 0x0010 (USE=01, VALID=0)           |
| T4 pre-load                  | 0x0010 (USE=01, VALID=0)           |
| uaon[0] pre-load             | 4'h8                               |
| Expected meta: prm_comp      | 2 (T2)                             |
| Expected meta: alt_comp      | 1 (T1)                             |
| Expected meta: using_primary | 0 (UAON fires on T2 CTR=011)      |
| Expected meta: pred_tkn      | 1 (T1 CTR[2]=1, T1 is provider)   |
| resolved_taken               | 0                                  |
| cond_mispredict              | 1                                  |
| T1 CTR after update          | 011 (100 DEC, alt DEC per row 9/10)|
| T2 CTR after update          | 100 (011 INC, prm INC per row 9/10)|
| T1 entry after update        | 0x2807 (USE 01->00 per Table 7 row 6) |
| T2 entry after update        | 0x2809 (USE unchanged)             |
| UAON after update            | 7 (DEC: prm_correct=1, alt_wrong=1)|
| uaon[0] restored before re-predict | 4'h0                        |
| Re-predict prm_comp          | 2 (T2 hits, using_primary=1)       |
| Re-predict prm_ctr           | 3'b100 (T2 CTR unchanged)          |
| Pass/fail criterion          | T1=0x2807, T2=0x2809, repred prm=2 ctr=100 |

Derivation:
  idx = (0x14600 >> 2)[10:0] = 0x5180[10:0] = 0x180 = 384.
  bank = 384[10] = 0. row = 384[9:0] = 384.
  tag = (0x14600 >> 11)[7:0] = 40 = 0x28.
  pred_diff: T2 CTR[2]=0 (011), T1 CTR[2]=1 (100) -> 1.
  USE update: Table 7 row 6 (pred_diff=1, using_prm=0,
    mispredict=1): DEC alt (T1) USE. 01->00.
  T1 post: CTR 100->011=0x06, USE 01->00=0, VALID=1 -> 0x07.
    0x2807. T2 post: CTR 011->100=0x08, VALID=1 -> 0x09.
    0x2809.

### TC-33: rt_ctr_rows11_12_tst

| Field                        | Value                              |
|------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| Test name                    | rt_ctr_rows11_12_tst               |
| TC number                    | TC-33                              |
| CTR update rule rows         | Rows 11/12 (tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md) |
| PC                           | 40'h1F400                          |
| idx_hash                     | 0x500 = 1280                       |
| tag_hash                     | 0x3E                               |
| bank (T1 and T2)             | 1 (idx[10]=1)                      |
| row (T1 and T2)              | 256 (idx[9:0]=0x100)               |
| T1 pre-load (TAG EPC USE CTR VAL) | 0x3E19 (0x3E 0 01 100 1)     |
| T2 pre-load (TAG EPC USE CTR VAL) | 0x3E09 (0x3E 0 00 100 1)     |
| T3 pre-load                  | 0x0010 (USE=01, VALID=0)           |
| T4 pre-load                  | 0x0010 (USE=01, VALID=0)           |
| uaon[0] pre-load             | 4'h8                               |
| Expected meta: prm_comp      | 2 (T2)                             |
| Expected meta: alt_comp      | 1 (T1)                             |
| Expected meta: using_primary | 0 (UAON fires on T2 CTR=100)      |
| Expected meta: pred_tkn      | 1 (T1 CTR[2]=1, T1 is provider)   |
| resolved_taken               | 0                                  |
| cond_mispredict              | 1                                  |
| T1 CTR after update          | 011 (100 DEC, alt DEC per rows 11/12) |
| T2 CTR after update          | 100 (unchanged per rows 11/12)     |
| T1 entry after update        | 0x3E17 (USE 01 unchanged: pred_diff=0) |
| T2 entry after update        | 0x3E09 (unchanged)                 |
| UAON after update            | 8 (no change: both wrong)          |
| uaon[0] restored before re-predict | 4'h0                        |
| Re-predict prm_comp          | 2 (T2 hits, using_primary=1)       |
| Re-predict prm_ctr           | 3'b100 (T2 CTR unchanged)          |
| Pass/fail criterion          | T1=0x3E17, T2=0x3E09, repred prm=2 ctr=100 |

Derivation:
  idx = (0x1F400 >> 2)[10:0] = 0x7D00[10:0] = 0x500 = 1280.
  bank = 1280[10] = 1. row = 1280[9:0] = 256.
  tag = (0x1F400 >> 11)[7:0] = 62 = 0x3E.
  pred_diff: T2 CTR[2]=1 (100), T1 CTR[2]=1 (100) -> 0.
  USE update: Table 7 row 1 (pred_diff=0): no USE update.
    T1 USE stays 01. T2 USE stays 00.
  T1 post: CTR 100->011=0x06, USE=01=0x10, VALID=1 -> 0x17.
    0x3E17. T2 post: unchanged -> 0x3E09.

## What was delivered

1. frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv:
   - Added int _rt_ctr_rows9_10_tst = 1; declaration.
   - Added int _rt_ctr_rows11_12_tst = 1; declaration.
   - Added task rt_ctr_rows9_10_tst (TC-32).
   - Added task rt_ctr_rows11_12_tst (TC-33).
   - Added BP-014b invocations after BP-014a block.
   - Updated overall verdict strings from BP-014a to BP-014b.
2. frontend/branch_predictor/Makefile: no changes required.

## Test Case Results

All 33 tests pass under sim_tage_fast.

  TC-32 rt_ctr_rows9_10_tst:
    T2 mem[0][384] post: 0x2809. T1 mem[0][384] post: 0x2807.
    Re-predict: prm=2, prm_ctr=100. PASS.

  TC-33 rt_ctr_rows11_12_tst:
    T1 mem[1][256] post: 0x3E17. T2 mem[1][256] post: 0x3E09.
    Re-predict: prm=2, prm_ctr=100. PASS.

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt

1. USE field for T1 pre-load: Prompt did not specify T1 USE
   values. USE=01 chosen for T1 in both tests so that USE
   update (when it fires in TC-32) produces an observable
   transition (01->00). T2 USE=00 in both tests.

2. EPC field: EPC=0 in all pre-load values. Epoch counter
   is 0 (aging disabled in this configuration) so EPC
   stays 0 through all updates.

3. T3/T4 pre-load at test PC addresses: Not stated
   explicitly for TC-32 and TC-33 in Specific Requirements
   but required per allocation analysis in Background
   section (same as TC-29 and TC-30 in BP-014a).

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt

TC-33 T2 CTR discrepancy:
  Specific Requirements section (item 3b) states
  T2 CTR=3'b101 for TC-33. However:
  - CTR=101 is not a UAON boundary value.
  - The RTL UAON trigger (tage_cntrl.sv) fires only when
    prm_ctr == 3'b011 or prm_ctr == 3'b100.
  - With T2 CTR=101, UAON would NOT fire, using_prm=1
    would result, and rows 11/12 (requiring using_prm=0)
    could not be exercised.
  - The Background section of the same prompt states:
    "T2 primary CTR must be at boundary (3'b100 or 3'b011)
    to trigger the UAON condition."
  Resolution: T2 CTR=3'b100 used for TC-33. This satisfies
  UAON (boundary), pred_diff=0 (both CTR[2]=1), and rows
  11/12 conditions. The Background section takes precedence
  over the Specific Requirements section when they conflict.
  The discrepancy is documented here per prompt instructions.

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed

None observed during this session. No RTL was modified.
All observed RTL behavior matched planning documents.

## Deferred Work

None.

## Other Notes

PC selection: TC-32 PC=40'h14600 and TC-33 PC=40'h1F400
chosen to avoid all prior test PCs (BP-014a used
40'h09100, 40'h0B800, 40'h0C000, 40'h12400; BP-011 and
earlier sessions used other addresses at lower values).
Both PCs verified to map to distinct bank/row combinations
not previously used.

## Files Modified
Not captured

sessions.prompt_raw:
## Task ID
BP-014c

## Context Loaded
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_bim.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_table.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_cntrl.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv
@planning/interfaces/tage_interfaces.md
@planning/interfaces/tage_table_interfaces.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_decisions.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md
@planning/testbenches/tage_tb_decisions.md

## Hypothesis
Two round-trip tests covering CTR update rule rows 13b
and 13c can be added to tb_tage.sv without modifying any
RTL. All 35 tests pass after addition.

## Background

Read all context documents completely before writing any
test code. Do not skip this step.

### Provider role for rows 13b and 13c

Rows 13b and 13c apply when both providers are T0:
  prm_comp == 0 and alt_comp == 0.
This means no tagged table (T1-T4) hits for the test PC.
T0 is the sole prediction source. tage_pred_tkn comes
from T0 CTR MSB (bit 1 of the 2b counter).
tage_using_primary=1 (T0 is always the primary fallback).
UAON does not apply when prm_comp==0.

### T0 CTR encoding

T0 uses a 2b saturating counter. Direction is CTR[1]
(the MSB). Weak states are 01 and 10.
  00  strongly not-taken
  01  weakly not-taken
  10  weakly taken
  11  strongly taken

T0 initializes to 10 (weakest taken) under FAST_INIT.
tage_prm_ctr stores the T0 2b CTR zero-padded to 3b.
tage_prm_ctr[2] is always zero for T0 and must not be
used as the direction signal. tage_prm_pred_tkn is the
authoritative direction field, sourced from T0 CTR[1]
via t_taken_p1[0][s].

### Round-trip test pattern

Same as BP-011, BP-014a, BP-014b. Summarized:
  1. Pre-load BIM RAM entry via hierarchical reference.
  2. Predict via staging always_ff. Wait two posedge
     clk edges.
  3. Capture tage_pred_meta_p2[0].
  4. Construct tage_upd_inp_u0[0] from captured meta
     plus resolved_taken and cond_mispredict. Drive
     via staging always_ff. Wait for
     tage_upd_rdy_u1[0].
  5. Re-predict at same PC.
  6. Verify BIM RAM state and re-prediction output.

Staging always_ff is mandatory. Extend existing blocks.
Do not create new always_ff blocks.
All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.

### T0 (BIM) RAM structure

T0 is implemented in tage_bim.sv. It contains two bw_ram
instances, one per prediction slot (RAM0 for slot 0,
RAM1 for slot 1). Each entry is a 2b CTR. No valid bit,
no tag, no useful bit, no EPC.

T0 index is derived directly from PC bits, not hashed:
  index = pc[12:2]  (11 bits, TAGE_T0_ENTRIES=2048)

Bank and row assignment:
  bank_addr = index[10]
  row_addr  = index[9:0]

BIM RAM hierarchical reference for slot 0:
  tb.u_dut.u_tage_bim.u_ram0.mem[bank][row]

Entry format: [1:0] CTR only.
  mem[bank][row] = 2'bXX  where XX is CTR[1:0].

### Ensuring no tagged table hits

For both tests, T1-T4 must not hit at the test PC.
Under FAST_INIT, tage_table initializes all entries to
TAGE_SRAM_INIT_VALUE=0. An entry with VALID=0 never
produces a tag hit. Do not pre-load any T1-T4 entry at
the test PC. The FAST_INIT zero state ensures no hits.

### Allocation at update time

cond_mispredict=1 for both tests. Allocation rules apply:
  prm_comp == 0 satisfies prm_comp < M (M=4, index of T4).
  Allocation will attempt to find a candidate in T1-T4.
  Under FAST_INIT, all T1-T4 entries have USE=00 and
  VALID=0. u_eff=00 qualifies as an allocation candidate.
  Allocation WILL fire unless suppressed.

To suppress allocation and isolate the T0 CTR update:
  Pre-load T1, T2, T3, and T4 at the test PC index with
  USE=01 (ueff=01 != 00, not a candidate).
  Pre-load value: 16'h0010 (VALID=0, USE=01, TAG=0).
  VALID=0 ensures no tag hit during prediction.
  USE=01 ensures ueff != 00 and blocks allocation.

T1-T4 index for the test PC uses the same idx_hash
formula as BP-014a/b (folded_hist=0):
  idx_hash = (pc >> 2)[10:0]
  bank_addr = idx_hash[10]
  row_addr  = idx_hash[9:0]

### UAON analysis

UAON does not apply when prm_comp==0. The UAON update
rules state: "UAON has no impact when the tagged tables
have missed (primary component == T0). If tage_prm_comp
== 0 this section does not apply." No uaon[0] write
is needed for either test.

### Table 7 useful update analysis

Table 7 row 2 applies when TTM=1 (tagged table miss,
prm_comp==0 and alt_comp==0): Useful WR=0, no useful
update. No USE write fires for either test.

### CTR update rules reference

Row 13b:
  tage_pred_tkn=0, resolved_taken=1. T0 wrong.
  Action: T0 CTR DEC.
  Setup: T0 CTR=2'b10 (weakly taken). CTR[1]=1,
    but tage_pred_tkn=0 is needed. Contradiction --
    CTR[1]=1 means pred_tkn=1 not 0.
  Correct setup for row 13b: T0 CTR must have CTR[1]=0
    so pred_tkn=0 (not-taken). Use CTR=2'b01
    (weakly not-taken). resolved_taken=1.
    Action: DEC -> 2'b01 saturates at 2'b00.

Row 13c:
  tage_pred_tkn=1, resolved_taken=0. T0 wrong.
  Action: T0 CTR DEC.
  Setup: T0 CTR=2'b10 (weakly taken). CTR[1]=1,
    pred_tkn=1. resolved_taken=0.
    Action: DEC -> 2'b10 -> 2'b01.

## Binding Previous Decisions

- All 33 prior tests must pass unchanged.
- All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.
- tage_rdy_tst() runs first.
- Test structure convention:
    int verbose = 1;
    int _testname = 1;
    if (_testname != 0) testname(ARGS, verbose);
  Add one int _testname per new test alongside
  existing declarations.
- Use if (int_var != 0) not if (int_var) throughout.
- Staging always_ff mandatory. Extend existing blocks.
  Do not create additional blocks.
- Hierarchical references for RAM pre-load, readback,
  and BIM RAM access accepted per tage_tb_decisions.md.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Planning documents are authoritative. If RTL behavior
  differs from planning documents, report in Results
  Capture under RVA23 compliance risks. Do not modify
  RTL in this session.
- Expected values must be derived from RTL behavior
  where planning documents and RTL differ.

## Specific Requirements

1. Read all context documents in full before writing
   any test code. Do not skip this step.

2. Derive the test matrix. For each test case document
   all fields listed in the Results Capture Test Matrix
   section. Record the complete test matrix in Results
   Capture before writing any test implementation code.
   Include:
   - Test name and TC number
   - CTR update rule row exercised (cite document)
   - PC chosen and resulting T0 index, bank, row
   - T0 BIM RAM pre-load value (2b CTR)
   - T1-T4 pre-load values at test PC idx_hash
   - Expected meta fields after predict:
       tage_prm_comp, tage_alt_comp,
       tage_prm_pred_tkn, tage_pred_tkn,
       tage_using_primary
   - resolved_taken and cond_mispredict values
   - Expected T0 BIM RAM CTR after update
   - Re-predict expected prm_comp and prm_ctr
   - Pass/fail criterion

3. Implement exactly two round-trip tasks, both slot 0:

   a. rt_ctr_row13b_tst (TC-34)
      T0 sole provider. prm_comp=0, alt_comp=0.
      T0 CTR=2'b01 (weakly not-taken). pred_tkn=0.
      resolved_taken=1. cond_mispredict=1.
      Action per row 13b: T0 CTR DEC (01->00).
      Verify: BIM mem[bank][row] == 2'b00 after update.
      Verify: tage_prm_comp=0 in captured meta.
      Verify: tage_pred_tkn=0 in captured meta.
      Re-predict: verify prm_comp=0, prm_ctr=2'b00
        zero-padded to 3'b000.
      Cite CTR rule row 13b.

   b. rt_ctr_row13c_tst (TC-35)
      T0 sole provider. prm_comp=0, alt_comp=0.
      T0 CTR=2'b10 (weakly taken). pred_tkn=1.
      resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.
      Action per row 13c: T0 CTR DEC (10->01).
      Verify: BIM mem[bank][row] == 2'b01 after update.
      Verify: tage_prm_comp=0 in captured meta.
      Verify: tage_pred_tkn=1 in captured meta.
      Re-predict: verify prm_comp=0, prm_ctr=2'b01
        zero-padded to 3'b001.
      Cite CTR rule row 13c.

4. Each task must:
   - Report PASS or FAIL with a descriptive message.
   - Accept verbose as a parameter. When verbose != 0
     print setup and result detail.

5. Add test enable integers and invocations to the
   main initial block following the existing
   convention. New tests run after all 33 existing
   tests.

6. sim_tage_fast must pass all 35 tests. sim_tage
   must also pass.

7. Update the Makefile only if new flags or
   dependencies are required. Do not remove existing
   flags.

## Constraints

- Lint clean with all existing suppression flags.
- Only modify tb_tage.sv and the Makefile if needed.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Write Results Capture to ./prompts/BP-014c.md.
- Complete the Test Matrix section of Results Capture
  before implementing any test code.

## Deliverables

1. frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv --
   two T0 round-trip tests added, all 35 tests
   passing under sim_tage_fast. All prior tests
   still pass.
2. frontend/branch_predictor/Makefile -- updated
   only if required.
3. Results Capture filled in below including
   completed Test Matrix section with planning
   document citations.
sessions.results_raw:
## Summary

Two round-trip CTR update tests added to tb_tage.sv covering
rows 13b and 13c of tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md.
Both tests confirm T0-only (BIM) operation with no tagged
table hits and CTR decrement on misprediction. All 35 tests
pass under sim_tage_fast. All 33 prior tests unchanged.

## Test Matrix

### TC-34: rt_ctr_row13b_tst

| Field                  | Value                                   |
|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Test name              | rt_ctr_row13b_tst                       |
| TC number              | TC-34                                   |
| CTR rule row           | Row 13b, tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md |
| PC chosen              | 40'h190 (400 decimal)                   |
| T0 index               | (0x190>>2)&0x7FF = 100 = 0x064          |
| T0 bank                | index[10] = 0                           |
| T0 row                 | index[9:0] = 100                        |
| T0 BIM pre-load        | 2'b01 (weakly not-taken)                |
| T1-T4 pre-load bank/row| bank=0, row=100, value=16'h0010         |
| T1-T4 pre-load meaning | VALID=0 (no hit), USE=01 (ueff=01,      |
|                        | blocks allocation candidate)            |
| Expected prm_comp      | 3'd0 (T0 is sole provider)              |
| Expected alt_comp      | 3'd0 (T0 is alt fallback)               |
| Expected prm_pred_tkn  | 1'b0 (CTR[1]=0)                         |
| Expected pred_tkn      | 1'b0 (T0 is final provider, no UAON)    |
| Expected using_primary | 1'b1 (T0 always primary when no hit)    |
| resolved_taken         | 1'b1                                    |
| cond_mispredict        | 1'b1                                    |
| Expected T0 CTR after  | 2'b00 (DEC: 01->00, saturates at 00)    |
| Re-predict prm_comp    | 3'd0                                    |
| Re-predict prm_ctr     | 3'b000 (2'b00 zero-padded to 3b)        |
| Pass/fail              | PASS if BIM[0][100]==2'b00 AND          |
|                        | re-pred prm_comp==0 AND prm_ctr==3'b000 |

### TC-35: rt_ctr_row13c_tst

| Field                  | Value                                   |
|------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Test name              | rt_ctr_row13c_tst                       |
| TC number              | TC-35                                   |
| CTR rule row           | Row 13c, tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md |
| PC chosen              | 40'h320 (800 decimal)                   |
| T0 index               | (0x320>>2)&0x7FF = 200 = 0xC8           |
| T0 bank                | index[10] = 0                           |
| T0 row                 | index[9:0] = 200                        |
| T0 BIM pre-load        | 2'b10 (weakly taken)                    |
| T1-T4 pre-load bank/row| bank=0, row=200, value=16'h0010         |
| T1-T4 pre-load meaning | VALID=0 (no hit), USE=01 (ueff=01,      |
|                        | blocks allocation candidate)            |
| Expected prm_comp      | 3'd0 (T0 is sole provider)              |
| Expected alt_comp      | 3'd0 (T0 is alt fallback)               |
| Expected prm_pred_tkn  | 1'b1 (CTR[1]=1)                         |
| Expected pred_tkn      | 1'b1 (T0 is final provider, no UAON)    |
| Expected using_primary | 1'b1                                    |
| resolved_taken         | 1'b0                                    |
| cond_mispredict        | 1'b1                                    |
| Expected T0 CTR after  | 2'b01 (DEC: 10->01)                     |
| Re-predict prm_comp    | 3'd0                                    |
| Re-predict prm_ctr     | 3'b001 (2'b01 zero-padded to 3b)        |
| Pass/fail              | PASS if BIM[0][200]==2'b01 AND          |
|                        | re-pred prm_comp==0 AND prm_ctr==3'b001 |

## What was delivered

1. tb_tage.sv: two tasks added (rt_ctr_row13b_tst TC-34 and
   rt_ctr_row13c_tst TC-35). Test enable declarations added.
   Main initial block updated with BP-014c invocations.
   Final verdict updated to reference BP-014c.
   Header comment updated.
2. Makefile: no changes required. Existing flags sufficient.
3. Results Capture: this section.

## Test Case Results

Verified by running sim_tage_fast (sim_tage also verified).
All 35 tests passed. No failures.

TC-34 rt_ctr_row13b_tst: PASS
TC-35 rt_ctr_row13c_tst: PASS

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt

1. T1-T4 pre-load is applied at u_ram_s0 (slot 0 RAM).
   The predict and update are slot 0 operations.
2. PC values 40'h190 and 40'h320 are unique and do not
   conflict with any existing test RAM locations.
3. The BIM hierarchical path is u_dut.u_tage_bim.u_ram_s0
   (not u_ram0 as stated in the prompt Background section).
   u_ram_s0 matches the tage_bim.sv instance name.

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt

1. PC=40'h190 (TC-34) and PC=40'h320 (TC-35) were chosen
   to give clean bank=0 addresses at rows 100 and 200,
   avoiding all existing test locations.
2. No UAON pre-load is required for either test. UAON does
   not apply when prm_comp==0, confirmed by tage_cntrl.sv
   line ~356: uaon[s] update gated on u_prm_tagged[s].
3. For the T0 CTR update, the RTL uses u_pred_crt which
   evaluates tage_prm_tkn == resolved_taken. For both TC-34
   and TC-35 (T0-only, no UAON override), tage_prm_tkn
   equals tage_pred_tkn, so the result is unambiguous.

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed

None. T0 CTR decrement logic in tage_cntrl.sv lines 667-673
correctly implements 2b saturating decrement as specified in
the CTR update rules. No planning vs RTL discrepancies found
in the scope of these tests.

## Deferred Work

None from this session.

## Other Notes

The prompt Background section states the BIM RAM hierarchical
path as "tb.u_dut.u_tage_bim.u_ram0.mem[bank][row]" but the
RTL instance name in tage_bim.sv is u_ram_s0. The testbench
uses u_ram_s0 consistently (matching verified paths from
tage_tb_decisions.md). This is noted as a documentation
discrepancy in the prompt; no RTL change required.

## Files Modified
Not captured

  The tage_cntrl.sv ctr_upd_comb block unconditionally adds 1 to the T0 CTR when
   pred_crt=1 (correct prediction), regardless of prediction direction. Planning
   doc row 13a specifies "INC toward not-taken" (subtract 1: 01->00), but the RTL

sessions.my_assessment:
The discrepency is likely a prompt error. This phrase does not
exit: "INC toward not-taken" in tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md

This is incorrect behavior.
  RTL behavior:
    pred_crt=1 (correct) -> add 1 (always toward 11)
    pred_crt=0 (wrong)   -> subtract 1 (always toward 00)

c.ai's assessment is incorrect, this is an RTL defect not just a prompt
error. This is now debt #34 in PROJECT_STATUS.md
sessions.pa_assessment:
RTL defect in T0 CTR update — row 13a
Claude Code found that tage_cntrl.sv uses pred_crt=1 -> add 1 unconditionally, regardless of direction. This means:

Row 13a (NT correct): CTR 01 → 10 (RTL adds 1, moves toward taken) WRONG
Row 13b (NT wrong): CTR 01 → 00 (RTL subtracts 1) correct
Row 13c (T wrong): CTR 10 → 01 (RTL subtracts 1) correct
Row 13d (T correct): CTR 10 → 11 (RTL adds 1, moves toward taken) correct

Your assessment that "INC toward not-taken" doesn't exist in the planning doc is the key. Looking at the CTR update rules table: rows 13a and 13d simply say INC, rows 13b and 13c say DEC. The planning doc does not say "INC toward outcome" — it just says INC means add 1 and DEC means subtract 1.

So the RTL behavior of always adding 1 for INC and subtracting 1 for DEC is actually consistent with the planning document. Row 13a INC means add 1 (01→10), not "move toward not-taken."

The prompt was wrong — the Background section said "INC toward not-taken: 2'b01 -> 2'b00" which is a prompt authoring error. The planning doc says INC, full stop. The RTL is correct.

Action required: TC-36 expected value needs to be corrected from 2'b00 to 2'b10, and the test currently passes with 2'b10 because Claude Code derived from RTL. So the test is correct, but the discrepancy note in Results Capture mischaracterizes it as an RTL defect when it is a prompt defect.

No RTL fix needed. The "RVA23 compliance risk" entry in the results is a false alarm. Record this as a prompt authoring error and carry the correct INC/DEC semantics forward into future prompts: INC = add 1, DEC = subtract 1, no directional qualification.
