## Task ID
BP-014d

## Context Loaded
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_bim.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_table.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_cntrl.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv
@planning/interfaces/tage_interfaces.md
@planning/interfaces/tage_table_interfaces.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_decisions.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md
@planning/testbenches/tage_tb_decisions.md

## Hypothesis
Two round-trip tests covering CTR update rule rows 13a
and 13d can be added to tb_tage.sv without modifying any
RTL. All 37 tests pass after addition.

## Background

Read all context documents completely before writing any
test code. Do not skip this step.

This session is symmetric to BP-014c which covered rows
13b and 13c (T0 wrong, CTR DEC). Rows 13a and 13d cover
the correct-prediction cases (T0 correct, CTR INC). The
setup is identical to BP-014c except resolved_taken agrees
with tage_pred_tkn and cond_mispredict=0.

### Provider role for rows 13a and 13d

Rows 13a and 13d apply when both providers are T0:
  prm_comp == 0 and alt_comp == 0.
No tagged table (T1-T4) hits for the test PC.
T0 is the sole prediction source. tage_pred_tkn comes
from T0 CTR[1]. tage_using_primary=1.
UAON does not apply when prm_comp==0.

### T0 CTR encoding

T0 uses a 2b saturating counter. Direction is CTR[1].
  00  strongly not-taken
  01  weakly not-taken
  10  weakly taken
  11  strongly taken

tage_prm_ctr stores T0 CTR zero-padded to 3b.
tage_prm_ctr[2] is always zero for T0 and must not be
used as the direction signal. tage_prm_pred_tkn is the
authoritative direction field, sourced from T0 CTR[1]
via t_taken_p1[0][s].

### Round-trip test pattern

Same as BP-011 through BP-014c:
  1. Pre-load BIM RAM entry via hierarchical reference.
  2. Predict via staging always_ff. Wait two posedge
     clk edges.
  3. Capture tage_pred_meta_p2[0].
  4. Construct tage_upd_inp_u0[0] from captured meta
     plus resolved_taken and cond_mispredict. Drive
     via staging always_ff. Wait for
     tage_upd_rdy_u1[0].
  5. Re-predict at same PC.
  6. Verify BIM RAM state and re-prediction output.

Staging always_ff is mandatory. Extend existing blocks.
Do not create new always_ff blocks.

### T0 (BIM) RAM structure

T0 is implemented in tage_bim.sv. Each entry is a 2b
CTR. No valid bit, no tag, no useful bit, no EPC.

T0 index:
  index = pc[12:2]  (11 bits)
  bank  = index[10]
  row   = index[9:0]

BIM RAM hierarchical reference for slot 0
(from tage_tb_decisions.md):
  tb.u_dut.u_tage_bim.u_ram_s0.mem[bank][row]

Entry format: [1:0] CTR only.

### Ensuring no tagged table hits

Under FAST_INIT all T1-T4 entries initialize to
TAGE_SRAM_INIT_VALUE=0. VALID=0 prevents any tag hit.
Do not pre-load any T1-T4 entry at the test PC.
The zero state ensures no tagged table hits.

### Allocation analysis

cond_mispredict=0 for both rows 13a and 13d (correct
prediction). Allocation trigger requires cond_mispredict=1.
Allocation does not fire. No T1-T4 pre-load needed to
suppress allocation.

### UAON analysis

UAON does not apply when prm_comp==0. No uaon[0] write
needed for either test.

### Table 7 useful update analysis

Table 7 row 2 applies when TTM=1 (prm_comp==0 and
alt_comp==0): Useful WR=0. No USE write fires.

### CTR update rules reference

Row 13a:
  pred_tkn=0, resolved_taken=0. T0 correct.
  Action: T0 CTR INC toward not-taken.
  Setup: T0 CTR=2'b01 (weakly not-taken). CTR[1]=0,
    pred_tkn=0. resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=0.
  INC toward strongly not-taken: 2'b01 -> 2'b00.
  Verify post-update CTR = 2'b00.

Row 13d:
  pred_tkn=1, resolved_taken=1. T0 correct.
  Action: T0 CTR INC toward taken.
  Setup: T0 CTR=2'b10 (weakly taken). CTR[1]=1,
    pred_tkn=1. resolved_taken=1. cond_mispredict=0.
  INC toward strongly taken: 2'b10 -> 2'b11.
  Verify post-update CTR = 2'b11.

### PC selection

Choose PCs not used in any prior test. Prior sessions
used (among others):
  BP-014a: 40'h09100, 40'h0B800, 40'h0C000, 40'h12400
  BP-014b: 40'h14600, 40'h1F400
  BP-014c: 40'h190,   40'h320
Do not reuse these PCs.

## Binding Previous Decisions

- All 35 prior tests must pass unchanged.
- All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.
- tage_rdy_tst() runs first.
- Test structure convention:
    int verbose = 1;
    int _testname = 1;
    if (_testname != 0) testname(ARGS, verbose);
  Add one int _testname per new test alongside
  existing declarations.
- Use if (int_var != 0) not if (int_var) throughout.
- Staging always_ff mandatory. Extend existing blocks.
  Do not create additional blocks.
- Hierarchical references for RAM pre-load, readback,
  and BIM RAM access accepted per tage_tb_decisions.md.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Planning documents are authoritative. If RTL behavior
  differs from planning documents, report in Results
  Capture under RVA23 compliance risks. Do not modify
  RTL in this session.
- Expected values must be derived from RTL behavior
  where planning documents and RTL differ.

## Specific Requirements

1. Read all context documents in full before writing
   any test code. Do not skip this step.

2. Derive the test matrix. Record in Results Capture
   before writing any test code. Include:
   - Test name and TC number
   - CTR update rule row exercised (cite document)
   - PC chosen and resulting T0 index, bank, row
   - T0 BIM RAM pre-load value (2b CTR)
   - Expected meta fields after predict:
       tage_prm_comp, tage_alt_comp,
       tage_prm_pred_tkn, tage_pred_tkn,
       tage_using_primary
   - resolved_taken and cond_mispredict values
   - Expected T0 BIM RAM CTR after update
   - Re-predict expected prm_comp and prm_ctr
   - Pass/fail criterion

3. Implement exactly two round-trip tasks, both slot 0:

   a. rt_ctr_row13a_tst (TC-36)
      T0 sole provider. prm_comp=0, alt_comp=0.
      T0 CTR=2'b01 (weakly not-taken). pred_tkn=0.
      resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=0.
      Action per row 13a: T0 CTR INC (01->00).
      Verify: BIM mem[bank][row] == 2'b00 after update.
      Verify: tage_prm_comp=0 in captured meta.
      Verify: tage_pred_tkn=0 in captured meta.
      Re-predict: verify prm_comp=0, prm_ctr=3'b000.
      Cite CTR rule row 13a.

   b. rt_ctr_row13d_tst (TC-37)
      T0 sole provider. prm_comp=0, alt_comp=0.
      T0 CTR=2'b10 (weakly taken). pred_tkn=1.
      resolved_taken=1. cond_mispredict=0.
      Action per row 13d: T0 CTR INC (10->11).
      Verify: BIM mem[bank][row] == 2'b11 after update.
      Verify: tage_prm_comp=0 in captured meta.
      Verify: tage_pred_tkn=1 in captured meta.
      Re-predict: verify prm_comp=0, prm_ctr=3'b011.
      Cite CTR rule row 13d.

4. Each task must:
   - Report PASS or FAIL with a descriptive message.
   - Accept verbose as a parameter. When verbose != 0
     print setup and result detail.

5. Add test enable integers and invocations to the
   main initial block following the existing convention.
   New tests run after all 35 existing tests.

6. sim_tage_fast must pass all 37 tests. sim_tage
   must also pass.

7. Update the Makefile only if new flags or
   dependencies are required. Do not remove existing
   flags.

## Constraints

- Lint clean with all existing suppression flags.
- Only modify tb_tage.sv and the Makefile if needed.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Write Results Capture to ./prompts/BP-014d.md.
- Complete the Test Matrix section of Results Capture
  before implementing any test code.

## Deliverables

1. frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv --
   two T0 correct-prediction round-trip tests added,
   all 37 tests passing under sim_tage_fast. All prior
   tests still pass.
2. frontend/branch_predictor/Makefile -- updated
   only if required.
3. Results Capture filled in below including completed
   Test Matrix section with planning document citations.
sessions.results_raw:
## Summary

Two round-trip CTR update tests added to tb_tage.sv covering rows 13a
and 13d of tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md. Both tests use T0 (BIM) as
the sole provider (prm_comp==0, alt_comp==0). cond_mispredict=0 for
both (correct predictions). All 37 tests pass under sim_tage_fast and
sim_tage. All 35 prior tests unchanged.

RTL divergence discovered: tage_cntrl.sv always adds 1 to the T0 CTR
when pred_crt=1, regardless of prediction direction. Planning doc row
13a says INC toward not-taken (subtract 1: 01->00); RTL does add 1
(01->10). TC-36 expected values reflect RTL behavior. Discrepancy
recorded under RVA23 compliance risks.

## Test Matrix

### TC-36: rt_ctr_row13a_tst

| Field                        | Value                                   |
|------------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Test name                    | rt_ctr_row13a_tst                       |
| TC number                    | TC-36                                   |
| CTR rule row                 | 13a (tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md)    |
| Rule description             | prm_comp=0, alt_comp=0, pred_tkn=0,     |
|                              | resolved_taken=0: T0 INC (01->00)       |
| PC chosen                    | 40'h480                                 |
| T0 index (pc[12:2])          | 11'h120 (288 decimal)                   |
| bank (index[10])             | 0                                       |
| row (index[9:0])             | 288                                     |
| T0 BIM pre-load              | 2'b01 (weakly not-taken)                |
| T1-T4 pre-load               | None. FAST_INIT ensures VALID=0 (no     |
|                              | tag hits). cond_mispredict=0 so no      |
|                              | allocation fires.                       |
| tage_prm_comp (expected)     | 0 (T0)                                  |
| tage_alt_comp (expected)     | 0 (T0)                                  |
| tage_prm_pred_tkn (expected) | 0 (CTR[1]=0, weakly NT)                 |
| tage_pred_tkn (expected)     | 0                                       |
| tage_using_primary (expected)| 1                                       |
| resolved_taken               | 0                                       |
| cond_mispredict              | 0                                       |
| Expected T0 CTR after update | 2'b10 (RTL: add 1 when pred_crt: 01->10)|

|                              | with RTL. Values derived from RTL.      |
| Re-predict prm_comp          | 0                                       |
| Re-predict prm_ctr           | 3'b010 (T0 2'b10 zero-padded to 3b)     |

|                              | repred prm_comp==0 AND prm_ctr==3'b010  |

|                              | allocation fires.                       |

| tage_alt_comp (expected)     | 0 (T0)                                  |
| tage_prm_pred_tkn (expected) | 1 (CTR[1]=1, weakly taken)              |
| tage_pred_tkn (expected)     | 1                                       |

| resolved_taken               | 1                                       |
| cond_mispredict              | 0                                       |
| Expected T0 CTR after update | 2'b11 (strongly taken, INC 10->11)      |

| Re-predict prm_ctr           | 3'b011 (T0 2'b11 zero-padded to 3b)     |
| Pass/fail criterion          | BIM mem[0][384]==2'b11 AND              |

TC-26  rt_mispredict_alloc_tst       PASS
TC-27  rt_no_alloc_last_tbl_tst      PASS
TC-28  rt_ctr_rows1_2_tst            PASS
TC-29  rt_ctr_rows3_4_tst            PASS
TC-30  rt_ctr_rows5_6_tst            PASS
TC-31  rt_ctr_rows7_8_tst            PASS
TC-32  rt_ctr_rows9_10_tst           PASS
TC-33  rt_ctr_rows11_12_tst          PASS
TC-34  rt_ctr_row13b_tst             PASS
TC-35  rt_ctr_row13c_tst             PASS
TC-36  rt_ctr_row13a_tst             PASS (new)
TC-37  rt_ctr_row13d_tst             PASS (new)

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt

1. T1-T4 pre-load is not needed for rows 13a and 13d because
   cond_mispredict=0 prevents allocation from firing, and FAST_INIT
   sets all T1-T4 entries to TAGE_SRAM_INIT_VALUE=0 (VALID=0),
   ensuring no tag hits. The prompt confirms this analysis explicitly.

2. PC=40'h480 and PC=40'h600 are confirmed distinct from all prior
   session PCs listed in the Background section.

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt

1. No T1-T4 entries are pre-loaded for either test (contrast with
   13b/13c tests which used USE=01 VALID=0 to block allocation).
   For correct-prediction tests, allocation cannot fire regardless of
   T1-T4 useful state. This simplifies setup and avoids aliasing with
   other test entries. Rationale noted in task comments.

2. Row 288 (bank=0) chosen for TC-36 and row 384 (bank=0) for TC-37.
   Both are in bank 0, consistent with prior tests using bank 0. The
   PCs were selected to yield clean decimal row indices distinct from
   all previously allocated rows.

3. TC-36 expected values (BIM CTR=2'b10, prm_ctr=3'b010) were derived
   from RTL behavior, not planning doc values (2'b00, 3'b000). The
   planning doc says "INC toward NT: 01->00" but the RTL adds 1 when
   pred_crt=1, yielding 01->10. RTL behavior was confirmed by examining
   tage_cntrl.sv ctr_upd_comb block and confirmed by simulation.

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed

RTL/planning divergence in T0 CTR update for row 13a:

  Planning doc (tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md row 13a):
    pred_tkn=0, resolved_taken=0, T0 correct ->
    INC toward not-taken: 2'b01 -> 2'b00 (subtract 1).

  RTL (tage_cntrl.sv ctr_upd_comb, lines ~658-665):
    pred_crt=1 -> INC = add 1 unconditionally ->
    2'b01 -> 2'b10 (increment toward taken side).

  Impact: T0 CTR update for row 13a (predict NT, actual NT)
  moves the counter toward the TAKEN direction instead of
  reinforcing NOT-TAKEN confidence. A branch that correctly
  predicts not-taken will have its BIM counter pushed toward
  taken, potentially degrading prediction accuracy over time.
  This is non-standard BIM behavior.

  Standard 2-bit saturating counter BIM behavior:
    resolved_taken=1 -> add 1 (toward 11)
    resolved_taken=0 -> subtract 1 (toward 00)

  RTL behavior:
    pred_crt=1 (correct) -> add 1 (always toward 11)
    pred_crt=0 (wrong)   -> subtract 1 (always toward 00)

  Row 13d (pred_tkn=1, resolved_taken=1) is unaffected
  because adding 1 to a taken-side CTR (10->11) matches
  the planning doc's "INC toward taken" intent.

  Test TC-36 expected values were derived from RTL behavior
  per Binding Previous Decisions. RTL not modified.

## Deferred Work

N/A

## Other Notes

Row 13a and 13d tests are symmetric to 13b/13c (correct vs. wrong
prediction). Together TC-34 through TC-37 cover all four cells in
the T0-sole-provider quadrant of the CTR update table:
  13a: NT predicted, NT resolved (INC NT)
  13b: NT predicted, T resolved  (DEC)
  13c: T predicted,  NT resolved (DEC)
  13d: T predicted,  T resolved  (INC T)

## Files Modified
Not captured

sessions.prompt_raw:
## Task ID
BP-014e

## Context Loaded
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_bim.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_table.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_cntrl.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv
@planning/interfaces/tage_interfaces.md
@planning/interfaces/tage_table_interfaces.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_decisions.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md
@planning/testbenches/tage_tb_decisions.md

## Hypothesis
Two round-trip tests covering CTR update rule rows 14/15
and 16/17 can be added to tb_tage.sv without modifying
any RTL. All 39 tests pass after addition.

## Background

Read all context documents completely before writing any
test code. Do not skip this step.

### Provider configuration for rows 14-17

Rows 14/15 and 16/17 apply when the primary provider is
a tagged table and the alternate provider is T0:
  prm_comp > 0, alt_comp == 0.

For both tests: T1 is the sole tagged table hit.
T2-T4 do not hit. T0 is always present as the fallback
alternate (alt_comp=0).

  prm_comp = 1 (T1)
  alt_comp = 0 (T0)
  tage_using_primary = 1 (no UAON setup needed)

T0 is not updated in rows 14-17. Only the primary
tagged table (T1) CTR is updated.

### UAON analysis

Keep T1 CTR away from boundary values (3'b011 and
3'b100) so UAON does not fire and using_prm=1 results
naturally without any uaon[0] write.

### pred_diff and Table 7

Both tests use pred_diff=1 to exercise Table 7 USE
updates alongside the CTR update.

pred_diff = (tage_prm_pred_tkn != tage_alt_pred_tkn)
          = (T1 CTR[2] != T0 CTR[1])

To achieve pred_diff=1:
  Set T1 CTR[2]=1 (taken direction).
  Set T0 CTR[1]=0 (not-taken direction), i.e. T0
  CTR=2'b01 (weakly not-taken).

Table 7 with pred_diff=1, TTM=0, using_prm=1:
  Row 3: mispredict=0 -> INC prm USE (rows 16/17 test)
  Row 4: mispredict=1 -> DEC prm USE (rows 14/15 test)

USE update targets prm (T1) at tage_prm_idx.
T0 USE is not affected (T0 has no useful field).

Set T1 initial USE=2'b01 so both INC (01->10) and
DEC (01->00) transitions are observable.

### T1 CTR and USE encoding

T1 uses a 3b saturating counter. Direction = CTR[2].
Choose CTR=3'b101 (wt1, weakly taken, not boundary).
CTR[2]=1 gives prm_pred_tkn=1 (taken).

T1 entry format (from tage_table_interfaces.md):
  [N:8] TAG  [7:6] EPC  [5:4] USE  [3:1] CTR  [0] VALID

With THIS_TAG_BITS=8, MAX_EPC_WIDTH=2, THIS_USE_WIDTH=2,
THIS_CTR_WIDTH=3, THIS_VAL_WIDTH=1:
  Bit layout: [15:8] TAG [7:6] EPC [5:4] USE [3:1] CTR [0] VALID

Pre-load word construction:
  TAG=tag_hash, EPC=2'b00, USE=2'b01, CTR=3'b101, VALID=1
  word = {tag_hash, 2'b00, 2'b01, 3'b101, 1'b1}
       = {tag_hash, 8'h1B}

### T0 (BIM) pre-load

T0 CTR=2'b01 (weakly not-taken). CTR[1]=0, pred_tkn=0.
BIM RAM hierarchical reference for slot 0
(from tage_tb_decisions.md):
  tb.u_dut.u_tage_bim.u_ram_s0.mem[bank][row]

T0 index:
  index = pc[12:2]
  bank  = index[10]
  row   = index[9:0]

### Address derivation

T1-T4 index (folded_hist=0):
  idx_hash = (pc >> 2)[10:0]
  bank     = idx_hash[10]
  row      = idx_hash[9:0]
  tag_hash = (pc >> 11)[7:0]

Note: T0 index and T1-T4 idx_hash use the same PC
bits so bank and row are identical for a given PC.

### Allocation suppression for rows 14/15

cond_mispredict=1 for rows 14/15. Allocation will
attempt to find a candidate in T(prm+1) through T4,
i.e. T2, T3, T4.
Pre-load T2, T3, T4 at the test PC with 16'h0010
(VALID=0, USE=01) to block allocation candidates.
T1 is the pre-loaded hit entry. T2-T4 pre-loads
have VALID=0 so no additional tag hits occur.

For rows 16/17 cond_mispredict=0 so allocation does
not fire. No T2-T4 pre-load needed.

### CTR and USE update rules reference

Rows 14/15 (TC-38):
  prm_comp>0, alt_comp=0, prm wrong.
  resolved_taken=0. T1 CTR[2]=1 (taken). Wrong.
  cond_mispredict=1.
  CTR action: T1 CTR DEC (3'b101 -> 3'b100).
  USE action: Table 7 row 4 (pred_diff=1, using_prm=1,
    mispredict=1): DEC prm USE. T1 USE 01->00.
  T0 CTR: no update (rows 14/15 specify T0 not touched).

Rows 16/17 (TC-39):
  prm_comp>0, alt_comp=0, prm correct.
  resolved_taken=1. T1 CTR[2]=1 (taken). Correct.
  cond_mispredict=0.
  CTR action: T1 CTR INC (3'b101 -> 3'b110).
  USE action: Table 7 row 3 (pred_diff=1, using_prm=1,
    mispredict=0): INC prm USE. T1 USE 01->10.
  T0 CTR: no update (rows 16/17 specify T0 not touched).

### PC selection

Choose PCs not used in any prior test. Prior sessions
used (among others):
  BP-014a: 40'h09100, 40'h0B800, 40'h0C000, 40'h12400
  BP-014b: 40'h14600, 40'h1F400
  BP-014c: 40'h190,   40'h320
  BP-014d: 40'h480,   40'h600
Do not reuse these PCs.

## Binding Previous Decisions

- All 37 prior tests must pass unchanged.
- All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.
- tage_rdy_tst() runs first.
- Test structure convention:
    int verbose = 1;
    int _testname = 1;
    if (_testname != 0) testname(ARGS, verbose);
  Add one int _testname per new test alongside
  existing declarations.
- Use if (int_var != 0) not if (int_var) throughout.
- Staging always_ff mandatory. Extend existing blocks.
  Do not create additional blocks.
- Hierarchical references for RAM pre-load, readback,
  and BIM RAM access accepted per tage_tb_decisions.md.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Planning documents are authoritative. If RTL behavior
  differs from planning documents, report in Results
  Capture under RVA23 compliance risks. Do not modify
  RTL in this session.
- Expected values must be derived from RTL behavior
  where planning documents and RTL differ.

## Specific Requirements

1. Read all context documents in full before writing
   any test code. Do not skip this step.

2. Derive the test matrix. Record in Results Capture
   before writing any test code. Include:
   - Test name and TC number
   - CTR update rule rows exercised (cite document)
   - Table 7 row exercised (cite document)
   - PC chosen and resulting idx_hash, tag_hash,
     bank, row for T1
   - T0 bank and row for the same PC
   - T1 RAM pre-load value (full entry word)
   - T0 BIM RAM pre-load value (2b CTR)
   - T2-T4 pre-load values (if applicable)
   - Expected meta fields after predict:
       tage_prm_comp, tage_alt_comp,
       tage_prm_pred_tkn, tage_alt_pred_tkn,
       tage_pred_tkn, tage_using_primary,
       pred_diff (derived)
   - resolved_taken and cond_mispredict values
   - Expected T1 RAM after update (CTR and USE fields)
   - Expected T0 BIM RAM after update (unchanged)
   - Re-predict expected prm_comp and prm_ctr
   - Pass/fail criterion

3. Implement exactly two round-trip tasks, both slot 0:

   a. rt_ctr_rows14_15_tst (TC-38)
      T1 prm (comp=1), T0 alt (comp=0).
      T1 CTR=3'b101 (wt1). T1 USE=2'b01.
      T0 CTR=2'b01 (weakly not-taken).
      pred_diff=1 (T1 CTR[2]=1, T0 CTR[1]=0).
      using_prm=1. resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.
      Action per rows 14/15: T1 CTR DEC (101->100).
      Action per Table 7 row 4: T1 USE DEC (01->00).
      T0 CTR unchanged.
      Verify: T1 entry CTR=3'b100, USE=2'b00.
      Verify: T0 BIM entry unchanged (2'b01).
      Verify: tage_prm_comp=1, tage_alt_comp=0 in meta.
      Re-predict: verify prm_comp=1, prm_ctr=3'b100.
      Cite CTR rule rows 14 or 15. Cite Table 7 row 4.

   b. rt_ctr_rows16_17_tst (TC-39)
      T1 prm (comp=1), T0 alt (comp=0).
      T1 CTR=3'b101 (wt1). T1 USE=2'b01.
      T0 CTR=2'b01 (weakly not-taken).
      pred_diff=1 (T1 CTR[2]=1, T0 CTR[1]=0).
      using_prm=1. resolved_taken=1. cond_mispredict=0.
      Action per rows 16/17: T1 CTR INC (101->110).
      Action per Table 7 row 3: T1 USE INC (01->10).
      T0 CTR unchanged.
      Verify: T1 entry CTR=3'b110, USE=2'b10.
      Verify: T0 BIM entry unchanged (2'b01).
      Verify: tage_prm_comp=1, tage_alt_comp=0 in meta.
      Re-predict: verify prm_comp=1, prm_ctr=3'b110.
      Cite CTR rule rows 16 or 17. Cite Table 7 row 3.

4. Each task must:
   - Report PASS or FAIL with a descriptive message.
   - Accept verbose as a parameter. When verbose != 0
     print setup and result detail.

5. Add test enable integers and invocations to the
   main initial block following the existing convention.
   New tests run after all 37 existing tests.

6. sim_tage_fast must pass all 39 tests. sim_tage
   must also pass.

7. Update the Makefile only if new flags or
   dependencies are required. Do not remove existing
   flags.

## Constraints

- Lint clean with all existing suppression flags.
- Only modify tb_tage.sv and the Makefile if needed.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Write Results Capture to ./prompts/BP-014e.md.
- Complete the Test Matrix section of Results Capture
  before implementing any test code.

## Deliverables

1. frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv --
   two round-trip tests added, all 39 tests passing
   under sim_tage_fast. All prior tests still pass.
2. frontend/branch_predictor/Makefile -- updated
   only if required.
3. Results Capture filled in below including completed
   Test Matrix section with planning document citations.

Test name: rt_ctr_rows16_17_tst

### Address derivation notes

Formula (folded_hist=0 in all tests):
  idx_hash = (pc >> INST_OFFSET)[10:0] where INST_OFFSET=2
  bank = idx_hash[10]
  row  = idx_hash[9:0]

             THIS_INDEX_BITS=11

T0 uses the same index formula as T1-T4 (pc[12:2]),
so bank and row are identical for T0 and T1 at a given PC.

## What was delivered

1. frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv
   - Header updated for BP-014e.
   - Two new test enable ints: _rt_ctr_rows14_15_tst,
     _rt_ctr_rows16_17_tst.
   - Two new task implementations:
       rt_ctr_rows14_15_tst (TC-38)
       rt_ctr_rows16_17_tst (TC-39)
   - Main block invocations added under BP-014e block.
   - Overall verdict message updated to BP-014e.
   - All 39 tests pass sim_tage_fast and sim_tage.

2. frontend/branch_predictor/Makefile
   - No changes required. All existing flags sufficient.

## Test Case Results

TC-38 (rt_ctr_rows14_15_tst):
  pre-predict: T1[0][480]=001B T0[0][480]=01 T2-4[0][480]=0010
  post-predict meta: prm_comp=1 alt_comp=0 using_prm=1
                     pred_tkn=1 prm_tkn=1 alt_tkn=0 alc_comp=0
  resolved_taken=0 cond_mispredict=1
  post-update: T1[0][480]=0009 T0[0][480]=01
  repred: prm_comp=1 prm_ctr=100

TC-39 (rt_ctr_rows16_17_tst):
  pre-predict: T1[0][576]=011B T0[0][576]=01
  post-predict meta: prm_comp=1 alt_comp=0 using_prm=1
                     pred_tkn=1 prm_tkn=1 alt_tkn=0
  resolved_taken=1 cond_mispredict=0
  post-update: T1[0][576]=012D T0[0][576]=01
  repred: prm_comp=1 prm_ctr=110

Both tests PASS under sim_tage_fast and sim_tage.

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt

1. tage_prm_useful in meta equals u_eff at predict time.
   With tage_enable_aging=0, EPC=0, epoch=0: age=0, so
   u_eff = USEFUL. For T1 USE=01, tage_prm_useful=01.
   DEC 01->00 (TC-38) and INC 01->10 (TC-39) as expected.

2. T2, T3, T4 (FAST_INIT=0) are all zero (VALID=0) at
   bank=0, row=576 since no prior test uses PC=40'h900.
   TC-39 relies on this for T2-T4 not hitting.

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt

1. TC-38 includes alc_comp=0 check in meta1 verification
   to confirm allocation suppression fired as expected.
   Not explicitly required by prompt but consistent with
   existing round-trip tests (e.g., rt_ctr_rows5_6_tst).

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed

None observed in this session. RTL behavior matches
planning documents for rows 14-17 CTR updates and
Table 7 USE updates.

## Deferred Work

None.

## Other Notes

PC 40'h780: tag_hash=0x00. T1 preloaded with TAG=0x00.
Match is valid because computed tag_hash=0x00 equals
stored tag=0x00.

EPC written during USE update is current epoch=0,
same as initial EPC=0 in preload. Entry word bits
[7:6] remain 0b00 after update.

## Task ID
BP-014f

## Context Loaded
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_bim.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_table.sv

@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv

@planning/interfaces/tage_interfaces.md
@planning/interfaces/tage_table_interfaces.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_decisions.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md
@planning/testbenches/tage_tb_decisions.md

## Note
From 2026.0530: It was necessary to restore prompt section content
from the PA session. 

Evidence suggests the IA overwrote the prompt section with this statement:
 [see .claude/tmp/current-prompt.md]

The /run command validates the task file and extracts the prompt from
the task file placing it in that path.  This abberation was not noticed 
at the time. 

## Hypothesis
Two UAON full round-trip tests can be added to tb_tage.sv
without modifying any RTL. All 41 tests pass after
addition.

## Background

Read all context documents completely before writing any
test code. Do not skip this step.

### UAON mechanism summary

Two 4b saturating counters, uaon[0] and uaon[1], one per
prediction slot. Owned by tage_cntrl.

Trigger condition at predict time:
  prm CTR is weak (3'b011 or 3'b100) AND uaon[s] >= TAGE_UAON_THRES

When trigger fires: tage_using_primary=0, alt supplies
prediction. When trigger does not fire: tage_using_primary=1
(subject to prm CTR not being weak, or counter below
threshold).

UAON update rules (from tage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md):
  if pred_strong: no change
  if prm_wrong AND alt_correct: INC uaon[s]
  if prm_correct AND alt_wrong: DEC uaon[s]
  else: no change

pred_strong reflects NOT WEAK on the final provider CTR
after UAON mux selection.

TAGE_UAON_THRES = 8 (from bp_defines_pkg.sv).

### Provider role assignment

The primary provider is the longest-history tagged table
that hits. The alternate provider is the next longest-
history tagged table that hits below the primary. When
both T1 and T2 hit and T3/T4 do not, T2 is primary
(comp=2, 13b history) and T1 is alternate (comp=1, 8b
history).

Both TC-40 and TC-41 use T2 as primary and T1 as
alternate, both hitting. T3/T4 do not hit.

### Test sequence overview

TC-40 and TC-41 use the same PC and RAM entries.
TC-41 begins from the RAM state left by TC-40.
The two tests are sequenced in the main initial block
so TC-40 runs before TC-41.

### TC-40 sequence

Step 1 -- Setup:
  Write uaon[0]=4'h7 via hierarchical reference.
  Pre-load T2 (prm) with weak CTR=3'b100 (boundary).
  Pre-load T1 (alt) with CTR not at boundary.
  Pre-load T0 at test PC (arbitrary, not boundary).
  T3/T4: FAST_INIT zero state (VALID=0, no hits).

Step 2 -- First predict:
  uaon[0]=7 < TAGE_UAON_THRES=8. Trigger condition
  not met (counter below threshold). using_prm=1.
  Capture meta1. Verify using_prm=1.

Step 3 -- First update:
  prm (T2) is wrong: resolved_taken != T2 CTR direction.
  alt (T1) is correct: resolved_taken == T1 CTR direction.
  pred_strong=0 (post-mux CTR is T2 CTR=100, which is
  weak). UAON INC fires: uaon[0] 7->8.
  cond_mispredict=1 (prm wrong).
  Allocation suppressed: pre-load T3/T4 with 16'h0010.

Step 4 -- Re-predict at same PC:
  uaon[0]=8 >= TAGE_UAON_THRES=8. T2 prm CTR still at
  boundary (only DEC fired on T2, moving 100->011, still
  boundary). Trigger condition met. using_prm=0.
  Verify using_prm=0. Verify tage_pred_tkn comes from
  T1 (alt) direction.

### TC-41 sequence

TC-41 begins immediately after TC-40 completes.
RAM state at start of TC-41:
  uaon[0]=8 (set by TC-40 UAON INC).
  T2 CTR=3'b011 (DEC from 100 in TC-40 update).
  T1 CTR unchanged from TC-40 pre-load.

Step 1 -- Second predict (no additional setup):
  uaon[0]=8 >= threshold. T2 CTR=011 is still boundary.
  Trigger fires. using_prm=0. T1 is provider.
  Capture meta2. Verify using_prm=0.

Step 2 -- Second update:
  alt (T1) is wrong: resolved_taken != T1 CTR direction.
  prm (T2) is correct: resolved_taken == T2 CTR direction.
  pred_strong=0 (post-mux CTR is T1 CTR, which must be
  weak or boundary -- choose T1 CTR=3'b100).
  UAON DEC fires: uaon[0] 8->7.
  cond_mispredict=1 (T1 alt is provider and wrong).
  Allocation: prm_comp=2, T3/T4 still have USE=01 from
  TC-40 pre-load, suppressing allocation.

Step 3 -- Re-predict at same PC:
  uaon[0]=7 < threshold. T2 CTR=011 still boundary but
  counter below threshold. Trigger does not fire.
  using_prm=1. Verify using_prm=1.

### CTR value selection

T2 (prm) initial CTR=3'b100:
  - Boundary: triggers UAON condition.
  - CTR[2]=1, predicts taken.
  - After TC-40 update (prm wrong, resolved=0): DEC 100->011.
  - 3'b011 is also boundary: TC-41 UAON still triggers.
  - After TC-41 update (prm correct, resolved=0,
    T2 CTR[2]=0): INC 011->100. Back to initial state
    (optional verify).

T1 (alt) CTR=3'b100:
  - Boundary value: pred_strong=0 for TC-41 post-mux CTR.
  - CTR[2]=1, predicts taken.
  - Wait -- T1 CTR[2]=1 and T2 CTR[2]=1 means pred_diff=0.
  - Need pred_diff for UAON update rules? No -- UAON update
    rules do not require pred_diff. They only require
    prm_wrong/alt_correct or prm_correct/alt_wrong.
  - pred_diff=0 is acceptable for UAON tests. Table 7 row 1
    fires (no USE update). This simplifies the test.

T1 (alt) CTR=3'b100, CTR[2]=1, predicts taken:
  TC-40: resolved_taken=0.
    prm (T2) CTR[2]=1, predicts taken, wrong.
    alt (T1) CTR[2]=1, predicts taken, also wrong.
    Both wrong -> UAON no change. This does not work.

Revise T1 CTR to achieve prm_wrong AND alt_correct
for TC-40, and alt_wrong AND prm_correct for TC-41:

TC-40 requires: resolved_taken=0.
  T2 (prm) CTR[2]=1 (taken), wrong. Good.
  T1 (alt) CTR[2]=0 (not-taken), correct. Good.
  Use T1 CTR=3'b011 (weakest not-taken, boundary).
  pred_strong=0 (post-mux CTR is T2 CTR=100, weak).
  pred_diff=1 (T2 CTR[2]=1 != T1 CTR[2]=0).

TC-41 requires: resolved_taken=0 (T2 CTR[2]=0 after
  DEC to 011, predicts not-taken, correct at resolved=0).
  T1 CTR=3'b011, CTR[2]=0, predicts not-taken, wrong
  at resolved=0. Wait -- resolved=0 means not-taken.
  T1 CTR[2]=0 predicts not-taken. not-taken==not-taken:
  alt_correct. That is wrong for TC-41 which needs
  alt_wrong.

Revise: TC-41 resolved_taken=1.
  T2 CTR=3'b011 after TC-40 DEC. CTR[2]=0, predicts
  not-taken. resolved=1 (taken). T2 wrong? No -- we
  need prm_correct for TC-41 UAON DEC.
  T2 CTR[2]=0 predicts not-taken. resolved=1 means
  prm_correct=(1==0)=0. prm wrong. This does not work.

Correct approach: after TC-40 update T2 CTR=011.
  For TC-41 to have prm_correct: resolved must match
  T2 CTR direction. T2 CTR[2]=0 -> not-taken direction.
  resolved_taken=0 makes prm_correct=1.
  T1 CTR[2]=0 (011) predicts not-taken.
  resolved=0 makes alt_correct=1. Both correct -> no
  UAON change. Does not work.

Root issue: after TC-40, both T1 and T2 predict
not-taken (both CTR[2]=0). Any resolved value makes
both correct or both wrong. Cannot get prm_correct
AND alt_wrong from same-direction CTRs.

Solution: use different T1 CTR value that survives
TC-40 update with CTR[2] differing from T2 post-update.

T1 CTR=3'b010 (wn1, not boundary, CTR[2]=0):
  TC-40: T1 alt correct at resolved=0 (CTR[2]=0,
  not-taken, resolved not-taken). Good.
  TC-40 update: alt_correct, prm_wrong -> UAON INC.
  T1 CTR update: prm_wrong means alt INC? No --
  rows 3/4 (prm wrong, pred_diff=1): prm DEC, alt INC.
  pred_diff=1 (T2 CTR[2]=1 != T1 CTR[2]=0).
  T1 CTR INC: 010->011. T1 CTR[2] still 0.
  TC-41: T2 CTR=011 (CTR[2]=0). T1 CTR=011 (CTR[2]=0).
  Same direction problem remains.

Solution: set T1 CTR=3'b101 (wt1, not boundary,
  CTR[2]=1, taken direction):
  TC-40: resolved_taken=0.
    T2 CTR[2]=1 taken, wrong.
    T1 CTR[2]=1 taken, also wrong.
    Both wrong -> UAON no change. Does not work.

Correct solution requires pred_diff=1 AND prm_wrong
AND alt_correct in TC-40. This means T2 and T1 must
predict opposite directions and resolved must match T1.

T2 CTR=3'b100 (taken, CTR[2]=1).
T1 CTR=3'b010 (not-taken, CTR[2]=0).
resolved_taken=0: T2 wrong, T1 correct. UAON INC. Good.
pred_diff=1. CTR rows 3/4: T2 DEC 100->011, T1 INC
010->011. After TC-40: T2=011, T1=011. Both CTR[2]=0.

For TC-41 need T1 and T2 to predict opposite directions
again. After TC-40 update both are 011. Cannot differ.

Alternative: use pred_diff=0 path for TC-40 UAON update.
UAON update does not require pred_diff. Only needs
prm_wrong and alt_correct (or prm_correct and alt_wrong).
Suppress CTR alt INC by making pred_diff=0 (rows 5/6:
prm DEC only, alt unchanged).

T2 CTR=3'b100 (taken, CTR[2]=1).
T1 CTR=3'b010 (not-taken, CTR[2]=0).
resolved_taken=0: T2 wrong (taken, resolved not-taken),
T1 correct (not-taken, resolved not-taken).
pred_diff=1 (CTR[2] differ). Rows 3/4 fire: T2 DEC,
T1 INC. T1 010->011 after update. T2 100->011.
Same direction problem after update.

Use pred_diff=0 for both tests. Force T1 and T2 to
same CTR direction so alt INC/DEC does not fire and
T1 CTR stays stable across TC-40 update.

T2 CTR=3'b100 (taken, boundary, CTR[2]=1).
T1 CTR=3'b101 (taken, not boundary, CTR[2]=1).
TC-40: resolved_taken=0.
  T2 wrong (taken, resolved not-taken).
  T1 wrong (taken, resolved not-taken).
  Both wrong -> UAON no change. Does not work.

Final solution: separate the UAON update trigger from
the direction. UAON INC requires prm_wrong AND
alt_correct. This fundamentally requires T1 and T2
to predict in opposite directions with resolved
matching T1.

Accept that T1 and T2 will have same CTR[2]=0 after
TC-40 update and handle TC-41 by hierarchically
writing T1 CTR to a taken value before TC-41 predict,
so that T1 and T2 again differ in direction.

TC-40:
  T2 CTR=3'b100, T1 CTR=3'b010.
  resolved_taken=0. UAON INC 7->8.
  After update: T2=011, T1=011 (INC from rows 3/4).

TC-41:
  Write T1 CTR back to a taken-direction value via
  hierarchical reference before predicting.
  T1 new CTR=3'b101 (taken, not boundary, CTR[2]=1).
  T2 CTR=3'b011 (not-taken, boundary, CTR[2]=0).
  Predict: uaon=8>=threshold, T2 prm CTR=011 boundary.
  UAON fires. using_prm=0. T1 (alt) is provider.
  resolved_taken=0.
    T1 CTR[2]=1 taken, wrong (alt wrong).
    T2 CTR[2]=0 not-taken, correct (prm correct).
  pred_strong=0 (post-mux CTR is T1 CTR=101, NOT weak).
  Wait -- 101 is not weak. pred_strong=1. UAON frozen.

T1 CTR must be weak for TC-41 pred_strong=0.
Use T1 CTR=3'b100 (boundary, CTR[2]=1, taken).
  post-mux CTR=100, weak, pred_strong=0. Good.
  T1 CTR[2]=1 taken, resolved=0, alt wrong. Good.
  T2 CTR[2]=0 not-taken, resolved=0, prm correct. Good.
  UAON DEC fires: 8->7. Good.

Summary of final CTR choices:

TC-40 initial state:
  uaon[0]=4'h7 (hierarchical write before predict).
  T2 CTR=3'b100 (prm, boundary, taken).
  T1 CTR=3'b010 (alt, not boundary, not-taken).
  T3/T4: 16'h0010 (alloc suppression).
  resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.
  UAON INC: 7->8.
  T2 DEC: 100->011. T1 INC: 010->011 (rows 3/4).

TC-41 initial state (after TC-40):
  uaon[0]=8 (result of TC-40 INC).
  T2 CTR=3'b011 (result of TC-40 DEC).
  Write T1 CTR=3'b100 via hierarchical reference.
    T1 entry: preserve TAG, EPC, USE from TC-40 result.
    New T1 lower byte: {EPC=00, USE=xx, CTR=100, VALID=1}.
    USE after TC-40: T1 USE was set to initial value.
    Read T1 RAM after TC-40 to get current entry, then
    write with CTR field replaced to 3'b100.
    Simpler: write full T1 entry with known TAG, EPC=0,
    USE=00, CTR=100, VALID=1.
  resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.
  UAON DEC: 8->7.
  T2 INC: 011->100 (rows 9/10: using_prm=0, prm INC
    when pred_diff=1). pred_diff: T2 CTR[2]=0 (011),
    T1 CTR[2]=1 (100). pred_diff=1. Rows 9/10.
  T1 DEC: 100->011 (alt DEC, rows 9/10).
  After TC-41 update: T2=100, T1=011.

Re-predict after TC-41 update:
  uaon[0]=7 < threshold.
  T2 CTR=100, boundary. But uaon=7 < 8.
  Trigger condition: prm_ctr boundary AND uaon>=threshold.
  uaon=7 < 8: trigger does not fire.
  using_prm=1. Mux restored to primary. Verify.

### Address derivation

T1-T4 index (folded_hist=0):
  idx_hash = (pc >> 2)[10:0]
  bank     = idx_hash[10]
  row      = idx_hash[9:0]
  tag_hash = (pc >> 11)[7:0]

T1 RAM hierarchical reference (slot 0,
from tage_tb_decisions.md):
  tb.u_dut.gen_tage_tbl[N].u_tage_table.u_ram_s0
    .mem[bank][row]

Choose PC not used in any prior test. Prior sessions
used (among others):

## Hypothesis
Two UAON full round-trip tests can be added to tb_tage.sv
without modifying any RTL. All 41 tests pass after
addition.

## Background

Read all context documents completely before writing any
test code. Do not skip this step.

### UAON mechanism summary

Two 4b saturating counters, uaon[0] and uaon[1], one per
prediction slot. Owned by tage_cntrl.

Trigger condition at predict time:
  prm CTR is weak (3'b011 or 3'b100) AND uaon[s] >= TAGE_UAON_THRES

When trigger fires: tage_using_primary=0, alt supplies
prediction. When trigger does not fire: tage_using_primary=1
(subject to prm CTR not being weak, or counter below
threshold).

UAON update rules (from tage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md):
  if pred_strong: no change
  if prm_wrong AND alt_correct: INC uaon[s]
  if prm_correct AND alt_wrong: DEC uaon[s]
  else: no change

pred_strong reflects NOT WEAK on the final provider CTR
after UAON mux selection.

TAGE_UAON_THRES = 8 (from bp_defines_pkg.sv).

### Provider role assignment

The primary provider is the longest-history tagged table
that hits. The alternate provider is the next longest-
history tagged table that hits below the primary. When
both T1 and T2 hit and T3/T4 do not, T2 is primary
(comp=2, 13b history) and T1 is alternate (comp=1, 8b
history).

Both TC-40 and TC-41 use T2 as primary and T1 as
alternate, both hitting. T3/T4 do not hit.

### Test sequence overview

TC-40 and TC-41 use the same PC and RAM entries.
TC-41 begins from the RAM state left by TC-40.
The two tests are sequenced in the main initial block
so TC-40 runs before TC-41.

### TC-40 sequence

Step 1 -- Setup:
  Write uaon[0]=4'h7 via hierarchical reference.
  Pre-load T2 (prm) with weak CTR=3'b100 (boundary).
  Pre-load T1 (alt) with CTR not at boundary.
  Pre-load T0 at test PC (arbitrary, not boundary).
  T3/T4: FAST_INIT zero state (VALID=0, no hits).

Step 2 -- First predict:
  uaon[0]=7 < TAGE_UAON_THRES=8. Trigger condition
  not met (counter below threshold). using_prm=1.
  Capture meta1. Verify using_prm=1.

Step 3 -- First update:
  prm (T2) is wrong: resolved_taken != T2 CTR direction.
  alt (T1) is correct: resolved_taken == T1 CTR direction.
  pred_strong=0 (post-mux CTR is T2 CTR=100, which is
  weak). UAON INC fires: uaon[0] 7->8.
  cond_mispredict=1 (prm wrong).
  Allocation suppressed: pre-load T3/T4 with 16'h0010.

Step 4 -- Re-predict at same PC:
  uaon[0]=8 >= TAGE_UAON_THRES=8. T2 prm CTR still at
  boundary (only DEC fired on T2, moving 100->011, still
  boundary). Trigger condition met. using_prm=0.
  Verify using_prm=0. Verify tage_pred_tkn comes from
  T1 (alt) direction.

### TC-41 sequence

TC-41 begins immediately after TC-40 completes.
RAM state at start of TC-41:
  uaon[0]=8 (set by TC-40 UAON INC).
  T2 CTR=3'b011 (DEC from 100 in TC-40 update).
  T1 CTR unchanged from TC-40 pre-load.

Step 1 -- Second predict (no additional setup):
  uaon[0]=8 >= threshold. T2 CTR=011 is still boundary.
  Trigger fires. using_prm=0. T1 is provider.
  Capture meta2. Verify using_prm=0.

Step 2 -- Second update:
  alt (T1) is wrong: resolved_taken != T1 CTR direction.
  prm (T2) is correct: resolved_taken == T2 CTR direction.
  pred_strong=0 (post-mux CTR is T1 CTR, which must be
  weak or boundary -- choose T1 CTR=3'b100).
  UAON DEC fires: uaon[0] 8->7.
  cond_mispredict=1 (T1 alt is provider and wrong).
  Allocation: prm_comp=2, T3/T4 still have USE=01 from
  TC-40 pre-load, suppressing allocation.

Step 3 -- Re-predict at same PC:
  uaon[0]=7 < threshold. T2 CTR=011 still boundary but
  counter below threshold. Trigger does not fire.
  using_prm=1. Verify using_prm=1.

### CTR value selection

T2 (prm) initial CTR=3'b100:
  - Boundary: triggers UAON condition.
  - CTR[2]=1, predicts taken.
  - After TC-40 update (prm wrong, resolved=0): DEC 100->011.
  - 3'b011 is also boundary: TC-41 UAON still triggers.
  - After TC-41 update (prm correct, resolved=0,
    T2 CTR[2]=0): INC 011->100. Back to initial state
    (optional verify).

T1 (alt) CTR=3'b100:
  - Boundary value: pred_strong=0 for TC-41 post-mux CTR.
  - CTR[2]=1, predicts taken.
  - Wait -- T1 CTR[2]=1 and T2 CTR[2]=1 means pred_diff=0.
  - Need pred_diff for UAON update rules? No -- UAON update
    rules do not require pred_diff. They only require
    prm_wrong/alt_correct or prm_correct/alt_wrong.
  - pred_diff=0 is acceptable for UAON tests. Table 7 row 1
    fires (no USE update). This simplifies the test.

T1 (alt) CTR=3'b100, CTR[2]=1, predicts taken:
  TC-40: resolved_taken=0.
    prm (T2) CTR[2]=1, predicts taken, wrong.
    alt (T1) CTR[2]=1, predicts taken, also wrong.
    Both wrong -> UAON no change. This does not work.

Revise T1 CTR to achieve prm_wrong AND alt_correct
for TC-40, and alt_wrong AND prm_correct for TC-41:

TC-40 requires: resolved_taken=0.
  T2 (prm) CTR[2]=1 (taken), wrong. Good.
  T1 (alt) CTR[2]=0 (not-taken), correct. Good.
  Use T1 CTR=3'b011 (weakest not-taken, boundary).
  pred_strong=0 (post-mux CTR is T2 CTR=100, weak).
  pred_diff=1 (T2 CTR[2]=1 != T1 CTR[2]=0).

TC-41 requires: resolved_taken=0 (T2 CTR[2]=0 after
  DEC to 011, predicts not-taken, correct at resolved=0).
  T1 CTR=3'b011, CTR[2]=0, predicts not-taken, wrong
  at resolved=0. Wait -- resolved=0 means not-taken.
  T1 CTR[2]=0 predicts not-taken. not-taken==not-taken:
  alt_correct. That is wrong for TC-41 which needs
  alt_wrong.

Revise: TC-41 resolved_taken=1.
  T2 CTR=3'b011 after TC-40 DEC. CTR[2]=0, predicts
  not-taken. resolved=1 (taken). T2 wrong? No -- we
  need prm_correct for TC-41 UAON DEC.
  T2 CTR[2]=0 predicts not-taken. resolved=1 means
  prm_correct=(1==0)=0. prm wrong. This does not work.

Correct approach: after TC-40 update T2 CTR=011.
  For TC-41 to have prm_correct: resolved must match
  T2 CTR direction. T2 CTR[2]=0 -> not-taken direction.
  resolved_taken=0 makes prm_correct=1.
  T1 CTR[2]=0 (011) predicts not-taken.
  resolved=0 makes alt_correct=1. Both correct -> no
  UAON change. Does not work.

Root issue: after TC-40, both T1 and T2 predict
not-taken (both CTR[2]=0). Any resolved value makes
both correct or both wrong. Cannot get prm_correct
AND alt_wrong from same-direction CTRs.

Solution: use different T1 CTR value that survives
TC-40 update with CTR[2] differing from T2 post-update.

T1 CTR=3'b010 (wn1, not boundary, CTR[2]=0):

  Same direction problem remains.

Solution: set T1 CTR=3'b101 (wt1, not boundary,

    T2 CTR[2]=1 taken, wrong.
    T1 CTR[2]=1 taken, also wrong.
    Both wrong -> UAON no change. Does not work.

Correct solution requires pred_diff=1 AND prm_wrong
AND alt_correct in TC-40. This means T2 and T1 must
predict opposite directions and resolved must match T1.

T2 CTR=3'b100 (taken, CTR[2]=1).
T1 CTR=3'b010 (not-taken, CTR[2]=0).
resolved_taken=0: T2 wrong, T1 correct. UAON INC. Good.
pred_diff=1. CTR rows 3/4: T2 DEC 100->011, T1 INC
010->011. After TC-40: T2=011, T1=011. Both CTR[2]=0.

For TC-41 need T1 and T2 to predict opposite directions
again. After TC-40 update both are 011. Cannot differ.

Alternative: use pred_diff=0 path for TC-40 UAON update.
UAON update does not require pred_diff. Only needs
prm_wrong and alt_correct (or prm_correct and alt_wrong).
Suppress CTR alt INC by making pred_diff=0 (rows 5/6:
prm DEC only, alt unchanged).

T2 CTR=3'b100 (taken, CTR[2]=1).
T1 CTR=3'b010 (not-taken, CTR[2]=0).
resolved_taken=0: T2 wrong (taken, resolved not-taken),
T1 correct (not-taken, resolved not-taken).
pred_diff=1 (CTR[2] differ). Rows 3/4 fire: T2 DEC,
T1 INC. T1 010->011 after update. T2 100->011.
Same direction problem after update.

Use pred_diff=0 for both tests. Force T1 and T2 to
same CTR direction so alt INC/DEC does not fire and
T1 CTR stays stable across TC-40 update.

T2 CTR=3'b100 (taken, boundary, CTR[2]=1).
T1 CTR=3'b101 (taken, not boundary, CTR[2]=1).
TC-40: resolved_taken=0.
  T2 wrong (taken, resolved not-taken).
  T1 wrong (taken, resolved not-taken).
  Both wrong -> UAON no change. Does not work.

Final solution: separate the UAON update trigger from
the direction. UAON INC requires prm_wrong AND
alt_correct. This fundamentally requires T1 and T2
to predict in opposite directions with resolved
matching T1.

Accept that T1 and T2 will have same CTR[2]=0 after
TC-40 update and handle TC-41 by hierarchically
writing T1 CTR to a taken value before TC-41 predict,
so that T1 and T2 again differ in direction.

TC-40:

## Summary

Two UAON round-trip tests added to tb_tage.sv:
  TC-40: uaon_threshold_cross_tst -- UAON INC 7->8 via
    prm_wrong+alt_correct update. Re-predict confirms
    using_primary flips to 0.
  TC-41: uaon_dec_restore_tst -- UAON DEC 8->7 via
    prm_correct+alt_wrong update. Re-predict confirms
    using_primary restores to 1.
All 41 tests pass under sim_tage_fast and sim_tage.
No RTL files modified. No Makefile changes required.

## Test Matrix

### TC-40: uaon_threshold_cross_tst

Test name: uaon_threshold_cross_tst
TC number: TC-40

UAON update rule exercised:
  INC rule -- tage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md
  Condition: !pred_strong AND prm_wrong AND alt_correct:
    INC uaon[s] (saturate at 4'hF).

CTR update rule rows exercised:
  Row 3 -- tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md
  Condition: using_prm=1, pred_tkn=1, resolved_taken=0,
             pred_diff=1, prm>0, alt>0: DEC prm, INC alt.

Table 7 row exercised:
  Row 4 -- tage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md Table 7
  Condition: pred_diff=1, TTM=0, using_prm=1,
             mispredict=1: Dec prm u_eff.
  T2 USE=00 -> saturates at 00 (no observable change).

PC chosen: 40'hB00

Address derivation (folded_hist=0):
  idx_hash = (pc >> 2)[10:0] = 0x2C0 = 704
  bank     = idx_hash[10] = 0 (704 < 1024)
  row      = idx_hash[9:0] = 704
  tag_hash = (pc >> THIS_INDEX_BITS)[7:0]
           = (0xB00 >> 11)[7:0] = 0x01
  Hierarchical prefix: u_dut.gen_tage_tbl[N].u_tage_tbl
                        .u_ram_s0.mem[0][704]

Initial RAM pre-loads:
  T2[0][704] = 16'h0109
    TAG=0x01, EPC=00, USE=00, CTR=3b100, VALID=1
    CTR=100 is boundary (taken direction): UAON trigger
    fires on predict.
  T1[0][704] = 16'h0105
    TAG=0x01, EPC=00, USE=00, CTR=3b010, VALID=1
    CTR=010 is not boundary, not-taken direction.
  T3[0][704] = 16'h0010
    TAG=0x00, USE=01, VALID=0: alloc suppression.
  T4[0][704] = 16'h0010
    TAG=0x00, USE=01, VALID=0: alloc suppression.

uaon[0] initial write value: 4'h7 (below threshold=8).
  Written via u_dut.u_tage_cntrl.uaon[0].

Expected meta fields after first predict:
  tage_prm_comp       = 3'd2 (T2 is longest tagged hit)
  tage_alt_comp       = 3'd1 (T1 is next longest hit)
  tage_using_primary  = 1 (uaon=7 < TAGE_UAON_THRES=8)
  tage_pred_tkn       = 1 (T2 CTR[2]=1, taken)
  tage_prm_tkn        = 1 (T2 CTR[2]=1)
  tage_alt_tkn        = 0 (T1 CTR[2]=0)
  pred_diff           = 1 (prm_tkn=1 != alt_tkn=0)
  tage_pred_strong    = 0 (post_mux_ctr=T2 CTR=100,
                          100 is boundary, NOT strong)

resolved_taken = 0, cond_mispredict = 1
  prm(T2) predicted taken=1, resolved not-taken=0: wrong.
  alt(T1) predicted not-taken=0, resolved=0: correct.
  pred_strong=0: UAON INC condition met.

Expected UAON counter after update: 8

Expected T1 and T2 RAM after update:
  T2: CTR DEC 100->011. USE DEC sat 00->00.
    T2[0][704] = 16'h0107
    {TAG=0x01, EPC=00, USE=00, CTR=011, VALID=1}
  T1: CTR INC 010->011 (CTR row 3 alt INC).
    T1[0][704] = 16'h0107
    {TAG=0x01, EPC=00, USE=00, CTR=011, VALID=1}
  Note: T1 USE not written (t_use_wr_u0[1][0]=0 since
    u_use_comp=T2 in Table 7 row 4, HAND-FIX-001 only
    fires for the USE target table).
  T3/T4: unchanged 16'h0010.

Re-predict expected:
  tage_using_primary = 0
    uaon[0]=8 >= TAGE_UAON_THRES=8.
    T2 prm CTR=011: uaon_trig fires.
    uaon[0][3]=1 (8 >= 8): UAON mux fires -> using_prm=0.
  tage_pred_tkn = 0 (alt T1 CTR=011, CTR[2]=0, not-taken)
  tage_prm_comp = 2

Pass/fail criterion:
  First predict: tage_using_primary=1, tage_prm_comp=2.
  Post-update: T2[0][704]=16'h0107, T1[0][704]=16'h0107,
    uaon[0]=8.
  Re-predict: tage_using_primary=0, tage_prm_comp=2.

---

### TC-41: uaon_dec_restore_tst

Test name: uaon_dec_restore_tst
TC number: TC-41

UAON update rule exercised:
  DEC rule -- tage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md
  Condition: !pred_strong AND prm_correct AND alt_wrong:
    DEC uaon[s] (saturate at 4'h0).

CTR update rule rows exercised:
  Row 9 -- tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md
  Condition: using_prm=0, pred_tkn=1, resolved_taken=0,
             pred_diff=1, prm>0, alt>0: INC prm, DEC alt.

Table 7 row exercised:
  Row 6 -- tage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md Table 7
  Condition: pred_diff=1, TTM=0, using_prm=0,
             mispredict=1: Dec alt u_eff.
  T1 USE=00 -> saturates at 00 (no observable change).

PC chosen: 40'hB00 (same as TC-40, continues TC-40 state)

RAM state at start of TC-41:
  uaon[0] = 8 (set by TC-40 INC)
  T2[0][704] = 16'h0107 (CTR=011, from TC-40 DEC)
  T1[0][704] written to 16'h0109 before predict:
    TAG=0x01, EPC=00, USE=00, CTR=3b100, VALID=1
    CTR=100 is boundary (taken direction).
    Written via hierarchical reference to override TC-40
    result (T1 CTR=011 from TC-40 INC).
  T3/T4: still 16'h0010 from TC-40 pre-load.

uaon[0] initial write value: 8 (from TC-40, no new write).

Expected meta fields after second predict:
  tage_prm_comp       = 3'd2 (T2 CTR=011)
  tage_alt_comp       = 3'd1 (T1 CTR=100)
  tage_using_primary  = 0
    uaon[0]=8 >= threshold. T2 prm CTR=011 is boundary.
    UAON trigger fires. uaon[0][3]=1. using_prm=0.
  tage_pred_tkn       = 1 (alt T1 CTR[2]=1, taken)
  tage_prm_tkn        = 0 (T2 CTR[2]=0, not-taken)
  tage_alt_tkn        = 1 (T1 CTR[2]=1)
  pred_diff           = 1 (prm_tkn=0 != alt_tkn=1)
  tage_pred_strong    = 0 (post_mux_ctr=T1 CTR=100,
                          100 is boundary, NOT strong)

resolved_taken = 0, cond_mispredict = 1
  alt(T1) predicted taken=1, resolved not-taken=0: wrong.
  prm(T2) predicted not-taken=0, resolved=0: correct.
  pred_strong=0: UAON DEC condition met.

Expected UAON counter after update: 7

Expected T1 and T2 RAM after update:
  T2: CTR INC 011->100 (CTR row 9 prm INC). USE unchanged.
    T2[0][704] = 16'h0109
    {TAG=0x01, EPC=00, USE=00, CTR=100, VALID=1}
  T1: CTR DEC 100->011 (CTR row 9 alt DEC).
    USE DEC sat 00->00 (Table 7 row 6, HAND-FIX-001).
    T1[0][704] = 16'h0107
    {TAG=0x01, EPC=00, USE=00, CTR=011, VALID=1}
  T3/T4: unchanged 16'h0010.

Re-predict expected:
  tage_using_primary = 1
    uaon[0]=7 < TAGE_UAON_THRES=8. Even though T2
    prm CTR=100 is still boundary, counter below
    threshold: UAON mux does not fire. using_prm=1.
  tage_pred_tkn = 1 (T2 CTR=100, CTR[2]=1, taken)
  tage_prm_comp = 2

Pass/fail criterion:
  Second predict: tage_using_primary=0, tage_prm_comp=2.
  Post-update: T2[0][704]=16'h0109, T1[0][704]=16'h0107,
    uaon[0]=7.
  Re-predict: tage_using_primary=1, tage_prm_comp=2.

---

### Address derivation notes

Formula (folded_hist=0 in all tests):
  idx_hash = (pc >> INST_OFFSET)[10:0] where INST_OFFSET=2
  bank = idx_hash[10]
  row  = idx_hash[9:0]
  tag_hash = (pc >> THIS_INDEX_BITS)[7:0] where
             THIS_INDEX_BITS=11

Tag formula verified from tage_table.sv lines 178-182:
  tag_hash = THIS_TAG_BITS'(
    (pc >> THIS_INDEX_BITS) ^ fh1_ext ^ (fh2_ext<<1))
  With fh=0: tag = (pc >> 11)[7:0]

T0 uses the same index formula as T1-T4 (pc[12:2]),
so bank and row are identical for T0 and T1 at a given PC.

## Assumptions

None. All values derived from RTL analysis of
tage_table.sv, tage_cntrl.sv, and bp_defines_pkg.sv.

## RVA23 Compliance Risks

None identified in this session. No RTL changes made.
The UAON mechanism follows the planning document spec
exactly as implemented in tage_cntrl.sv.

## RTL Behavior vs Planning Document Notes

The UAON update in tage_cntrl.sv does NOT require
pred_diff to be set. The planning document confirms this.
The test uses pred_diff=1 (T2 and T1 predict opposite
directions) but UAON update would also fire for pred_diff=0
scenarios (tested indirectly).

HAND-FIX-001 (use_we_s0 = val & use_wr_u0 & prm_alt_match)
is gated by t_use_wr_u0 which is already table-specific in
tage_cntrl.sv (only asserted for the USE target table).
USE write fires only for the target table, not for both
prm and alt simultaneously. Verified from tage_cntrl.sv
lines 870-872.

sessions.overview:
Add two allocation round-trip tests to tb_tage.sv.
TC-42 verifies that when T0 is the provider and
mispredicts, allocation fires into T1 with correct
entry contents (CTR=100, USE=00, EPC=0, TAG=alloc_tag,
VALID=1). TC-43 verifies that when T1 is the provider
and mispredicts, allocation fires into T2 (shortest
qualifying) and T3 (consecutive to T2, also u_eff=0)
is not written. All 41 prior tests must pass unchanged.
All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.
sessions.ia_output:
BP-014g complete. All 43 tests pass under both sim_tage_fast and sim_tage.
                                                                                
  What was done:
                                                                                
  - Added alloc_t0_provider_tst (TC-42): T0 sole provider, mispredict allocates 
  into T1. Verified T1 entry = 0x0109, T0 CTR 10→01, T2-T4 unchanged.           
  - Added alloc_no_consecutive_tst (TC-43): T1 provider, mispredict allocates   
  into T2. Verified T2 entry = 0x0209, T1 CTR 101→100, T3 = 0x0000 (consecutive 
  guard), T4 = 0x0000.
                                                                                
  One non-obvious issue resolved: PC 40'h1100 aliases bank=1, row=64 with PC    
  40'h09100 from BP-014a. That prior test left T2[1][64]=0x121d with USE=1 →
  u_eff=1, which caused the RTL alloc scan to skip T2 and select T3. Fix:       
  explicitly zero T2, T3, T4 at [1][64] before TC-43. This is defensive pre-load
   practice, documented in Results Capture as a decision.

  Makefile: no changes needed.
sessions.my_assessment:
No issues with this pass.

Note: context is driving towards compaction. I suspect the issue is 
the volume of context info necessary to perform these late stage tasks.
sessions.pa_assessment:
43/43 passing. Clean results. Both allocation behaviors verified correctly — T1 allocated on T0 misprediction, T2 allocated and T3 left untouched on T1 misprediction.
Address aliasing observation
The PC=40'h1100 aliasing with BP-014a at bank=1 row=64 is worth noting as a general hygiene rule for future prompts. The prior test PC list in the Background section prevents PC reuse but does not prevent index aliasing — two different PCs can map to the same bank/row. Claude Code caught it at runtime and applied defensive zeroing. Going forward the prompt should note that explicit pre-loads or zeroing of T1-T4 at the test address is always required when cond_mispredict=1 and allocation behavior is under test, regardless of FAST_INIT state.

  u_eff=1, which caused the RTL alloc scan to skip T2 and select T3. Fix:

## My Assessment

## Claude.ai Assessment

Address aliasing observation
The PC=40'h1100 aliasing with BP-014a at bank=1 row=64 is worth noting as a general hygiene rule for future prompts. sessions.prompt_raw:
## Task ID
BP-014g

## Context Loaded
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_bim.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_table.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/tage_cntrl.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv
@frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv
@planning/interfaces/tage_interfaces.md
@planning/interfaces/tage_table_interfaces.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_decisions.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md
@planning/arch/tage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md
@planning/testbenches/tage_tb_decisions.md

## Hypothesis
Two allocation round-trip tests can be added to
tb_tage.sv without modifying any RTL. All 43 tests
pass after addition.

## Background

Read all context documents completely before writing
any test code. Do not skip this step.

### Allocation rules summary

From tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md:

Allocation fires at update time when:
  cond_mispredict == 1
  AND tage_prm_comp < M  (M = index of T4 = 4)

Candidate selection at predict time:
  Scan T(provider+1) through T4 for u_eff == 0.
  Select the shortest-history qualifying table.
  One entry allocated per misprediction.
  tage_alloc_comp == 0 indicates no candidate found.

No-consecutive-table constraint:
  Once Tj is selected as allocation target, Tj+1
  is not allocated even if u_eff == 0. Since only
  one entry is ever allocated per misprediction,
  this constraint guards against spurious writes
  to the consecutive table.

Allocated entry initialization:
  CTR   = 3'b100  (weakest taken)
  USE   = 2'b00
  EPC   = current lcl_epoch (0 when aging disabled)
  TAG   = tage_alloc_tag (captured at predict time)
  VALID = 1'b1

tage_alloc_idx is pre-hashed at predict time and
used directly as the RAM address at update time.
No rehashing at update.

### Provider role assignment

The primary provider is the longest-history tagged
table that hits. When no tagged table hits, T0 is
the provider (prm_comp=0).

### u_eff computation

With aging disabled (tage_enable_aging=0):
  lcl_epoch=0, EPC=0, age=0, u_eff=USEFUL.
  u_eff == 0 when USEFUL == 0.
  Under FAST_INIT all entries have USEFUL=0,
  so all T1-T4 entries are allocation candidates
  unless pre-loaded with USE!=0.

### TC-42 setup: T0 provider, allocate into T1

T0 is provider: no tagged table must hit.
Under FAST_INIT all T1-T4 entries have VALID=0,
so no tag hits occur without explicit pre-load.
Do not pre-load any T1-T4 entry at the test PC.

T0 mispredicts: resolved_taken != T0 CTR direction.
T0 CTR=2'b10 (weakly taken, CTR[1]=1, predicts taken).
resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.

Allocation candidate: T1 is T(prm+1) = T(0+1) = T1.
T1 USEFUL=0 under FAST_INIT. u_eff=0. T1 selected.
tage_alloc_comp=1, tage_alloc_idx=idx_hash for T1.
tage_alloc_tag=tag_hash for T1 at test PC.

After update T1 entry at alloc_idx must contain:
  CTR=3'b100, USE=2'b00, EPC=2'b00,
  TAG=tag_hash, VALID=1'b1

T0 CTR update: T0 provider, mispredicts.
Row 13c (pred_tkn=1, resolved=0): T0 CTR DEC.
Note: debt #34 documents that T0 CTR update in RTL
keys on pred_crt not resolved_taken. pred_crt=0
(wrong prediction) -> subtract 1 -> 2'b10 -> 2'b01.
Expected T0 CTR after update = 2'b01.

### TC-43 setup: T1 provider, allocate into T2,
###             T3 not written (consecutive guard)

T1 is provider: pre-load T1 with a valid hitting
entry. T2-T4 must not hit (VALID=0 from FAST_INIT).

T1 CTR: choose away from boundary to keep using_prm=1
without UAON setup. Use CTR=3'b101 (wt1, CTR[2]=1,
predicts taken, not boundary).

T1 mispredicts: resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.
prm_comp=1 < M=4: allocation fires.

Candidate scan: T(1+1)=T2 through T4.
T2 USEFUL=0 under FAST_INIT. u_eff=0. T2 selected.
tage_alloc_comp=2.

No-consecutive guard: T3 is consecutive to T2.
T3 must not be written even though T3 USEFUL=0.
Verify T3 entry unchanged after update.

T4: not reached (selection stops at T2).
Verify T4 entry unchanged after update.

After update T2 entry at alloc_idx must contain:
  CTR=3'b100, USE=2'b00, EPC=2'b00,
  TAG=tag_hash, VALID=1'b1

T1 CTR update: prm_comp=1, prm wrong.
alt_comp=0 (T0 fallback). rows 14/15: T1 CTR DEC.
T1 CTR 101->100.
T1 USE: pred_diff = T1 CTR[2] != T0 CTR[1].
T0 CTR under FAST_INIT = 2'b10 (weakest taken).
T0 CTR[1]=1. T1 CTR[2]=1. pred_diff=0.
Table 7 row 1 (pred_diff=0): no USE update.

### Address derivation

T1-T4 index (folded_hist=0):
  idx_hash = (pc >> 2)[10:0]
  bank     = idx_hash[10]
  row      = idx_hash[9:0]
  tag_hash = (pc >> 11)[7:0]

T0 index:
  index = pc[12:2]
  bank  = index[10]
  row   = index[9:0]

BIM RAM hierarchical reference for slot 0
(from tage_tb_decisions.md):
  tb.u_dut.u_tage_bim.u_ram_s0.mem[bank][row]

T1-T4 RAM hierarchical reference (slot 0,
from tage_tb_decisions.md):
  tb.u_dut.gen_tage_tbl[N].u_tage_table.u_ram_s0
    .mem[bank][row]

Choose PCs not used in any prior test. Prior sessions
used (among others):
  BP-014a: 40'h09100, 40'h0B800, 40'h0C000, 40'h12400
  BP-014b: 40'h14600, 40'h1F400
  BP-014c: 40'h190,   40'h320
  BP-014d: 40'h480,   40'h600
  BP-014e: 40'h780,   40'h900
  BP-014f: 40'hB00
Do not reuse these PCs.

### Allocated entry word construction

From tage_table_interfaces.md entry format:
  [15:8] TAG  [7:6] EPC  [5:4] USE  [3:1] CTR  [0] VALID

Allocated entry:
  CTR=3'b100, USE=2'b00, EPC=2'b00, VALID=1'b1
  lower byte = {EPC=00, USE=00, CTR=100, VALID=1}
             = 8'h09
  Full word  = {tag_hash, 8'h09}

## Binding Previous Decisions

- All 41 prior tests must pass unchanged.
- All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.
- tage_rdy_tst() runs first.
- Test structure convention:
    int verbose = 1;
    int _testname = 1;
    if (_testname != 0) testname(ARGS, verbose);
  Add one int _testname per new test alongside
  existing declarations.
- Use if (int_var != 0) not if (int_var) throughout.
- Staging always_ff mandatory. Extend existing blocks.
  Do not create additional blocks.
- Hierarchical references for RAM pre-load, readback
  accepted per tage_tb_decisions.md.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Planning documents are authoritative. If RTL behavior
  differs from planning documents, report in Results
  Capture under RVA23 compliance risks. Do not modify
  RTL in this session.
- Expected values must be derived from RTL behavior
  where planning documents and RTL differ.

## Specific Requirements

1. Read all context documents in full before writing
   any test code. Do not skip this step.

2. Derive the test matrix. Record in Results Capture
   before writing any test code. Include for each test:
   - Test name and TC number
   - PC chosen, idx_hash, tag_hash, bank, row
   - Provider identity and prm_comp value
   - Initial RAM pre-loads (all tables at test PC)
   - Expected meta fields after predict:
       tage_prm_comp, tage_alt_comp,
       tage_alloc_comp, tage_alloc_idx, tage_alloc_tag
   - resolved_taken and cond_mispredict values
   - Expected allocated entry word and location
   - Expected provider CTR after update
   - Expected unchanged entries (negative checks)
   - Pass/fail criterion

3. Implement exactly two round-trip tasks, both slot 0:

   a. alloc_t0_provider_tst (TC-42)
      T0 sole provider. prm_comp=0.
      T0 CTR=2'b10. resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.
      Verify: T1 entry at alloc_idx contains allocated
        entry word {tag_hash, 8'h09} after update.
      Verify: T0 CTR decremented (RTL behavior per
        debt #34: pred_crt=0 -> subtract 1 -> 2'b01).
      Verify: T2, T3, T4 entries at test PC unchanged
        (all still zero from FAST_INIT).
      Cite alloc rules from tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md.

   b. alloc_no_consecutive_tst (TC-43)
      T1 provider. prm_comp=1. T1 CTR=3'b101.
      resolved_taken=0. cond_mispredict=1.
      Verify: T2 entry at alloc_idx contains allocated
        entry word {tag_hash, 8'h09} after update.
      Verify: T3 entry at test PC unchanged (zero from
        FAST_INIT) -- consecutive guard confirmed.
      Verify: T4 entry at test PC unchanged (zero from
        FAST_INIT).
      Verify: T1 CTR decremented (101->100).
      Cite alloc rules from tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md.

4. Each task must:
   - Report PASS or FAIL with a descriptive message.
   - Accept verbose as a parameter. When verbose != 0
     print setup and result detail.

5. Add test enable integers and invocations to the
   main initial block following the existing convention.
   New tests run after all 41 existing tests.

6. sim_tage_fast must pass all 43 tests. sim_tage
   must also pass.

7. Update the Makefile only if new flags or
   dependencies are required. Do not remove existing
   flags.

## Constraints

- Lint clean with all existing suppression flags.
- Only modify tb_tage.sv and the Makefile if needed.
- Do not modify any RTL file or package file.
- Write Results Capture to ./prompts/BP-014g.md.
- Complete the Test Matrix section of Results Capture
  before implementing any test code.

## Deliverables

1. frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv --
   two allocation round-trip tests added, all 43
   tests passing under sim_tage_fast. All prior
   tests still pass.
2. frontend/branch_predictor/Makefile -- updated
   only if required.

sessions.results_raw:
## Summary
Two allocation round-trip tests added to tb_tage.sv.
TC-42 (alloc_t0_provider_tst): T0 sole provider,
mispredict fires allocation into T1, T0 CTR decremented.
TC-43 (alloc_no_consecutive_tst): T1 provider, mispredict
fires allocation into T2, T3 unchanged (consecutive guard),
T4 unchanged (not reached). All 43 tests pass under both
sim_tage_fast and sim_tage. Hypothesis confirmed.

## Test Matrix

### TC-42: alloc_t0_provider_tst

Test name: alloc_t0_provider_tst
TC number: TC-42
PC: 40'hD00

Address derivation (folded_hist=0):
  idx_hash = (pc >> 2)[10:0] = (3328>>2)&0x7FF = 832 = 0x340
  bank     = idx_hash[10] = 0  (832 < 1024)
  row      = idx_hash[9:0] = 832
  tag_hash = (pc >> 11)[7:0] = (3328>>11) = 1 = 0x01
  T0 index: pc[12:2] = 832 (same formula, no hash)

Provider: T0 (no tagged table hits). prm_comp=0.

Initial RAM pre-loads:
  T0 u_ram_s0.mem[0][832] = 2'b10 (weakly taken, CTR[1]=1)
  T1-T4: no pre-load. FAST_INIT=0. VALID=0 USE=0 u_eff=0.
  PC=40'hD00 is fresh; no prior test aliases bank=0 row=832.

Expected meta fields after predict:
  tage_prm_comp = 0 (T0 provider, no tagged hit)
  tage_alt_comp = 0 (T0 fallback, no tagged hit)
  tage_alc_comp = 1 (T1 first candidate, T(0+1))
  tage_alc_idx  = 832 = 0x340 (pre-hashed at predict time)
  tage_alc_tag  = 0x01
  tage_pred_tkn = 1 (T0 CTR[1]=1, taken)
  tage_using_primary = 1

resolved_taken = 0, cond_mispredict = 1.

Expected allocated entry: T1 u_ram_s0.mem[0][832] = 0x0109
  {TAG=0x01, EPC=00, USE=00, CTR=100, VALID=1}
  From alloc rules (tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md):
  CTR=100, USE=00, EPC=lcl_epoch=0, TAG=alc_tag=0x01, VALID=1
  lower byte = {00, 00, 100, 1} = 8'h09
  Full word  = {0x01, 0x09} = 16'h0109

Expected provider CTR after update:
  T0 CTR 2'b10 -> 2'b01 (DEC, row 13c, RTL debt #34)
  RTL keys on pred_crt not resolved_taken.
  pred_crt=0 -> subtract 1 -> 2'b10 -> 2'b01.

Expected unchanged entries (negative checks):
  T2 u_ram_s0.mem[0][832] = 0x0000 (allocation stops at T1)
  T3 u_ram_s0.mem[0][832] = 0x0000
  T4 u_ram_s0.mem[0][832] = 0x0000

Re-predict: T1 now valid, tag=0x01 hits.
  prm_comp=1, prm_ctr=3'b100.

Pass criterion:
  alc_comp=1, alc_tag=0x01, pred_tkn=1
  T1[0][832]=0x0109, T0[0][832]=2'b01
  T2-T4[0][832]=0x0000
  repred prm_comp=1, prm_ctr=3'b100

Citation: tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md -- trigger, candidate
selection, no-candidate sentinel, alloc initialization.

---

### TC-43: alloc_no_consecutive_tst

Test name: alloc_no_consecutive_tst
TC number: TC-43
PC: 40'h1100

Address derivation (folded_hist=0):
  idx_hash = (pc >> 2)[10:0] = (4352>>2)&0x7FF = 1088 = 0x440
  bank     = idx_hash[10] = 1  (1088 = 1024+64, bit[10]=1)
  row      = idx_hash[9:0] = 64
  tag_hash = (pc >> 11)[7:0] = (4352>>11) = 2 = 0x02

Provider: T1 (pre-loaded hitting entry). prm_comp=1.

Initial RAM pre-loads:
  T1 u_ram_s0.mem[1][64] = 16'h020B
    {TAG=0x02, EPC=00, USE=00, CTR=101, VALID=1}
    TAG=0x02 matches tag_hash -> T1 hits.
  T0 u_ram_s0.mem[1][64] = 2'b10 (CTR[1]=1)
    T1 CTR[2]=1 == T0 CTR[1]=1 -> pred_diff=0
    Table 7 row 1 (pred_diff=0): no USE update.
  T2 u_ram_s0.mem[1][64] = 16'h0000 (explicit reset)
    bank=1 row=64 aliased by prior tests (PC=40'h09100).
    Reset ensures USE=0 u_eff=0 -> T2 selected as candidate.
  T3 u_ram_s0.mem[1][64] = 16'h0000 (explicit reset)
    Ensures clean baseline for consecutive guard check.
  T4 u_ram_s0.mem[1][64] = 16'h0000 (explicit reset)
    Ensures clean baseline for not-reached check.

Expected meta fields after predict:
  tage_prm_comp = 1 (T1 provider)
  tage_alt_comp = 0 (T0 fallback)
  tage_alc_comp = 2 (T2 first candidate, T(1+1))
  tage_alc_idx  = 1088 = 0x440 (pre-hashed at predict time)
  tage_alc_tag  = 0x02
  tage_pred_tkn = 1 (T1 CTR[2]=1, taken)
  tage_using_primary = 1

resolved_taken = 0, cond_mispredict = 1.

Expected allocated entry: T2 u_ram_s0.mem[1][64] = 0x0209
  {TAG=0x02, EPC=00, USE=00, CTR=100, VALID=1}
  From alloc rules (tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md):
  CTR=100, USE=00, EPC=0, TAG=alc_tag=0x02, VALID=1
  lower byte = 8'h09
  Full word  = {0x02, 0x09} = 16'h0209

Expected provider CTR after update:
  T1 CTR 3'b101 -> 3'b100 (DEC, CTR rows 14/15)
  Row 14: prm_comp>0, alt_comp=0, using_prm=1,
          pred_tkn=1, resolved_taken=0: prm DEC.
  T1 entry: 0x020B -> 0x0209 (CTR 101->100, USE unchanged)
  USE: pred_diff=0 (T1 CTR[2]=1, T0 CTR[1]=1) ->
  Table 7 row 1: no USE write.

Expected unchanged entries (negative checks):
  T3 u_ram_s0.mem[1][64] = 0x0000
    Consecutive to T2 (T2+1=T3): not written.
    Confirms no-consecutive-table constraint from
    tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md.
  T4 u_ram_s0.mem[1][64] = 0x0000
    Scan stops at T2: T4 not reached.

Re-predict: T2 now valid, tag=0x02 hits.
  T1 also hits but T2 has longer history.
  prm_comp=2, prm_ctr=3'b100.

Pass criterion:
  alc_comp=2, alc_tag=0x02, pred_tkn=1
  T2[1][64]=0x0209, T1[1][64]=0x0209
  T3[1][64]=0x0000, T4[1][64]=0x0000
  repred prm_comp=2, prm_ctr=3'b100

Citation: tage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md -- trigger, candidate
selection, no-consecutive constraint, initialization.

## What was delivered

1. frontend/branch_predictor/tb/tb_tage.sv
   - File header updated with BP-014f and BP-014g entries.
   - _alloc_t0_provider_tst and _alloc_no_consecutive_tst
     enable integers added.
   - alloc_t0_provider_tst task (TC-42) added.
   - alloc_no_consecutive_tst task (TC-43) added.
   - Main initial block: BP-014g invocations added after
     BP-014f tests.
   - Final verdict updated from BP-014f to BP-014g.
   - All 43 tests pass under sim_tage_fast and sim_tage.
2. frontend/branch_predictor/Makefile -- no changes required.
3. Results Capture in prompts/BP-014g.md (this file).

## Test Case Results

TC-42 (alloc_t0_provider_tst):
  PASS: alc_comp=1, alc_tag=0x01, pred_tkn=1.
  PASS: T1[0][832]=0x0109 (allocated correctly).
  PASS: T0[0][832]=2'b01 (CTR 10->01 per debt #34).
  PASS: T2-T4[0][832]=0x0000 (unchanged).
  PASS: repred prm_comp=1, prm_ctr=3'b100.

TC-43 (alloc_no_consecutive_tst):
  PASS: alc_comp=2, alc_tag=0x02, pred_tkn=1.
  PASS: T2[1][64]=0x0209 (allocated correctly).
  PASS: T1[1][64]=0x0209 (CTR 101->100, USE unchanged).
  PASS: T3[1][64]=0x0000 (consecutive guard confirmed).
  PASS: T4[1][64]=0x0000 (not reached, unchanged).
  PASS: repred prm_comp=2, prm_ctr=3'b100.

sim_tage_fast: all 43 tests pass.
sim_tage:      all 43 tests pass.

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt

1. The prompt states "T0 CTR under FAST_INIT = 2'b10".
   TAGE_SRAM_INIT_VALUE=0 gives T0 CTR=2'b00 under FAST_INIT.
   Resolved: T0 is explicitly pre-loaded with 2'b10 in TC-43
   to achieve pred_diff=0 as intended by the prompt.
   TC-42 also pre-loads T0 with 2'b10 per the prompt spec
   (T0 provider must predict taken).

2. The prompt says T2-T4 are unloaded in TC-43 relying on
   FAST_INIT=0. In practice, PC=40'h1100 aliases bank=1
   row=64 which is shared with PC=40'h09100 (BP-014a). T2
   at that address had USE=1 (u_eff=1) from a prior test,
   causing T3 to be selected instead of T2.
   Resolved: T2, T3, T4 are explicitly zeroed before TC-43.
   This is necessary defensive initialization, not a protocol
   change. See Decisions section for details.

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt

1. Explicit zero pre-load for T2-T4 in TC-43.
   PC=40'h1100 maps to idx_hash=1088 (bank=1, row=64).
   PC=40'h09100 (used in rt_ctr_rows1_2_tst, BP-014a) maps
   to the same idx_hash. That test left T2[1][64]=0x121d
   with USE=1 (EPC=0, lcl_epoch=0 -> age=0 -> u_eff=USE=1).
   The RTL alloc scan (tage_cntrl.sv lines 529-537) checks
   ueff_p1[t][s]==2'b00 strictly. T2 with u_eff=1 is skipped;
   T3 was selected (alc_comp=3) without the fix.
   Fix: zero T2, T3, T4 at [1][64] before TC-43 to ensure
   u_eff=0 and T2 is the first qualifying candidate.

2. The no-consecutive-table constraint was verified via the
   RTL scan loop (lines 529-537 of tage_cntrl.sv). The loop
   uses the condition alc_comp_p1[s]=='0 to stop after the
   first selection. Once T2 is selected, alc_comp is non-zero
   and T3 is not re-evaluated. T3 remains 0.

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed

1. Debt #34 (pre-existing): T0 CTR update in tage_cntrl.sv
   keys on pred_crt (correct/incorrect) not resolved_taken
   direction. Row 13c in the CTR update rules documents
   T0 DEC when pred_tkn=1 and resolved_taken=0. RTL adds 1
   when pred_crt=1, subtracts 1 when pred_crt=0. When T0
   mispredicts (pred_crt=0) the CTR moves away from the
   prediction regardless of absolute direction. This is
   functionally correct for saturating counter management
   but deviates from the literal table semantics.
   Test TC-42 verifies the RTL behavior (2'b10->2'b01).
   Planning doc compliance: not a new gap, previously noted.

2. Address aliasing: PC=40'h09100 (BP-014a) and PC=40'h1100
   (TC-43) share idx_hash=1088 (bank=1, row=64) for T1-T4.
   Tests in a shared simulation accumulate state across
   address-aliased entries. Explicit pre-load / zeroing is
   required for any test where the target address may have
   been touched by a prior test. This is a testbench hygiene
   issue, not an RTL compliance risk.

## Deferred Work

None identified in this session.

## Other Notes

Run log entries (sim_tage_fast, relevant portion):
  [PASS] alloc_t0_provider_tst: 0 failures
  [PASS] alloc_no_consecutive_tst: 0 failures
  [PASS] BP-014g: all tests passed

## Files Modified
Not captured

Add two aging round-trip tests to tb_tage.sv. TC-44
verifies that an entry with age=1 has u_eff=USEFUL>>1
and is not selected as an allocation candidate when
u_eff!=0. TC-45 verifies that an entry with age=2 has
u_eff=0 and IS selected as an allocation candidate
despite raw USEFUL!=0. Both tests use lcl_epoch
advanced via hierarchical write. All 43 prior tests
must pass unchanged. All tests use +TAGE_FAST_INIT=1.

sessions.pa_assessment:
45/45 passing. Clean results. Both aging behaviors verified — age=1 entry correctly skipped as allocation candidate, age=2 entry correctly selected despite raw USEFUL!=0, and EPC=2 written correctly into the allocated entry for TC-45.
Two items worth noting:
lcl_epoch array notation — the prompt said lcl_epoch_0 (scalar) but RTL uses lcl_epoch[0] (array). Claude Code caught this and used the correct path. The prompt template for any future session touching epoch should use lcl_epoch[0] not lcl_epoch_0.
EPC timing note (RVA2) — the observation that EPC is written from the current cycle's lcl_epoch rather than the predict-time epoch is worth keeping in mind for debt #33 (simultaneous pred+update protocol). If a prediction fires in the same cycle as an update that advances the epoch, the EPC written could differ from what the predictor used. Not a current gap with aging disabled but relevant for the full integration.
