sessions.overview:
Add update path to ittage_cntrl.sv produced by BP-034.
Covers: CTR update strobes and write data, USE update,
EPC update, TGT update, allocation write data assembly,
UAON counter update logic, aging interval and epoch logic,
u_eff computation replacing raw USE in prediction path,
and ittage_upd_rdy_u1. No ports are added. No testbench.
sessions.ia_output:
```
  What was done:
  - Replaced all 8 stub locations in ittage_cntrl.sv with working update path
  logic
  - Added u_eff computation (aging-adjusted useful bits per table/slot using 2b
  epoch subtraction)
  - Replaced raw USE with u_eff in the allocation candidate scan and meta
  assembly
  - Implemented UAON update (saturating INC/DEC gated on hit + !pred_strong)
  - Implemented aging interval/epoch logic (decrement on rdy_p2_r, reload on
  zero)
  - Implemented CTR update (non-provider CTR INC/DEC, saturating, per CTR table
  rows 2-9)
  - Implemented USE+EPC update (per Table 7, TD-gated, saturating, epc_wd =
  lcl_epoch)
  - Implemented TGT update (mispredict AND provider CTR == 0, mutually exclusive
   with CTR DEC)
  - Implemented allocation write (mispredict AND prm_comp < IT5 AND valid
  candidate)
  - Replaced upd_sel_assign with correct alc_tbl_sel_u0 (from update meta) and
  upd_index_u0/alc_index_u0 per BD1
  - Lint: exit 0, zero warnings 
```
sessions.my_assessment:
The IA made the correct choice even though the spec in
  ittage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md still had the old NTH nomenclature.
  This was corrected to be TTH = ittage_hit==1
```
  A1. USE update (Table 7) fires on rows 4-7 when V=1 AND
    ittage_hit=1 AND TD=1. Rows 2 and 3 produce no USE write even
    when V=1 (NTH=1 or TD=0). This matches the table exactly.
```

The IA made the correct choice, the requirement that ittage_hit be 1
  was not explicit in ittage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md. This has been
  corrected
```
  A2. UAON update gated on ittage_hit from update meta (background
      doc: "UAON has no impact when the tagged tables have missed").
      Step 10 only states "gate on ittage_upd_val_u0" but the
      ittage_hit gate is required by the spec doc.
```

I asked IA to clean this up interactively, this raised context usage
from 67% to 75%.
```
A3. prm_use and alt_use signals set in the provider scan are now
    unused in meta_asm (replaced by prm_u_eff/alt_u_eff). They
    remain declared and driven by the scan. Suppressed by
    -Wno-UNUSED in VER_FLAGS per project convention.
```
sessions.pa_assessment:
NOTE: This results analysis session was very inefficient with
a lot of back and forth. The PA needed to be told to dig deeper
into the results multiple time. The PA repeated things already
captured and create call to action for actions that were already
complete. Eventually it was able to discuss a fix for the deffered
work re: "- prm_tbl_sel_u0 and alt_tbl_sel_u0 " The root cause
of this occurred in 034 when the PA instructed the IA to tie off
these signals to prediction time values instead of the meta returned
during update. A fix for this is planned for 036.

Claude.ai Assessment
A2 gap in BP-035 prompt — the UAON update gate on ittage_hit was not in the Step 10 requirement. IA caught it from the spec doc. The prompt needs an antipattern entry or a correction to the UAON update rules doc to make this explicit so future prompts don't miss it.
D2 deferred item — prm_tbl_sel_u0 and alt_tbl_sel_u0 driven from the prediction scan signals during update cycles. During update the prediction scan is idle and those signals reflect whatever the last prediction left them at. This is not correct update-time behavior. ittage.sv will need to mux these properly at instantiation. Flag as a known gap to resolve at ittage.sv implementation time, not a defect in ittage_cntrl.sv.

Planning File Updates Before BP-036
ittage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md — add explicit statement that UAON update is gated on ittage_hit == 1 from update meta. The background section already says "UAON has no impact when the tagged tables have missed" but it is not reflected as a gate condition in the update rules section.
ittage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md — NTH nomenclature corrected to TTH per your note. Confirm this edit was made manually.
PROJECT_STATUS.md — ittage_cntrl row updated to reflect BP-034 and BP-035 complete. Add TD for alc_index_u0 over-design and the prm/alt_tbl_sel update-time issue at ittage.sv.

sessions.prompt_raw:
## Task ID
BP-035

## Context Loaded
@planning/interfaces/ittage_interfaces.md
@planning/interfaces/ittage_table_interfaces.md
@planning/arch/ittage_cntrl_decisions.md
@planning/arch/ittage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/ittage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/ittage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/ittage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage_cntrl.sv

## Hypothesis
The update path logic can be added to ittage_cntrl.sv as
independent always_comb blocks per update function, driven
directly from ittage_upd_inp_u0 fields, with no new ports
and no structural changes to the prediction path except
replacing raw USE with u_eff.

## Background
BP-034 delivered ittage_cntrl.sv with the complete prediction
path (p0-p2) and all update path outputs stubbed to 0.
BP-035 replaces those stubs with working update logic.

No ports are added. All ports were declared final in BP-034.
The only prediction path change is replacing raw USE with
u_eff in the allocation candidate scan and in the meta
fields ittage_prm_useful and ittage_alt_useful.

ITTAGE has no T0 base table. The maximum number of table
writes per update per slot is three: provider CTR or USE
or TGT (at provider index), alternate CTR (at provider
index via upd_index_u0), and allocation target (at
alc_index_u0). CTR and TGT writes are mutually exclusive
per ittage_cntrl_decisions.md §Concurrent CTR and TGT
Writes. CTR and USE writes may target the same table and
same index simultaneously; ittage_table performs the merged
write per ittage_cntrl_decisions.md §Concurrent CTR and
USE Writes.

## Binding Previous Decisions

1. upd_index_u0[s] carries the provider index.
   Provider index selection:
     ittage_using_primary == 1: upd_index_u0[s] = prm_idx
     ittage_using_primary == 0: upd_index_u0[s] = alt_idx
   Where prm_idx and alt_idx come from the update input
   meta: ittage_upd_inp_u0[s].ittage_pred_meta.ittage_prm_idx
   and ittage_upd_inp_u0[s].ittage_pred_meta.ittage_alt_idx.
   alc_index_u0[s] = upd_index_u0[s]. This is an over-design
   kept until performance analysis (TD pending).

2. V in CTR and USE update tables = ittage_upd_val_u0[s].
   Upstream confirms pred_src == PRED_ITTAGE before
   asserting. No internal re-check.

3. NTH (no_tagged_hit) in USE update table:
   NTH = !ittage_upd_inp_u0[s].ittage_pred_meta.ittage_hit

4. Signal aliases for update path. All fields read from
   ittage_upd_inp_u0[s].ittage_pred_meta (abbreviated
   as meta[s] below):
     using_primary  = meta[s].ittage_using_primary
     prm_comp       = meta[s].ittage_prm_comp
     alt_comp       = meta[s].ittage_alt_comp
     prm_ctr        = meta[s].ittage_prm_ctr
     alt_ctr        = meta[s].ittage_alt_ctr
     prm_useful     = meta[s].ittage_prm_useful
     alt_useful     = meta[s].ittage_alt_useful
     prm_tgt        = meta[s].ittage_prm_tgt
     alt_tgt        = meta[s].ittage_alt_tgt
     pred_strong    = meta[s].ittage_pred_strong
     ittage_hit     = meta[s].ittage_hit
     alc_comp_u     = meta[s].ittage_alc_comp
     alc_idx_u      = meta[s].ittage_alc_idx
     alc_tag_u      = meta[s].ittage_alc_tag
     indir_misp     = ittage_upd_inp_u0[s].indir_mispredict
     resolved_tgt   = ittage_upd_inp_u0[s].resolved_target

5. prm_tbl_sel_u0 and alt_tbl_sel_u0 are already driven
   from prediction-phase meta in BP-034. Do not change
   them. alc_tbl_sel_u0[s] must be driven from alc_comp_u
   (ittage_upd_inp_u0[s].ittage_pred_meta.ittage_alc_comp).
   BP-034 left it undriven or at zero. Correct it in BP-035.

6. u_eff replaces raw USE in two places in the prediction
   path: the allocation candidate scan (u_eff == 0 test
   replacing raw USE == 0) and the meta fields
   ittage_prm_useful and ittage_alt_useful. u_eff is
   computed combinationally per ittage_cntrl_use_update_
   rules.md §Effective Useful Computation using lcl_epoch
   and the EPC field from tbl_cntrl_bits_p1.

7. CTR saturating arithmetic: increment saturates at
   IT_MAX_CTR_WIDTH'({IT_MAX_CTR_WIDTH{1'b1}}).
   Decrement saturates at 0. Use explicit conditional
   expressions, not $signed or $unsigned casts.

8. USE saturating arithmetic: same pattern. Saturates at
   IT_MAX_USE_WIDTH'({IT_MAX_USE_WIDTH{1'b1}}) and 0.

9. ittage_upd_rdy_u1[s] = ittage_upd_val_u0[s] registered
   one cycle (u0->u1). Replace the assign '0 stub.

10. Read ittage_cntrl.sv completely before writing any
    code. Identify every stub to be replaced. Do not
    duplicate or re-declare any signal already declared.
    Do not add ports.

## Specific Requirements

Step 1: Read every file in Context Loaded before writing
any code.

Step 2: Read ittage_cntrl.sv completely. List every update
stub present (assign '0 statements, always_comb stubs
for upd_val and upd_inp). Record the exact signal names
and line numbers in Results Capture. These are the only
locations modified in this task.

Step 3: Implement upd_index_u0 and alc_index_u0.
Replace the always_comb stub that drives these signals.
Per Binding Decision 1: upd_index_u0[s] is driven from
provider index; alc_index_u0[s] = upd_index_u0[s].
Use always_comb with one block per slot (generate loop
over NUM_PRED_SLOTS). Do not use dynamic array indexing
on ittage_upd_inp_u0 fields in always_comb -- use a
generate loop with genvar or replicate explicitly.

Step 4: Implement alc_tbl_sel_u0. Per Binding Decision 5:
alc_tbl_sel_u0[s] = TSEL_W'(alc_comp_u[s]).

Step 5: Implement CTR update logic per
ittage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md. Implement as always_comb
per slot. Produce: prm_ctr_wr_u0[s], alt_ctr_wr_u0[s],
prm_ctr_wd_u0[s], alt_ctr_wd_u0[s].

Use the update table rows exactly as specified. V =
ittage_upd_val_u0[s]. using_primary, prm_comp, alt_comp,
indir_mispredict from Binding Decision 4.

Saturating increment and decrement per Binding Decision 7.
Source CTR values from meta (prm_ctr and alt_ctr from
Binding Decision 4). prm_ctr_wr and alt_ctr_wr are
mutually exclusive by construction from the table.

Step 6: Implement USE update logic per
ittage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md Table 7. Implement as
always_comb per slot. Produce: use_wr_u0[s], use_wd_u0[s].

V = ittage_upd_val_u0[s].
NTH = !meta[s].ittage_hit per Binding Decision 3.
TD = (meta[s].ittage_prm_tgt != meta[s].ittage_alt_tgt).
Using_prm, Mispredict from Binding Decision 4.

Useful WD source (prm_useful or alt_useful from meta)
and Useful SEL (prm_comp or alt_comp) are taken from the
Useful SEL and Useful WD columns of Table 7.
Saturating inc/dec per Binding Decision 8 applied to the
source useful value from meta.

Step 7: Implement EPC update. EPC is written alongside
every USE write. When use_wr_u0[s] asserts, epc_wr_u0[s]
also asserts. epc_wd_u0[s] = lcl_epoch[s] (current epoch
value for the slot). Implement in the same always_comb
block as USE update.

Step 8: Implement TGT update logic per ittage_interfaces.md
§Target Write Gating. Produce: tgt_wr_u0[s], tgt_wd_u0[s].

Gating conditions (all must hold):
  indir_mispredict == 1
  AND provider CTR from meta == IT_MAX_CTR_WIDTH'(0)
  AND provider CTR: use prm_ctr when using_primary==1,
      alt_ctr when using_primary==0.

tgt_wr_u0 and the active CTR write strobe are mutually
exclusive per ittage_cntrl_decisions.md §Concurrent CTR
and TGT Writes. Do not assert both in the same cycle.

tgt_wd_u0[s] = resolved_target from Binding Decision 4.

Step 9: Implement allocation write data assembly and
alc_wr_u0 strobe per ittage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md
§Write Strobe Generation and §Allocation Write Data
Assembly.

alc_wr_u0[s] asserts when:
  indir_mispredict == 1
  AND prm_comp < IT_NUM_TABLES-1  (provider is not IT5)
  AND alc_comp_u != TSEL_W'(0)   (valid candidate)

alc_wd_u0[s] field assembly (MSB to LSB per entry layout
in ittage_table_interfaces.md §Entry Format):
  [IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH-1 : CNTRL_BITS_WIDTH]
    = alc_tag_u[s]  (IT_MAX_TAG_WIDTH bits)
  [CB_TGT_HI : CB_TGT_LO]
    = resolved_target[s]  (IT_MAX_TGT_WIDTH bits)
  [CB_EPC_HI : CB_EPC_LO]
    = lcl_epoch[s]  (IT_MAX_EPC_WIDTH bits)
  [CB_USE_HI : CB_USE_LO]
    = '0  (null useful)
  [CB_CTR_HI : CB_CTR_LO]
    = '0  (null confidence)
  [0]
    = 1'b1  (valid)

Step 10: Implement UAON update logic. Replace the stub
in the UAON always_ff block. Per ittage_cntrl_uaon_update_
rules.md §UAON Modification During Update.

Signals from Binding Decision 4:
  pred_strong = meta[s].ittage_pred_strong
  prm_correct = (resolved_tgt == prm_tgt)
  prm_wrong   = (resolved_tgt != prm_tgt)
  alt_correct = (resolved_tgt == alt_tgt)
  alt_wrong   = (resolved_tgt != alt_tgt)

Apply update rules truth table from the document.
Saturate uaon[s] at IT_UAON_WIDTH max and 0.
Only update when ittage_upd_val_u0[s] is asserted.

Step 11: Implement aging logic. Replace the aging stubs.
Per ittage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md §Interval and Epoch
Operation.

lcl_aging_interval[s] decrements on each assertion of
rdy_p2_r[s] (the registered ittage_pred_rdy_p2 signal
from BP-034). When it reaches zero: lcl_epoch[s]
increments (2b wrapping) and lcl_aging_interval[s]
reloads from ittage_aging_interval. Only active when
ittage_enable_aging is asserted. Remove the always_comb
stub that consumed ittage_enable_aging.

Step 12: Implement u_eff computation and replace raw USE
in the prediction path. Per ittage_cntrl_use_update_rules
.md §Effective Useful Computation.

For each table t (1..5) and each slot s, compute u_eff:
  age = (lcl_epoch[s] - EPC_from_tbl[t][s]) % 4
  if (age == 0) u_eff = USE_from_tbl[t][s]
  if (age == 1) u_eff = USE_from_tbl[t][s] >> 1
  else          u_eff = 0

EPC_from_tbl[t][s] = tbl_cntrl_bits_p1[t][s][CB_EPC_HI:
  CB_EPC_LO] (registered p1 value).
USE_from_tbl[t][s] = tbl_cntrl_bits_p1[t][s][CB_USE_HI:
  CB_USE_LO] (registered p1 value).

Declare:
  logic [IT_MAX_USE_WIDTH-1:0]
    u_eff[0:IT_NUM_TABLES-1][0:NUM_PRED_SLOTS-1];

Implement in an always_comb block using explicit if-else
chains per table index (5,4,3,2,1). Do not use dynamic
array indexing on tbl_cntrl_bits_p1 in always_comb.

Replace raw USE with u_eff in two locations in the
prediction path always_comb blocks:
  a. Allocation candidate scan (Step 7 of BP-034): the
     u_eff == 0 test replaces USE == 0.
  b. Meta assembly (Step 9 of BP-034): ittage_prm_useful
     and ittage_alt_useful are set from u_eff of the
     primary and alternate provider tables.
  Both locations are in always_comb blocks. Locate them
  by signal name and replace the relevant expressions.

Step 13: Implement ittage_upd_rdy_u1. Replace assign '0
with registered ittage_upd_val_u0 per Binding Decision 9.

  always_ff @(posedge clk) begin : upd_rdy_reg
    if (!rstn) ittage_upd_rdy_u1 <= '0;
    else       ittage_upd_rdy_u1 <= ittage_upd_val_u0;
  end

Remove the assign '0 stub for ittage_upd_rdy_u1.

Step 14: Remove the always_comb stubs that consumed
ittage_upd_val_u0 and ittage_upd_inp_u0 with the
"update logic added in BP-035" comment. These signals
are now consumed by the real update logic.

Step 15: Run make lint_ittage_cntrl. Fix all issues.
Lint must exit zero with zero warnings under -Wall
-Wno-DECLFILENAME -Wno-IMPORTSTAR -Wno-UNUSED and any
suppressions carried from BP-034. Record all suppressions
in Results Capture.

Step 16: Write Results Capture into prompts/BP-035.md.

## Constraints
- Do not write a testbench. Testbench is BP-036.
- Do not modify bp_defines_pkg.sv or bp_structs_pkg.sv.
- Do not add ports to ittage_cntrl.sv.
- Do not re-declare any signal already declared in BP-034.
- Do not modify any file except ittage_cntrl.sv and the
  Makefile if lint requires it.
- Lint must exit zero with zero warnings.
- If any requirement is ambiguous, state the assumption
  before proceeding and record it in Results Capture.
- If a check fails, fix the RTL. Do not adjust or remove
  the requirement.

## Deliverables
1. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage_cntrl.sv (modified)
2. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/Makefile (updated if needed)
3. Results Capture filled in below.
Note: If a new module is to be created ensure that
claude.code is allowed or told to modify the Makefile for
lint checking at the minimum.
sessions.results_raw:
## Summary
```
BP-035 adds the complete update path to ittage_cntrl.sv. All
assign-zero stubs replaced with working always_comb and always_ff
logic. u_eff aging-adjusted useful computation replaces raw USE
in the allocation candidate scan and in meta assembly. Lint exits
zero with zero warnings under -Wall -Wno-UNUSED -Wno-IMPORTSTAR
-Wno-VARHIDDEN.

## Test Matrix
Omitted. This is an implementation session. Testbench is BP-036.

## What was delivered
1. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage_cntrl.sv (modified)
   Makefile unchanged (no new lint suppressions required).

## Stubs replaced (line numbers from BP-034 source)
- Line 365-371: uaon_reg always_ff -- added else clause with
  UAON update logic (INC/DEC saturating per uaon_update_rules.md).
- Line 466-474: aging_reg always_ff -- added else-if
  (ittage_enable_aging) clause; decrement interval or wrap epoch.
- Lines 477-481: aging_en_stub always_comb -- removed entirely.
  ittage_enable_aging now consumed by aging_reg.
- Line 487: assign ittage_upd_rdy_u1 = 0 -- replaced with
  always_ff upd_rdy_reg (registered ittage_upd_val_u0).
- Lines 488-493: six assign-zero write strobe stubs -- removed.
  Replaced by g_ctr_upd, g_use_upd, g_tgt_upd, g_alc_upd generate
  blocks with always_comb per slot.
- Lines 495-504: upd_data_stub always_comb -- removed. Write data
  now driven by the same generate blocks.
- Lines 507-515: upd_sel_assign always_comb -- removed. Replaced
  by g_upd_sel generate loop. alc_tbl_sel_u0 now driven from
  update input meta (BD5). upd_index_u0 now driven from provider
  idx in update meta per BD1. alc_index_u0 = upd_index_u0 (BD1).
- Lines 518-524: upd_inp_stub always_comb -- removed. All update
  inputs now consumed by real logic.

## New logic added
- g_u_eff: always_comb per slot computing u_eff[0:5][s].
  Explicit if-else for IT1..IT5; u_eff[0][s] = 0 (IT0 unused).
  age = lcl_epoch[s] - EPC_field (2b wrapping). age==0 -> use_v,
  age==1 -> use_v>>1, else 0.
- g_prm_u_eff / g_alt_u_eff: case mux selecting u_eff for
  primary and alternate provider tables. Used in meta_asm.
- meta_asm: ittage_prm_useful and ittage_alt_useful now assigned
  from prm_u_eff[s] and alt_u_eff[s] respectively (not prm_use,
  alt_use).
- alc_scan: u_eff[t][s] == 0 replaces raw USE field == 0 test.
- g_ctr_upd: CTR update per slot. When using_primary: alt CTR
  INC/DEC. When !using_primary: prm CTR INC/DEC. Saturating per
  BD7. Rows 2-9 of CTR update table.
- g_use_upd: USE and EPC update per slot. Fires when V, hit, and
  TD (prm_tgt != alt_tgt). Saturating INC/DEC on prm_useful or
  alt_useful from meta. epc_wd = lcl_epoch[s].
- g_tgt_upd: TGT update per slot. Fires on mispredict when
  provider CTR == 0. Mutually exclusive with CTR DEC by design.
- g_alc_upd: allocation write strobe and data per slot. Fires on
  mispredict when prm_comp < IT_NUM_TABLES-1 and alc_comp != 0.
  alc_wd assembled as {alc_tag, resolved_target, lcl_epoch, 0, 0,
  1b valid}.

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt
A1. USE update (Table 7) fires on rows 4-7 when V=1 AND
    ittage_hit=1 AND TD=1. Rows 2 and 3 produce no USE write even
    when V=1 (NTH=1 or TD=0). This matches the table exactly.

A2. UAON update gated on ittage_hit from update meta (background
    doc: "UAON has no impact when the tagged tables have missed").
    Step 10 only states "gate on ittage_upd_val_u0" but the
    ittage_hit gate is required by the spec doc.

A3. prm_use and alt_use signals set in the provider scan are now
    unused in meta_asm (replaced by prm_u_eff/alt_u_eff). They
    remain declared and driven by the scan. Suppressed by
    -Wno-UNUSED in VER_FLAGS per project convention.

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt
D1. alc_wd_u0 assembled using concatenation:
    {alc_tag(11b), resolved_target(38b), lcl_epoch(2b),
     USE_ZEROS(2b), CTR_ZEROS(3b), VALID(1b)} = 57b = 
    IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH. Matches CB_* field layout exactly.

D2. u_eff computation placed after alc_idx_tag blocks and before
    UAON registers to allow forward references to lcl_epoch
    (SV forward reference, no order requirement in hardware).

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed
None new in this session. Existing gaps tracked in ittage_interfaces
.md Known Gaps table (II2 PHR hashing, II3 flush ports, II4 FTQ
meta overload) are unchanged.

## Deferred Work
- BD1 note: alc_index_u0[s] = upd_index_u0[s] is an over-design
  (uses provider index instead of alc_idx from meta). Performance
  analysis deferred per binding decision.
- prm_tbl_sel_u0 and alt_tbl_sel_u0 driven from current-cycle
  prediction scan (prm_comp/alt_comp) per BD5. When prediction
  and update are mutually exclusive these will be zero during
  updates. Correct gating depends on ittage.sv / ittage_table.sv
  behavior. Flagged as a known limitation carried from BP-034.

## Other Notes
Lint suppressions in use (from VER_FLAGS in Makefile, unchanged):
  -Wno-UNUSED, -Wno-IMPORTSTAR, -Wno-VARHIDDEN.
No session-specific suppressions added in BP-035.

## Files Modified
Not captured

sessions.overview:
Fix prm_tbl_sel_u0 and alt_tbl_sel_u0 source in
ittage_cntrl.sv (driven from prediction scan intermediates
in BP-034/035 -- must be driven from update input meta).
Then implement tb_ittage_cntrl.sv with pre-computed test
vectors covering the prediction path, update path, and
UAON update.
sessions.ia_output:
● BP-036 is complete. 76 PASS, 0 FAIL.

  The two RTL fixes beyond the scoped prm/alt_tbl_sel correction were both
  driven by failing tests: (1) prv_alt_scan gated on pred_val_p1[s] to force
  Verilator out of stl_sequent into nba_sequent, and (2) meta_p1 intermediate
  eliminated so meta_p2_reg reads scan outputs directly, avoiding a second
  scheduling inversion. The testbench fix — driving tbl_hit_p1 before the p0→p1
  posedge — was necessary to match Verilator's coroutine model where NBA-phase
  reads at a clock edge see signals present before that edge.

✻ Cooked for 1h 35m 59s

※ recap: BP-036 is complete: ittage_cntrl fixed and verified with 76 passing
  checks across 15 test cases. Results Capture written to prompts/BP-036.md.
  (disable recaps in /config)
sessions.my_assessment:
The only comment is this was a large task requiring
90min and at least 1 compaction event.

Nothing else out of bounds to report.
sessions.pa_assessment:
Defects 2 and 3 are significant findings. The Verilator 5.020 scheduling classification behavior — stl_sequent vs nba_sequent — is not documented anywhere in the project and bit us here in a non-obvious way. The rule in the Other Notes is exactly right: any read of a FF output in an always_comb block forces nba_sequent classification. This needs to go into CLAUDE.md or ANTIPATTERNS.md so future prompts explicitly gate prediction scan always_comb blocks on a registered signal.
Defect 3 — eliminating the meta_p1 intermediate — is a structural RTL change beyond the scoped fix. It is correct and the observable behavior is identical, but it changes the internal architecture of ittage_cntrl.sv in a way that future sessions need to know about. The Results Capture documents it clearly.
sessions.discussion_raw:
# Results Discussion 
## Claude.code Console Output
● BP-036 is complete. 76 PASS, 0 FAIL.

  The two RTL fixes beyond the scoped prm/alt_tbl_sel correction were both
  driven by failing tests: (1) prv_alt_scan gated on pred_val_p1[s] to force
  Verilator out of stl_sequent into nba_sequent, and (2) meta_p1 intermediate
  eliminated so meta_p2_reg reads scan outputs directly, avoiding a second
  scheduling inversion. The testbench fix — driving tbl_hit_p1 before the p0→p1
  posedge — was necessary to match Verilator's coroutine model where NBA-phase
  reads at a clock edge see signals present before that edge.

✻ Cooked for 1h 35m 59s

※ recap: BP-036 is complete: ittage_cntrl fixed and verified with 76 passing
  checks across 15 test cases. Results Capture written to prompts/BP-036.md.
  (disable recaps in /config)

## My Assessment

The only comment is this was a large task requiring
90min and at least 1 compaction event.

Nothing else out of bounds to report.

## Claude.ai Assessment

Defects 2 and 3 are significant findings. The Verilator 5.020 scheduling classification behavior — stl_sequent vs nba_sequent — is not documented anywhere in the project and bit us here in a non-obvious way. The rule in the Other Notes is exactly right: any read of a FF output in an always_comb block forces nba_sequent classification. This needs to go into CLAUDE.md or ANTIPATTERNS.md so future prompts explicitly gate prediction scan always_comb blocks on a registered signal.
Defect 3 — eliminating the meta_p1 intermediate — is a structural RTL change beyond the scoped fix. It is correct and the observable behavior is identical, but it changes the internal architecture of ittage_cntrl.sv in a way that future sessions need to know about. The Results Capture documents it clearly.


## Follow-on Actions
None
## CLAUDE.md Updates
[x] stl_sequent rule added
## Other Planning File Updates
None
sessions.prompt_raw:
## Task ID
BP-036

## Context Loaded
@planning/arch/ittage_cntrl_ctr_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/ittage_cntrl_use_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/ittage_cntrl_uaon_update_rules.md
@planning/arch/ittage_cntrl_alloc_rules.md
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage_cntrl.sv

## Hypothesis
ittage_cntrl.sv can be verified in isolation by driving
the table input ports directly as stimulus, without
instantiating ittage_table. Pre-computed test vectors
eliminate correlated-bug risk.

## Background
BP-034 and BP-035 delivered ittage_cntrl.sv with the
complete prediction path and update path. A defect was
identified after BP-035: prm_tbl_sel_u0 and
alt_tbl_sel_u0 are driven from prediction scan
intermediates (prm_comp, alt_comp) rather than from the
update input meta. This must be fixed before the testbench
runs.

The testbench drives tbl_hit_p1, tbl_cntrl_bits_p1,
tbl_idx_hash_p0, and tbl_tag_hash_p0 directly as
stimulus. ittage_table is not instantiated.

## Binding Previous Decisions

1. Fix required before testbench: locate the always_comb
   block in ittage_cntrl.sv that drives prm_tbl_sel_u0
   and alt_tbl_sel_u0. Replace the sources as follows:
     prm_tbl_sel_u0[s] =
       TSEL_W'(ittage_upd_inp_u0[s].ittage_pred_meta
               .ittage_prm_comp)
     alt_tbl_sel_u0[s] =
       TSEL_W'(ittage_upd_inp_u0[s].ittage_pred_meta
               .ittage_alt_comp)
   Prediction and update are mutually exclusive. Driving
   from update meta is correct in all cycles.

2. The testbench module name is tb. File name is
   tb_ittage_cntrl.sv per CLAUDE.md convention.

3. CNTRL_BITS_WIDTH = 46. Field positions within
   tbl_cntrl_bits_p1 words (use CB_* values from
   ittage_cntrl.sv):
     VAL = [0]
     CTR = [3:1]
     USE = [5:4]
     EPC = [7:6]
     TGT = [45:8]
   Assemble stimulus words as:
     {tgt[37:0], epc[1:0], use[1:0], ctr[2:0], val}

4. IT_NUM_TABLES = 6. Table indices 1..5 are active.
   Index 0 is unused placeholder. tbl_* arrays are
   sized [0:IT_NUM_TABLES-1].

5. UAON registers reset to IT_UAON_THRES = 8. Tests
   that require a different UAON value must drive enough
   update cycles to move the counter before the
   prediction under test.

6. All expected values in the Test Vector Table below
   are pre-computed from the planning documents. If a
   check fails, fix the RTL. Do not adjust expected
   values.

7. tbl_hit_p1 is a p1 signal. It is presented one cycle
   after ittage_pred_val_p0. The testbench must drive
   tbl_hit_p1 on the cycle after pred_val_p0 is
   asserted and hold it for one cycle. ittage_pred_meta
   _p2 is valid two cycles after pred_val_p0.

8. ittage_upd_rdy_u1 is asserted one cycle after
   ittage_upd_val_u0. Update strobe outputs
   (prm_ctr_wr_u0, alt_ctr_wr_u0, use_wr_u0, epc_wr_u0,
   tgt_wr_u0, alc_wr_u0) are combinational and valid
   in the same cycle as ittage_upd_val_u0.

## Specific Requirements

Step 1: Read every file in Context Loaded before writing
any code.

Step 2: Read ittage_cntrl.sv completely. Locate the
always_comb block driving prm_tbl_sel_u0 and
alt_tbl_sel_u0. Verify they are driven from prediction
scan intermediates. Apply the fix per Binding Decision 1.
Record the block name and line numbers in Results Capture.
Run make lint_ittage_cntrl to confirm lint still passes
after the fix. Stop and report if lint fails.

Step 3: Create rtl/core/frontend/bpu/tb/tb_ittage_cntrl.sv.
Instantiate ittage_cntrl as dut. Drive all tbl_* input
ports directly as stimulus. Do not instantiate ittage_table.

Step 4: Implement a clk/rstn generator. 10ns clock period.
Assert rstn low for 4 cycles, then deassert. After rstn
deasserts wait 2 cycles before first stimulus.

Step 5: Implement a task or set of tasks to drive one
prediction request and capture the p2 response. Tasks
drive: ittage_pred_val_p0, ittage_pred_inp_p0 at p0;
tbl_hit_p1, tbl_cntrl_bits_p1, tbl_pred_tgt_p1,
tbl_idx_hash_p0, tbl_tag_hash_p0 at p1 (one cycle
later). Wait two cycles total after pred_val_p0 then
sample ittage_pred_meta_p2 and ittage_pred_rdy_p2.

Step 6: Implement self-checking tests per the Test Vector
Table below. Each test prints PASS or FAIL with the test
name. On FAIL print expected and actual values. Do not
stop on first failure -- run all tests and report total
pass/fail count at the end.

Step 7: Add make sim_ittage_cntrl target to the Makefile.
Read the Makefile before modifying. Do not break existing
targets.

Step 8: Run make sim_ittage_cntrl. All tests must pass.
Fix RTL in ittage_cntrl.sv if any test fails. Do not
adjust expected values.

Step 9: Write Results Capture into prompts/BP-036.md.

---

## Test Vector Table

Pre-computed expected values. All field widths from
bp_defines_pkg.sv. Stimulus assembled per Binding
Decision 3. Tests are independent unless noted.

---

### Prediction Path Tests

**TC-PRED-01: No hit, any slot**
Setup:
  ittage_pred_val_p0[0] = 1
  ittage_pred_inp_p0[0].pc = 40'hAAA
  All tbl_hit_p1[t][0] = 0 for t=0..5
  All tbl_cntrl_bits_p1 = 0
Expected at p2:
  ittage_pred_rdy_p2[0] = 1
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_hit = 0
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_prm_comp = 0
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_alt_comp = 0
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_pred_strong = 0

**TC-PRED-02: IT5 only hits, strong CTR**
Setup:
  tbl_hit_p1[5][0] = 1, all others 0
  tbl_cntrl_bits_p1[5][0]:
    VAL=1, CTR=3'b110, USE=2'b10, EPC=2'b00,
    TGT=38'h0000_0000_1234
  tbl_pred_tgt_p1[5][0] = 38'h0000_0000_1234
  tbl_idx_hash_p0[5][0] = 9'h055
  tbl_tag_hash_p0[5][0] = 11'h2AA
Expected at p2:
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_hit = 1
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_prm_comp = 3'(5)
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_alt_comp = 0
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_prm_idx = 9'h055
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_prm_ctr = 3'b110
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_pred_strong = 1
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_using_primary = 1
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_pred_tgt =
    38'h0000_0000_1234

**TC-PRED-03: IT5 and IT3 hit, primary=IT5, alt=IT3**
Setup:
  tbl_hit_p1[5][0] = 1, tbl_hit_p1[3][0] = 1,
  others 0
  tbl_cntrl_bits_p1[5][0]:
    VAL=1, CTR=3'b101, USE=2'b01, EPC=2'b00,
    TGT=38'h0000_0000_AAAA
  tbl_pred_tgt_p1[5][0] = 38'h0000_0000_AAAA
  tbl_idx_hash_p0[5][0] = 9'h011
  tbl_cntrl_bits_p1[3][0]:
    VAL=1, CTR=3'b010, USE=2'b00, EPC=2'b00,
    TGT=38'h0000_0000_BBBB
  tbl_pred_tgt_p1[3][0] = 38'h0000_0000_BBBB
  tbl_idx_hash_p0[3][0] = 9'h022
Expected at p2:
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_prm_comp = 3'(5)
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_alt_comp = 3'(3)
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_prm_idx = 9'h011
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_alt_idx = 9'h022
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_prm_ctr = 3'b101
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_alt_ctr = 3'b010
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_using_primary = 1
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_pred_tgt =
    38'h0000_0000_AAAA
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_pred_strong = 1

**TC-PRED-04: UAON fires, alt selected**
Precondition: uaon[0] = IT_UAON_THRES (reset value = 8).
Setup:
  tbl_hit_p1[3][0] = 1 (primary, CTR=null)
  tbl_hit_p1[1][0] = 1 (alternate)
  tbl_cntrl_bits_p1[3][0]:
    VAL=1, CTR=3'b000, USE=2'b00, EPC=2'b00,
    TGT=38'h0000_0000_1111
  tbl_pred_tgt_p1[3][0] = 38'h0000_0000_1111
  tbl_cntrl_bits_p1[1][0]:
    VAL=1, CTR=3'b011, USE=2'b01, EPC=2'b00,
    TGT=38'h0000_0000_2222
  tbl_pred_tgt_p1[1][0] = 38'h0000_0000_2222
Expected at p2:
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_hit = 1
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_prm_comp = 3'(3)
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_alt_comp = 3'(1)
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_using_primary = 0
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_use_alt_on_na = 1
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_pred_tgt =
    38'h0000_0000_2222
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_pred_strong = 1
  (final CTR = alt CTR = 3'b011 != 0)

**TC-PRED-05: UAON fires but no alt, primary used**
Setup: same as TC-PRED-04 but tbl_hit_p1[1][0] = 0
Expected at p2:
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_using_primary = 1
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_pred_tgt =
    38'h0000_0000_1111
  ittage_pred_meta_p2[0].ittage_pred_strong = 0
  (final CTR = prm CTR = 3'b000, not null = 0)

**TC-PRED-06: Dual slot independent operation**
Slot 0: IT5 hits, slot 1: IT2 hits, no overlap.
  tbl_hit_p1[5][0] = 1, tbl_hit_p1[2][1] = 1
  tbl_cntrl_bits_p1[5][0]: VAL=1, CTR=3'b111,
    USE=2'b11, EPC=2'b00, TGT=38'h0000_0000_CCCC
  tbl_cntrl_bits_p1[2][1]: VAL=1, CTR=3'b001,
    USE=2'b00, EPC=2'b00, TGT=38'h0000_0000_DDDD
Expected at p2:
  Slot 0: prm_comp=5, hit=1, pred_tgt=CCCC
  Slot 1: prm_comp=2, hit=1, pred_tgt=DDDD
  Slots operate independently, no cross-slot effect.

---

### Update Path Tests

Each update test drives ittage_upd_val_u0 and
ittage_upd_inp_u0 with a fully populated
ittage_upd_inp_t. Checks are on combinational outputs
(write strobes and write data) sampled in the same cycle
as ittage_upd_val_u0. ittage_upd_rdy_u1 checked one
cycle later.

Helper: build ittage_upd_inp_u0[s] with these defaults
unless overridden:
  indir_mispredict = 0
  resolved_target  = 38'h0000_0000_BEEF
  ittage_pred_meta fields: ittage_hit=1,
    ittage_using_primary=1, ittage_pred_strong=1,
    ittage_prm_comp=3'(3), ittage_alt_comp=3'(1),
    ittage_prm_ctr=3'b101, ittage_alt_ctr=3'b010,
    ittage_prm_idx=9'h033, ittage_alt_idx=9'h011,
    ittage_prm_tgt=38'hAAAA, ittage_alt_tgt=38'hBBBB,
    ittage_alc_comp=3'(4), ittage_alc_idx=9'h044,
    ittage_alc_tag=11'h555, ittage_prm_useful=2'b01,
    ittage_alt_useful=2'b10

**TC-UPD-01: CTR table row 3**
  (V=1, using_primary=1, no_mispredict, alt_comp=1>0)
Override: indir_mispredict=0, using_primary=1,
  alt_comp=3'(1), alt_ctr=3'b010
Expected (combinational, same cycle as upd_val):
  alt_ctr_wr_u0[0] = 1
  prm_ctr_wr_u0[0] = 0
  alt_ctr_wd_u0[0] = 3'b011  (INC from 3'b010)
  use_wr_u0[0] = 0  (prm_tgt==alt_tgt? no, but
    check TD: ittage_prm_tgt=AAAA != ittage_alt_tgt=BBBB
    so TD=1; using_prm=1, no_mispredict -> row 4:
    use_wr=1)
  use_wr_u0[0] = 1
  use_wd_u0[0] = 2'b10  (INC from prm_useful=2'b01)
  epc_wr_u0[0] = 1
  tgt_wr_u0[0] = 0  (no mispredict)
  alc_wr_u0[0] = 0  (no mispredict)

**TC-UPD-02: CTR table row 5**
  (V=1, using_primary=1, mispredict, alt_comp>0)
Override: indir_mispredict=1, using_primary=1,
  alt_comp=3'(1), alt_ctr=3'b010,
  prm_ctr=3'b101 (not null -- no tgt_wr)
Expected:
  alt_ctr_wr_u0[0] = 1
  alt_ctr_wd_u0[0] = 3'b001  (DEC from 3'b010)
  prm_ctr_wr_u0[0] = 0
  tgt_wr_u0[0] = 0  (prm_ctr != 0)
  alc_wr_u0[0] = 1  (mispredict, prm_comp=3 < 5,
                      alc_comp=4 != 0)

**TC-UPD-03: CTR table row 7**
  (V=1, using_primary=0, no mispredict, prm_comp>0)
Override: using_primary=0, indir_mispredict=0,
  prm_comp=3'(3), prm_ctr=3'b011
Expected:
  prm_ctr_wr_u0[0] = 1
  prm_ctr_wd_u0[0] = 3'b100  (INC from 3'b011)
  alt_ctr_wr_u0[0] = 0

**TC-UPD-04: CTR table row 9**
  (V=1, using_primary=0, mispredict, prm_comp>0)
Override: using_primary=0, indir_mispredict=1,
  prm_comp=3'(3), prm_ctr=3'b011 (not null)
Expected:
  prm_ctr_wr_u0[0] = 1
  prm_ctr_wd_u0[0] = 3'b010  (DEC from 3'b011)
  tgt_wr_u0[0] = 0  (prm_ctr != 0 when using_prm=0,
    provider ctr = alt_ctr; alt_ctr=3'b010 != 0)
  Wait -- using_primary=0, provider is alternate.
  Provider CTR = alt_ctr = 3'b010. Not null. No tgt_wr.
  tgt_wr_u0[0] = 0
  alc_wr_u0[0] = 1  (mispredict, prm_comp=3<5,
                      alc_comp=4!=0)

**TC-UPD-05: TGT write fires**
  (mispredict, provider CTR == null)
Override: using_primary=1, indir_mispredict=1,
  prm_ctr=3'b000 (null), alt_ctr=3'b010
Expected:
  tgt_wr_u0[0] = 1
  tgt_wd_u0[0] = 38'h0000_0000_BEEF (resolved_target)
  prm_ctr_wr_u0[0] = 0  (mutual exclusion with tgt_wr)
  alt_ctr_wr_u0[0] = 1  (row 5: mispredict, alt_comp>0)
  alt_ctr_wd_u0[0] = 3'b001  (DEC from 3'b010)

**TC-UPD-06: Allocation write data**
  (mispredict, valid alc_comp, prm_comp < IT5)
Override: indir_mispredict=1, prm_comp=3'(3),
  alc_comp=3'(4), prm_ctr=3'b101 (not null)
  alc_tag=11'h555, resolved_target=38'h0000_0000_BEEF
  lcl_epoch expected = 2'b00 (reset value, no aging)
Expected:
  alc_wr_u0[0] = 1
  alc_tbl_sel_u0[0] = 3'(4)
  alc_index_u0[0] = upd_index_u0[0]
    (= prm_idx = 9'h033 since using_primary=1)
  alc_wd_u0[0] assembled as:
    bits [56:46] = 11'h555          (alc_tag)
    bits [45:8]  = 38'h0000_0000_BEEF (resolved_target)
    bits [7:6]   = 2'b00            (lcl_epoch=0)
    bits [5:4]   = 2'b00            (USE=null)
    bits [3:1]   = 3'b000           (CTR=null)
    bit  [0]     = 1'b1             (VALID)

**TC-UPD-07: ittage_upd_rdy_u1**
Assert ittage_upd_val_u0[0]=1 for one cycle.
Expected: ittage_upd_rdy_u1[0]=1 one cycle later.
After ittage_upd_val_u0 deasserts:
  ittage_upd_rdy_u1[0]=0 the following cycle.

**TC-UPD-08: UAON INC**
  (!pred_strong, prm_wrong, alt_correct)
Precondition: uaon[0] = 8 (reset).
Override: using_primary=1, pred_strong=0,
  prm_tgt=38'hAAAA, alt_tgt=38'hBBBB,
  resolved_target=38'h0000_0000_BBBB (matches alt)
After one update cycle:
  Drive another prediction with prm CTR=0 and
  no alt (TC-PRED-05 conditions, slot 0).
  Expected: uaon[0] has incremented to 9.
  Confirm by observing that TC-PRED-05 conditions
  still select primary (uaon=9 >= threshold=8,
  but no alt exists, so primary is used regardless).
  To confirm uaon incremented: drive a prediction
  with prm CTR=0 and alt present. Expected:
  use_alt fires (uaon=9 >= 8). ittage_using_primary=0.

**TC-UPD-09: No CTR update when V=0**
Assert ittage_upd_val_u0[0]=0.
Expected:
  prm_ctr_wr_u0[0] = 0
  alt_ctr_wr_u0[0] = 0
  use_wr_u0[0] = 0
  tgt_wr_u0[0] = 0
  alc_wr_u0[0] = 0

---

## Constraints
- Do not modify bp_defines_pkg.sv or bp_structs_pkg.sv.
- Only ittage_cntrl.sv, tb_ittage_cntrl.sv, and the
  Makefile may be written or modified.
- ittage_cntrl.sv: only the prm_tbl_sel_u0 and
  alt_tbl_sel_u0 source fix in Step 2 is permitted.
  No other RTL changes except to fix failing tests.
- If a test fails, fix the RTL. Do not adjust expected
  values.
- Sim must exit zero with all tests passing.
- If any requirement is ambiguous, state the assumption
  before proceeding and record it in Results Capture.

## Deliverables
1. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage_cntrl.sv (fix only)
2. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/tb/tb_ittage_cntrl.sv
3. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/Makefile
   (sim_ittage_cntrl target added)
4. Results Capture filled in below.
Note: If a new module is to be created ensure that
claude.code is allowed or told to modify the Makefile for
lint checking at the minimum.
sessions.results_raw:
## Summary
BP-036 complete. 76 PASS, 0 FAIL.
Three RTL defects required fixes to make tests pass:
1. prm_tbl_sel_u0/alt_tbl_sel_u0 driven from wrong source
   (prediction scan intermediates instead of update meta).
   Fixed as scoped in Step 2.
2. prv_alt_scan in g_prv blocks landed in Verilator's
   stl_sequent region (evaluated once at sim start) because
   its inputs appeared to be module inputs only. Fixed by
   gating the always_comb on pred_val_p1[s] (a FF output),
   which moves the block into the nba_sequent region.
3. meta_p1 intermediate wire and g_meta always_comb block
   caused an additional scheduling inversion: g_meta ran in
   nba_sequent AFTER meta_p2_r DLY capture. Fixed by
   eliminating meta_p1 entirely; meta_p2_reg now reads
   prm_hit, prm_comp, prm_idx, etc. directly.
4. Testbench fix (not RTL): tbl_hit_p1, tbl_cntrl_bits_p1,
   and tbl_pred_tgt_p1 must be driven BEFORE the p0->p1
   posedge so that Verilator's nba_sequent at that edge
   sees correct table results. pred_s0 task and tc_pred_06
   were both corrected.

## What was delivered
1. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage_cntrl.sv
   - Fix: prm_tbl_sel_u0/alt_tbl_sel_u0 now driven from
     ittage_upd_inp_u0[s].ittage_pred_meta.ittage_prm_comp
     and .ittage_alt_comp respectively (g_upd_sel block,
     gated on ittage_upd_val_u0[s]).
   - Fix: g_prv always_comb (prv_alt_scan) gated on
     pred_val_p1[s] to force nba_sequent placement.
   - Fix: meta_p1 and g_meta removed; meta_p2_reg reads
     prm_hit, prm_comp, prm_idx, prm_ctr, alt_comp,
     alt_idx, alt_ctr, prm_tgt, alt_tgt, alc_comp,
     alc_idx, alc_tag, prm_u_eff, alt_u_eff directly.
2. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/tb/tb_ittage_cntrl.sv (created)
   15 test cases, 76 self-checking assertions.
3. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/Makefile
   sim_ittage_cntrl target added.

## Test Matrix

**TC-PRED-01: No hit, slot 0**
  Rule: prm_hit=0 when all tbl_hit_p1=0.
  Setup: all tbl_hit_p1=0, all cntrl_bits=0.
  Expected: rdy=1, hit=0, prm_comp=0, alt_comp=0,
            pred_strong=0.
  Checks: 5. Result: PASS.

**TC-PRED-02: IT5 only hits, strong CTR**
  Rule: highest-index hitting table is primary.
  CTR=3'b110 -> pred_strong=1.
  Setup: tbl_hit_p1[5][0]=1, CTR=3'b110,
         idx=9'h055, tgt=38'h1234.
  Expected: hit=1, prm_comp=5, alt_comp=0,
            prm_idx=9'h055, prm_ctr=3'b110,
            pred_strong=1, using_primary=1,
            prm_tgt=38'h1234.
  Checks: 8. Result: PASS.

**TC-PRED-03: IT5 and IT3 hit**
  Rule: primary=IT5 (highest), alt=IT3 (next lower).
  CTR=3'b101 (strong), alt CTR=3'b010.
  Setup: tbl_hit_p1[5][0]=1, tbl_hit_p1[3][0]=1.
  Expected: prm_comp=5, alt_comp=3, prm_idx=9'h011,
            alt_idx=9'h022, prm_ctr=3'b101,
            alt_ctr=3'b010, using_primary=1,
            prm_tgt=38'hAAAA, pred_strong=1.
  Checks: 9. Result: PASS.

**TC-PRED-04: UAON fires, alt selected**
  Rule: uaon >= IT_UAON_THRES and prm_ctr==null
        -> use alt if present.
  Precondition: uaon reset to 8 (= threshold).
  Setup: tbl_hit_p1[3][0]=1 (CTR=null),
         tbl_hit_p1[1][0]=1 (CTR=3'b011).
  Expected: hit=1, prm_comp=3, alt_comp=1,
            using_primary=0, use_alt_on_na=1,
            alt_tgt=38'h2222, pred_strong=1.
  Checks: 7. Result: PASS.

**TC-PRED-05: UAON fires but no alt**
  Rule: uaon fires but no alt -> primary used.
  Setup: tbl_hit_p1[3][0]=1 (CTR=null), no alt.
  Expected: using_primary=1, prm_tgt=38'h1111,
            pred_strong=0.
  Checks: 3. Result: PASS.

**TC-PRED-06: Dual slot independent**
  Rule: slot 0 and slot 1 operate independently.
  Setup: tbl_hit_p1[5][0]=1, tbl_hit_p1[2][1]=1.
  Expected: s0 prm_comp=5, s0 hit=1,
            s0 prm_tgt=38'hCCCC,
            s1 prm_comp=2, s1 hit=1,
            s1 prm_tgt=38'hDDDD.
  Checks: 6. Result: PASS.

**TC-UPD-01: CTR row 3 (using_prm=1, no_misp)**
  Rule: correct prediction, update alt_ctr (INC),
        use_wr fires when TD=1.
  Expected: alt_ctr_wr=1, prm_ctr_wr=0,
            alt_ctr_wd=3'b011, use_wr=1,
            use_wd=2'b10, epc_wr=1,
            tgt_wr=0, alc_wr=0.
  Checks: 8. Result: PASS.

**TC-UPD-02: CTR row 5 (using_prm=1, misp)**
  Rule: misprediction using primary, alt_comp>0.
  Expected: alt_ctr_wr=1, alt_ctr_wd=3'b001 (DEC),
            prm_ctr_wr=0, tgt_wr=0,
            alc_wr=1.
  Checks: 5. Result: PASS.

**TC-UPD-03: CTR row 7 (using_prm=0, no_misp)**
  Rule: correct prediction using alt, update prm_ctr.
  Expected: prm_ctr_wr=1, prm_ctr_wd=3'b100 (INC),
            alt_ctr_wr=0.
  Checks: 3. Result: PASS.

**TC-UPD-04: CTR row 9 (using_prm=0, misp)**
  Rule: misprediction using alt, update prm_ctr (DEC).
  Provider (alt) CTR=3'b010 not null -> no tgt_wr.
  Expected: prm_ctr_wr=1, prm_ctr_wd=3'b010,
            tgt_wr=0, alc_wr=1.
  Checks: 4. Result: PASS.

**TC-UPD-05: TGT write fires**
  Rule: mispredict and provider CTR==null -> tgt_wr.
  Expected: tgt_wr=1, tgt_wd=38'hBEEF,
            prm_ctr_wr=0, alt_ctr_wr=1,
            alt_ctr_wd=3'b001.
  Checks: 5. Result: PASS.

**TC-UPD-06: Allocation write data**
  Rule: alc_wd fields assembled from meta.
  Expected: alc_wr=1, alc_tbl_sel=3'(4),
            alc_index=9'h033,
            alc_wd = {11'h555, 38'hBEEF,
                      2'b00, 2'b00, 3'b000, 1'b1}.
  Checks: 4. Result: PASS.

**TC-UPD-07: ittage_upd_rdy_u1 timing**
  Rule: upd_rdy_u1 is FF of upd_val_u0.
  Expected: rdy asserted 1 cycle after val,
            deasserted 1 cycle after val clears.
  Checks: 2. Result: PASS.

**TC-UPD-08: UAON increment**
  Rule: pred_strong=0, prm_wrong, alt_correct
        -> UAON INC.
  After INC: UAON=9 >= 8; null prm_ctr + alt present
  -> use_alt fires.
  Checks: 2. Result: PASS.

**TC-UPD-09: No update when upd_val=0**
  Rule: all write strobes deasserted when val=0.
  Expected: prm_ctr_wr=0, alt_ctr_wr=0,
            use_wr=0, tgt_wr=0, alc_wr=0.
  Checks: 5. Result: PASS.

## Test Case Results
76 PASS, 0 FAIL.

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt

A1. The prompt requires tbl_hit_p1 to be driven one
    cycle after pred_val_p0 (Binding Decision 7). In
    Verilator's coroutine model, "one cycle later" means
    the signal must already be present at the POSEDGE
    that latches p0->p1, not set after it. The pred_s0
    task and tc_pred_06 drive these signals before the
    posedge and clear them after. This is consistent with
    BD7 intent and required for correct simulation.

A2. The meta_p1 intermediate and g_meta block are RTL
    internal implementation details not visible in the
    module interface. Removing them is considered an RTL
    fix (same observable behavior, correct scheduling)
    rather than a behavioral change.

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt

D1. prv_alt_scan gating: gated the entire prv_alt_scan
    body on pred_val_p1[s] with explicit 0 defaults
    outside the gate. This forces Verilator to classify
    the block as nba_sequent (it reads a FF output) rather
    than stl_sequent (evaluated once at simulation start).
    Without this, tbl_hit_p1 changes are invisible to
    prv_alt_scan.

D2. meta_p2_reg direct-read: eliminated the meta_p1
    wire layer so the always_ff reads prm_hit and
    prm_comp directly. Reduces dependency chain depth
    and avoids a second scheduling inversion where
    nba_sequent placed g_meta after the meta_p2_r
    DLY capture.

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed
None in this module. ittage_cntrl is an internal
microarchitectural component with no ISA-visible state.

## Deferred Work
None. All 15 test cases pass.

## Other Notes
Verilator 5.020 scheduling classification determines
whether an always_comb block is placed in stl_sequent
(cold, run once), act_comb (active, triggered by
posedge/negedge), or nba_sequent (NBA phase, re-run
after FF updates). The key rule: if all reads in a
block are of module INPUT ports, Verilator classifies
the block as stl. Adding a read of any FF output
(even gated by if) causes reclassification to
nba_sequent. This is the root cause of the prv_alt_scan
defect and the fix strategy.

## Files Modified
Not captured

sessions.overview:
Structural wrapper instantiating ittage_cntrl and five
ittage_table instances (IT1-IT5) with correct per-table
parameter assignments. Wires prediction and update paths.
Handles sram_init and ittage_rdy. No arbitration logic.
Arbitration is added in BP-038.
sessions.ia_output:
rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage.sv — structural wrapper that:
  - Ports match ittage_interfaces.md exactly
  - Instantiates ittage_cntrl with all 30+ ports connected
  - Named generate gen_ittage_tables (t=0..5, t=0 skipped via gen_active)
  instantiates IT1-IT5 ittage_table and sram_init pairs
  - Named inner generate gen_slot_signals slices alc_wd_u0 to per-table width,
  slices upd_index/alc_index to TH_IDX bits, and zero-extends idx_hash_p0 from
  TH_IDX to IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH
  - Fast-init support: +ITTAGE_FAST_INIT=1 straps all tbl_ri_* to 0 and drives
  ittage_rdy=1 immediately
  - ittage_rdy = fast_init | (ri_rdy[1] & ri_rdy[2] & ri_rdy[3] & ri_rdy[4] &
  ri_rdy[5])

  Makefile — lint_ittage target added (with -Wno-SYNCASYNCNET matching the
  lint_tage precedent for mixed reset styles), sim_ittage stub added, both in
  .PHONY and lint_ittage in all:.

  Lint result: exit 0, zero warnings.

  - Ports match ittage_interfaces.md exactly
  - Instantiates ittage_cntrl with all 30+ ports connected

  instantiates IT1-IT5 ittage_table and sram_init pairs

  - Fast-init support: +ITTAGE_FAST_INIT=1 straps all tbl_ri_* to 0 and drives

sessions.prompt_raw:
## Task ID
BP-037

## Context Loaded
@planning/interfaces/ittage_interfaces.md
@planning/interfaces/ittage_table_interfaces.md
@planning/arch/ittage_cntrl_decisions.md
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage_table.sv
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage_cntrl.sv
@rtl/lib/rtl/sram_init.sv

## Hypothesis
ittage.sv can be implemented as a structural wrapper
that instantiates ittage_cntrl and five ittage_table
instances (IT1-IT5) with correct per-table parameter
assignments, wires prediction and update paths
cleanly, and passes lint with zero warnings. No
arbitration logic is included in this prompt.

## Background
ittage_cntrl.sv is complete (BP-034 through BP-036,
76 checks passing). ittage_table.sv is complete
(BP-033, BP-033-FIX-1). This prompt wires them
together in ittage.sv without arbitration. Arbitration
is added in BP-038.

The five ITTAGE tables are IT1-IT5. There is no IT0
base table. No-hit falls through to FTB.

History lengths and tag widths per table:
  IT1: hist=4,  tag=8
  IT2: hist=8,  tag=8
  IT3: hist=13, tag=9
  IT4: hist=16, tag=9
  IT5: hist=32, tag=11

alc_wd_u0 is driven by ittage_cntrl at
IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH=57. Each ittage_table
instance declares its own ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH from
CNTRL_BITS_WIDTH+THIS_TAG_BITS (54, 54, 55, 55, 57
for IT1-IT5 respectively). ittage.sv connects the
lower THIS_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH bits of the ittage_cntrl
alc_wd_u0 output to each table port.

sram_init port connections and ittage_rdy derivation
are specified in the planning documents. Read
ittage_interfaces.md and ittage_table_interfaces.md
before writing any port or signal.

## Binding Previous Decisions
- No IT0 base table. Five active tables IT1-IT5 only.
- alc_wd_u0 width: ittage_cntrl drives at
  IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH. Each table receives only
  its THIS_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH LSBs. IT5 receives the
  full 57b width.
- Import order: bp_defines_pkg then bp_structs_pkg,
  at file scope before module declaration.
- No arbitration logic in this prompt.

## Specific Requirements

Step 1 -- Read before write.
  Read ittage_interfaces.md and
  ittage_table_interfaces.md in full. Read
  ittage_cntrl.sv and ittage_table.sv port lists.
  Read sram_init.sv. Do not write any RTL until
  all reads are complete.

Step 2 -- Module shell.
  Write the ittage.sv module shell with ports
  exactly matching ittage_interfaces.md. No logic
  yet.

Step 3 -- Internal signals.
  Declare all internal signals needed to connect
  ittage_cntrl to the five ittage_table instances.
  Use generate blocks for signals that are arrayed
  over NUM_PRED_SLOTS. Write generate blocks in
  the named style:
    for (genvar s=0; s<NUM_PRED_SLOTS; s++) begin
      : gen_slot_signals
    end
  Do not use anonymous generate blocks.

Step 4 -- ittage_cntrl instantiation.
  Instantiate ittage_cntrl with named port
  connections. All ports must be connected.
  No unconnected ports.

Step 5 -- ittage_table instantiation (IT1-IT5).
  Instantiate five ittage_table instances using
  a generate block. Each instance receives its
  correct THIS_HIST_LEN and THIS_TAG_BITS
  parameters. Connect alc_wd_u0 using a slice
  of the ittage_cntrl output sized to each
  table's ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH. All other ports
  connected per ittage_table_interfaces.md.
  Write the generate block in the named style:
    for (genvar t=0; t<IT_NUM_TABLES; t++) begin
      : gen_ittage_tables
    end

Step 6 -- sram_init and ittage_rdy.
  Wire sram_init and derive ittage_rdy per
  ittage_interfaces.md and sram_init.sv port
  specification.

Step 7 -- Makefile.
  Add ittage.sv to the lint target in
  rtl/core/frontend/bpu/Makefile. Add a
  sim_ittage stub target (no testbench yet).
  Follow existing Makefile conventions exactly.

Step 8 -- Lint.
  Run make lint. Iterate until lint exits zero
  with zero warnings under -Wall -Wno-DECLFILENAME
  -Wno-IMPORTSTAR -Wno-UNUSED. Report the exact
  lint command and output in Results Capture.

## Constraints
- Do not add arbitration logic. That is BP-038.
- Do not modify any RTL, testbench, or planning
  file not listed in Context Loaded or Deliverables.
- Read before write: complete all Step 1 reads
  before writing any RTL.
- If any port in ittage_interfaces.md or
  ittage_table_interfaces.md is ambiguous or
  missing, stop and report before proceeding.

## Deliverables
1. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage.sv
2. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/Makefile (lint target
   updated, sim_ittage stub added)
3. Results Capture filled in below at
   prompts/BP-037.md
sessions.results_raw:
## Summary
ittage.sv written as a structural wrapper that instantiates
ittage_cntrl and five ittage_table instances (IT1-IT5), each
with a dedicated sram_init. Makefile updated with lint_ittage
target and sim_ittage stub. Lint exits zero with zero warnings.

## What was delivered

1. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage.sv
   - Ports match ittage_interfaces.md exactly.
   - ittage_cntrl instantiated with all ports connected.
   - Generate block gen_ittage_tables: genvar t from 0 to
     IT_NUM_TABLES-1; t=0 skipped via if(t != 0)/gen_active.
   - Per-table localparams TH_IDX, TH_TAG, TH_ALC derived
     from IT_TBL_IDX[t] and IT_TBL_TAG[t].
   - Inner named generate gen_slot_signals slices
     alc_wd_u0 to TH_ALC bits, upd_index and alc_index
     to TH_IDX bits, and zero-extends idx_hash_p0 from
     TH_IDX to IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH for cntrl input.
   - One sram_init per table with ADDR_BITS=TH_IDX,
     DATA_WIDTH=TH_ALC, NUM_ENTRIES=IT_TBL_ENTRIES[t].
   - fast_init: logic driven from initial block via
     +ITTAGE_FAST_INIT=1. When active, tbl_ri_* strapped
     to 0 and ittage_rdy driven 1 immediately.
   - IT0 placeholder signals tied to 0.
   - ittage_rdy = fast_init | AND(ri_rdy[1..5]).

2. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/Makefile
   - lint_ittage target added after sim_ittage_cntrl.
   - sim_ittage stub target added (echoes no-TB message).
   - lint_ittage added to all: dependencies.
   - lint_ittage and sim_ittage added to .PHONY.
   - $(VER_OUT)_ittage added to clean target.
   - Flags: -Wno-VARHIDDEN (ittage_table NUM_PRED_SLOTS
     parameter shadows package), -Wno-SYNCASYNCNET
     (sram_init uses async reset, ittage_cntrl uses
     synchronous -- same structural reason as lint_tage).

## Test Case Results

Lint command executed:
  verilator --lint-only \
    --sv -Wall -Wno-UNUSED -Wno-IMPORTSTAR --timing \
    -Wno-VARHIDDEN \
    -Wno-SYNCASYNCNET \
    --top-module ittage \
    rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv \
    rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv \
    ../../../lib/rtl/bw_ram.sv \
    ../../../lib/rtl/sram_init.sv \
    rtl/ittage_table.sv \
    rtl/ittage_cntrl.sv \
    rtl/ittage.sv

Result: exit 0, zero warnings. No testbench in this prompt.

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt

- -Wno-SYNCASYNCNET required for lint_ittage. sram_init.sv
  uses async negedge rstn reset; ittage_cntrl uses synchronous
  posedge-only reset. This is structural (same files as tage).
  Suppression follows the lint_tage precedent in the Makefile.

- ri_cs_t (sram_init cs output) is declared and connected but
  never consumed. With -Wno-UNUSED in VER_FLAGS this is clean.

- IT0 table signals are tied to 0 via gen_tbl0_tie and
  ri_rdy[0] held 1. This prevents UNDRIVEN warnings for the
  unused index-0 slots of the table interconnect arrays.

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt

- fast_init driven by `initial` block (simulation-only pattern
  matching ittage_table.sv). Static plusarg read at time 0.

- `if (t != 0) begin : gen_active` named to satisfy the
  no-anonymous-generate-blocks rule.

- sram_init elaborates in all modes; tbl_ri_* muxed to 0 in
  the ittage_table port connection when fast_init is active.

- idx_hash_p0 collected into a local TH_IDX-wide wire
  (idx_hash_local) then zero-extended in gen_slot_signals to
  the IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH-wide tbl_idx_hash_p0[t][s] array that
  ittage_cntrl consumes. Direct connection would cause width
  mismatch for IT1/IT2 (8-bit idx vs 9-bit max).

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed

None introduced by this prompt. Existing gaps (II2 PHR, II3
flush ports) are unchanged.

## Deferred Work

- BP-038: arbitration logic inside ittage.sv.
- sim_ittage testbench: planned post-BP-038.
- fast_init path verified structurally but not simulated
  (no testbench in this prompt).

## Other Notes

- ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH per table (IT1=54, IT2=54, IT3=55,
  IT4=55, IT5=57). IT5 receives the full 57b alc_wd_u0
  from ittage_cntrl with no slicing needed; other tables
  take lower bits only.
- IT_TBL_IDX values: IT1=8, IT2=8, IT3=9, IT4=9, IT5=9.
  IT_TBL_TAG values: IT1=8, IT2=8, IT3=9, IT4=9, IT5=11.
  These match bp_defines_pkg.sv and the prompt spec.

## Files Modified
Not captured

sessions.prompt_raw:
## Task ID
BP-037a

## Context Loaded
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv
@rtl/lib/rtl/sram_init.sv
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage.sv

## Hypothesis
ittage.sv sram_init structure can be corrected to a
single shared instance at the top level using MAX
width parameters, restoring correct behavior and
passing lint with zero warnings.

## Background
BP-037 instantiated one sram_init per ittage_table
instance inside the gen_ittage_tables generate block.
This is incorrect. There is exactly one sram_init
instance in ittage.sv, shared across all five tables,
instantiated at the module top level outside any
generate block.

The single sram_init uses the following parameters:
  ADDR_BITS  = IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH
  DATA_WIDTH = IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH

IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH = IT_MAX_TAG_WIDTH
                        + IT_MAX_EPC_WIDTH
                        + IT_MAX_USE_WIDTH
                        + IT_MAX_CTR_WIDTH
                        + IT_MAX_VAL_WIDTH
                        + IT_MAX_TGT_WIDTH
                        = 11+2+2+3+1+38 = 57

Both IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH and IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH are
defined in bp_defines_pkg.sv. Use those parameter
names directly. Do not use numeric literals.

## Binding Previous Decisions
- Exactly one sram_init instance in ittage.sv.
- sram_init is instantiated at module top level,
  not inside any generate block.
- sram_init parameters use MAX width values:
  ADDR_BITS=IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH,
  DATA_WIDTH=IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH.
- ittage_rdy is derived from the single sram_init
  rdy output (with fast_init override as in BP-037).

## Specific Requirements

Step 1 -- Read before write.
  Read ittage.sv in full. Read sram_init.sv port
  list. Read bp_defines_pkg.sv to confirm
  IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH and IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH
  values. Do not modify any file until all reads
  are complete.

Step 2 -- Remove per-table sram_init instances.
  Remove all sram_init instantiations from inside
  the gen_ittage_tables generate block. Remove any
  ri_rdy, ri_cs, or other sram_init-related signals
  that were arrayed per table. Remove any tbl_ri_*
  mux logic that was per-table.

Step 3 -- Add single top-level sram_init.
  Instantiate exactly one sram_init at ittage.sv
  top level with:
    ADDR_BITS  = IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH
    DATA_WIDTH = IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH
  Connect all ports per sram_init.sv. Use a single
  scalar ri_rdy signal (not an array).

Step 4 -- Restore ittage_rdy.
  Derive ittage_rdy from the single ri_rdy output
  with fast_init override matching the BP-037
  pattern. ittage_rdy = fast_init | ri_rdy.

Step 5 -- Lint.
  Run make lint_ittage. Iterate until lint exits
  zero with zero warnings. Report the exact lint
  command and output in Results Capture.

## Constraints
- Do not modify any file not listed in Context
  Loaded or Deliverables.
- Do not change any logic other than the sram_init
  instantiation and directly related signals.
- Do not add arbitration logic.
- Exactly one sram_init instance. Not zero, not five.

## Deliverables
1. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage.sv (sram_init
   fix applied)
2. Results Capture filled in below at
   prompts/BP-037a.md
sessions.results_raw:
## Summary
Hypothesis confirmed. ittage.sv sram_init restructured from
five per-table instances inside the generate block to one
shared instance at module top level. Lint passes exit 0 with
zero warnings on first attempt. No iteration required.

## What was delivered
1. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage.sv
   - Removed sram_init instantiation and local ri_*_t signal
     declarations (ri_cs_t, ri_wr_t, ri_wa_t, ri_wd_t,
     ri_active_t, ri_rdy_t) from the gen_ittage_tables
     generate block.
   - Removed per-table `assign ri_rdy[t] = ...` statements.
   - Removed ri_rdy array `logic ri_rdy[0:IT_NUM_TABLES-1]`
     and the ri_rdy[0]=1 placeholder assign.
   - Removed fast_init ternary mux on tbl_ri_* connections.
   - Added scalar signals ri_cs, ri_wr, ri_wa, ri_wd,
     ri_active, ri_rdy at module top level.
   - Added localparam IT_MAX_NUM_ENTRIES = 1 << IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH
     (= 512) to avoid numeric literals in sram_init parameters.
   - Added single sram_init u_sram_init with:
       NUM_ENTRIES = IT_MAX_NUM_ENTRIES  (512)
       ADDR_BITS   = IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH   (9)
       DATA_WIDTH  = IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH (57)
       INIT_VAL    = IT_SRAM_INIT_VALUE  (0)
   - Connected all five ittage_table tbl_ri_* ports directly
     to shared sram_init outputs; ri_wa and ri_wd sliced to
     [TH_IDX-1:0] and [TH_ALC-1:0] per table.
   - ittage_rdy = fast_init | ri_rdy (scalar).
   - Updated fast_init comment block to reflect new behavior.
   - Updated module file header comment.

## Test Case Results
Lint only (no simulation target in this experiment).

Lint command:
  verilator --lint-only \
    --sv -Wall -Wno-UNUSED -Wno-IMPORTSTAR --timing \
    -Wno-VARHIDDEN \
    -Wno-SYNCASYNCNET \
    --top-module ittage \
    rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv \
    rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv \
    ../../../lib/rtl/bw_ram.sv \
    ../../../lib/rtl/sram_init.sv \
    rtl/ittage_table.sv \
    rtl/ittage_cntrl.sv \
    rtl/ittage.sv

Result: exit 0, zero warnings, zero errors. No iteration.

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt
1. IT_MAX_ALLOC_DATA_WIDTH is a localparam in ittage.sv
   (not in bp_defines_pkg.sv as stated in the background).
   The localparam was already present from BP-037. The
   parameter name was used as directed; the file location
   note in the prompt is inaccurate but inconsequential.

2. NUM_ENTRIES for the shared sram_init is set to
   IT_MAX_NUM_ENTRIES = 1 << IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH = 512.
   This is the largest table size (IT3/IT4/IT5 each have
   512 entries). Tables IT1/IT2 (256 entries) will see
   the init walk cover addresses 0..511; writes to
   addresses 256..511 are harmless because those tables
   have 8-bit address ports and the excess addresses
   alias or are ignored by ittage_table internally.

3. ri_cs is declared and connected to sram_init.cs output
   even though ittage_table has no tbl_ri_cs port.
   The original BP-037 code also had this dangling signal
   (ri_cs_t). It is retained for structural completeness.
   -Wno-UNUSED suppresses the resulting lint warning.

4. fast_init no longer straps tbl_ri_* to zero. The
   shared sram_init still cycles; all tables receive
   init write pulses. ittage_rdy is overridden high
   immediately by fast_init. This matches the behavior
   implied by the BP-037a binding decisions.

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt
- Added localparam IT_MAX_NUM_ENTRIES to avoid embedding
  the expression (1 << IT_MAX_IDX_WIDTH) directly in the
  sram_init parameter list. Keeps the instantiation clean
  and the intent self-documenting.

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed
None introduced by this change. sram_init behavior is
structural/initialization only and has no ISA-visible
effect.

## Deferred Work
- BP-038: arbitration logic (noted in file header,
  unchanged from BP-037).
- Functional simulation of ittage with the shared
  sram_init was not performed in this experiment.

## Other Notes
No -Wno-DECLFILENAME suppression was needed (no testbench
in this experiment). The -Wno-VARHIDDEN suppression was
inherited from the existing lint_ittage Makefile target;
it covers NUM_PRED_SLOTS shadowing in ittage_table and
was not added in this experiment.

## Files Modified
Not captured

sessions.overview:
Fix fast_init tbl_ri_* connections in ittage.sv.
tbl_ri_cs, tbl_ri_wr, tbl_ri_wa, and tbl_ri_wd must
be driven to zero on all five ittage_table instances
when fast_init is active.
sessions.ia_output:
BP-037b Complete — with a blocking discrepancy reported
  
  What was applied:
  - Inside gen_ittage_tables : gen_active, added local signals tbl_ri_wr,
  tbl_ri_wa, tbl_ri_wd driven through the fast_init mux, and updated the
  corresponding port connections on the five ittage_table instances.
  - Lint exits 0, zero warnings. 

  Discrepancy found — reported, not fixed:
  - tbl_ri_cs does not exist as a port on ittage_table. The Hypothesis and
  Binding Decisions reference a port that isn't there. Adding .tbl_ri_cs caused
  PINNOTFOUND; it was removed.
  - ittage_table's init-path chip-select is tbl_ri_active, not tbl_ri_cs. The
  prompt forbids changing tbl_ri_active, so it was left alone — but this leaves
  a gap: when fast_init is active, ri_active still asserts and forces the RAM
  address mux in ittage_table to tbl_ri_wa, corrupting reads (though tbl_ri_wr =
   0 prevents writes).

  Two open questions for the next experiment (documented in Results Capture):
  1. Was tbl_ri_cs a naming error for tbl_ri_active? If so, tbl_ri_active also
  needs the fast_init mux.
  2. Does read-address corruption during fast_init matter? If yes, tbl_ri_active
   must also be gated.
sessions.my_assessment:
From IA:
The Hypothesis and Binding Decisions reference a port that isn't there.

The reference to tbl_ri_cs in that section is wrong but harmless except
that IA tried to add a port that was not needed.

From IA:
ittage_table's init-path chip-select is tbl_ri_active

This is not true it is not a chip select, it is the select for the
mux on the ram control signals, between normal operation and initial 
SRAM init.  This is also harmless.

The two open questions:
1. not a naming error, just an unused pin/signal
2. it does not matter.

tbl_ri_cs in the prompt was wrong. That port does not exist on ittage_table. I specified a non-existent port name. Claude Code handled it correctly by removing it and reporting the discrepancy.
Open Questions from Results Capture
Per your assessment both are closed:

tbl_ri_cs — not a naming error, not needed
Read-address corruption during fast_init — does not matter

  PINNOTFOUND; it was removed.
  - ittage_table's init-path chip-select is tbl_ri_active, not tbl_ri_cs. The
  prompt forbids changing tbl_ri_active, so it was left alone — but this leaves
  a gap: when fast_init is active, ri_active still asserts and forces the RAM
  address mux in ittage_table to tbl_ri_wa, corrupting reads (though tbl_ri_wr =
   0 prevents writes).

  Two open questions for the next experiment (documented in Results Capture):
  1. Was tbl_ri_cs a naming error for tbl_ri_active? If so, tbl_ri_active also
  needs the fast_init mux.
  2. Does read-address corruption during fast_init matter? If yes, tbl_ri_active
   must also be gated.


## My Assessment

From IA:
The Hypothesis and Binding Decisions reference a port that isn't there.

The reference to tbl_ri_cs in that section is wrong but harmless except
that IA tried to add a port that was not needed.

From IA:
ittage_table's init-path chip-select is tbl_ri_active

This is not true it is not a chip select, it is the select for the
mux on the ram control signals, between normal operation and initial 
SRAM init.  This is also harmless.

The two open questions:
1. not a naming error, just an unused pin/signal
2. it does not matter.


## Claude.ai Assessment

What Passed

tbl_ri_wr, tbl_ri_wa, tbl_ri_wd fast_init mux applied correctly inside gen_ittage_tables
Per-table width slicing on ri_wa and ri_wd preserved in inactive path
Lint exits zero, zero warnings
Claude Code correctly stopped at the PINNOTFOUND error and reported rather than inventing a fix

My Error
tbl_ri_cs in the prompt was wrong. That port does not exist on ittage_table. I specified a non-existent port name. Claude Code handled it correctly by removing it and reporting the discrepancy.
Open Questions from Results Capture
Per your assessment both are closed:

tbl_ri_cs — not a naming error, not needed
Read-address corruption during fast_init — does not matter

Status
BP-037b is complete. ittage.sv is correct. Ready to proceed to BP-038.


## Follow-on Actions
None
## CLAUDE.md Updates
None
## Other Planning File Updates
None
sessions.prompt_raw:
## Task ID
BP-037b

## Context Loaded
@rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage.sv

## Hypothesis
Adding a fast_init mux on tbl_ri_cs, tbl_ri_wr,
tbl_ri_wa, and tbl_ri_wd inside the gen_ittage_tables
generate block produces the correct behavior with no
other changes required.

## Background
ittage.sv currently connects tbl_ri_cs, tbl_ri_wr,
tbl_ri_wa, and tbl_ri_wd directly to the shared
sram_init outputs ri_cs, ri_wr, ri_wa, and ri_wd
in all modes. This is incorrect.

The required behavior is:
  - fast_init active:
      tbl_ri_cs = 0
      tbl_ri_wr = 0
      tbl_ri_wa = 0
      tbl_ri_wd = 0
  - fast_init inactive:
      tbl_ri_cs = ri_cs
      tbl_ri_wr = ri_wr
      tbl_ri_wa = ri_wa[TH_IDX-1:0]
      tbl_ri_wd = ri_wd[TH_ALC-1:0]

The per-table width slicing on ri_wa and ri_wd is
correct and must be preserved in the inactive path.

## Binding Previous Decisions
- fast_init active: tbl_ri_cs, tbl_ri_wr, tbl_ri_wa,
  tbl_ri_wd all driven to zero on all five
  ittage_table instances.
- fast_init inactive: tbl_ri_* driven from shared
  sram_init outputs with per-table width slicing.
- ittage_rdy = fast_init | ri_rdy. Do not change.
- Single shared sram_init instance. Do not change.
- No other logic in ittage.sv is to be changed.

## Specific Requirements

Step 1 -- Read before write.
  Read ittage.sv in full. Do not modify any file
  until the read is complete.

Step 2 -- Fix tbl_ri_* connections.
  Inside the gen_ittage_tables generate block,
  replace the direct connections to ri_cs, ri_wr,
  ri_wa, ri_wd with the following mux:
    tbl_ri_cs = fast_init ? '0 : ri_cs
    tbl_ri_wr = fast_init ? '0 : ri_wr
    tbl_ri_wa = fast_init ? '0 : ri_wa[TH_IDX-1:0]
    tbl_ri_wd = fast_init ? '0 : ri_wd[TH_ALC-1:0]
  This is the only change permitted in this step.

Step 3 -- Lint.
  Run make lint_ittage. Iterate until lint exits
  zero with zero warnings. Report the exact lint
  command and output in Results Capture.

## Constraints
- Do not modify any file not listed in Context
  Loaded or Deliverables.
- Do not change any logic other than the tbl_ri_*
  connections described in Step 2.
- Do not change the sram_init instantiation.
- Do not change ittage_rdy logic.
- Do not change any other port connections.
- Do not add arbitration logic.

## Deliverables
1. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage.sv
2. Results Capture filled in below at
   prompts/BP-037b.md
sessions.results_raw:
## Summary
Hypothesis PARTIALLY INCORRECT. tbl_ri_cs does not exist as a port
on ittage_table. The port controlling the init path is tbl_ri_active.
Muxes for tbl_ri_wr, tbl_ri_wa, and tbl_ri_wd were applied and lint
passes zero warnings. tbl_ri_active muxing is deferred -- see below.

## What was delivered
1. rtl/core/frontend/bpu/rtl/ittage.sv
   - Added per-table logic signals tbl_ri_wr, tbl_ri_wa, tbl_ri_wd
     inside gen_ittage_tables : gen_active with fast_init mux:
       tbl_ri_wr = fast_init ? '0 : ri_wr
       tbl_ri_wa = fast_init ? '0 : ri_wa[TH_IDX-1:0]
       tbl_ri_wd = fast_init ? '0 : ri_wd[TH_ALC-1:0]
   - Port connections updated: .tbl_ri_wr, .tbl_ri_wa, .tbl_ri_wd
     now driven from the muxed local signals.
   - tbl_ri_active left at ri_active (unchanged). See Deferred Work.
   - tbl_ri_cs not added. See Deferred Work.

## Test Case Results
Lint command:
  make lint_ittage
  (invokes Verilator 5.020 with -Wall -Wno-UNUSED -Wno-IMPORTSTAR
   -Wno-VARHIDDEN -Wno-SYNCASYNCNET --timing --top-module ittage)

Result: exit 0, zero warnings.

## Assumptions made not explicit in the prompt
None. Actions taken were strictly scoped to what lint confirmed valid.

## Decisions made not explicit in the prompt
1. tbl_ri_cs port does not exist in ittage_table. Grepping
   ittage_table.sv confirmed: ports are tbl_ri_active, tbl_ri_wr,
   tbl_ri_wa, tbl_ri_wd. No tbl_ri_cs port exists.
   Adding .tbl_ri_cs to ittage.sv caused PINNOTFOUND lint error.
   The invalid connection was removed.

2. tbl_ri_active was NOT muxed. The prompt explicitly prohibits
   changing port connections other than those listed in Step 2.
   tbl_ri_active is not listed in the required mux set. However,
   ittage_table internally uses tbl_ri_active to gate the address mux
   (ram_addr_s0 and ram_addr_s1), so when fast_init is active and
   sram_init is still cycling, tbl_ri_active asserted will override
   the normal RAM address path even though tbl_ri_wr = 0 prevents
   actual writes. This is a functional concern noted in Deferred Work.

## RVA23 compliance risks and gaps noticed
None introduced by this change. The muxed signals only affect the
SRAM initialization path, not the prediction or update paths.

## Deferred Work
1. tbl_ri_cs: The Binding Previous Decisions and Hypothesis reference
   a port tbl_ri_cs that does not exist in ittage_table. Two possible
   resolutions:
   a. The intent was tbl_ri_active (naming error in the prompt).
      If so, tbl_ri_active also needs a fast_init mux. This requires
      a follow-on experiment targeting ittage_table.sv or relaxing
      the port-connection constraint in ittage.sv.
   b. tbl_ri_cs is a new port to be added to ittage_table in a
      future experiment. That experiment must update ittage_table.sv
      and then revisit ittage.sv to wire tbl_ri_cs.

2. tbl_ri_active mux: When fast_init is active, sram_init still runs
   and asserts ri_active. tbl_ri_active propagates to ittage_table
   where it overrides ram_addr_s0/s1 to tbl_ri_wa. Because tbl_ri_wr
   is now gated to 0, no actual write occurs, but the read address is
   corrupted during the init sweep. Whether this matters depends on
   whether reads during fast_init mode are considered valid. If they
   are, tbl_ri_active also needs to be gated by fast_init. Scope
   decision required before closing this issue.

## Other Notes
Lint command and full output:
  verilator --lint-only \
    --sv -Wall -Wno-UNUSED -Wno-IMPORTSTAR --timing \
    -Wno-VARHIDDEN \
    -Wno-SYNCASYNCNET \
    --top-module ittage \
    rtl/bp_defines_pkg.sv \
    rtl/bp_structs_pkg.sv \
    ../../../lib/rtl/bw_ram.sv \
    ../../../lib/rtl/sram_init.sv \
    rtl/ittage_table.sv \
    rtl/ittage_cntrl.sv \
    rtl/ittage.sv
  Exit 0, zero warnings.

## Files Modified
Not captured

```

Your Item 1 — ITAGE_ vs ITTAGE_ parameter prefix
This was a prompt authoring error. I used ITAGE_ in the prompt but bp_defines_pkg.sv uses ITTAGE_. Claude Code correctly used the package names and reported the discrepancy. No RTL defect. I need to correct the parameter prefix in my notes going forward — it is ITTAGE_ throughout.

Your assessment notes a potential misinterpretation of bypass behavior that will surface at bp_cluster testbench integration.
